data.key_responsibilities:
The Ceremonies Production Integration role is a key position within the FIFA World Cup 26™ Events & Entertainment Team (EET), consolidating the responsibilities of Production Leaders, Production Managers, Operations Managers, Show Delivery Managers, Scenic/Systems Managers, Site & Load Out Managers, Dissolution Managers, PMC & Operations Managers, and Production Support roles across all ceremonies programs. These programs include Opening Ceremony (OC), the Ceremony of the Opening Country Match USA (OCM USA), the Ceremony of the Opening Country Match Canada (OCM CAN), the Closing Ceremony (CC), the Halftime Show at the Final (HT), the Trophy and Awards Ceremony (TRO) and the Bronze Final (3rd).

This role supports the full lifecycle of ceremonies and stadium entertainment – from concept development and technical/production planning, through rehearsals, live show delivery, site and back-of-house operations, to dissolution, load out, and legacy handover. It ensures that all ceremonies-related production, staging, scenic, systems, site operations, and logistics are delivered on time, safely, efficiently, and in alignment with FIFA’s creative vision, operational standards, and safety policies.

The position works in close collaboration with EET internal departments, Event Management Companies (EMCs), stadium management, Logistics, Venue Management, Broadcast, Marketing, IT, Security, Accreditation, Workforce & Volunteers, and other FIFA World Cup 26™ Functional Areas (FAs). The role also plays a central part in managing documentation, project plans, production schedules, call sheets, reports, creative assets, and operational data to support seamless ceremonies delivery across multiple venues and territories (Canada, USA, Mexico).

This is a high-intensity, live-event operations role requiring strong project management, show delivery, technical understanding, stakeholder management, and the ability to adapt quickly to changing circumstances in a stadium environment.

Project & Production Management
  • Support the development, management, and distribution of production, staging, scenic, systems, and ceremonies operational plans from concept to delivery.
  • Assist in the development of ceremonies deliverables, coordinating event details, internal alignment, and approval processes.
  • Work alongside Heads of Operations, Technical, and Resourcing to oversee technical requirements and integration of ceremonies with stadiums and show environments.
  • Develop and implement broadcast, staging, and scenic requirements and processes, in collaboration with EMCs and relevant Functional Areas.
  • Align ceremonies production, staging, and systems plans with venue operating requirements and cross-functional integration (e.g. Stadium Entertainment, INF, Logistics, Venue Operations).
  • Support the creation and maintenance of detailed production schedules and timelines for build, rehearsals, show delivery, dissolution, and load out.
Ceremonies Operations, Site & Show Delivery
  • Manage and/or support ceremonies operations at stadium level, working closely with Stadium Entertainment Producers and Technical/Operations Managers.
  • Set up and manage ceremonies and operational spaces, including field-of-play areas, backstage, compounds, green rooms, dressing rooms, storage, BOH areas, and operational work zones.
  • Assist the Show Operations – Stadium team and Show Delivery team in executing show operations, cast movements, and in-bowl activities according to approved movement plans.
  • Ensure FF&E (what goes where) plans for ceremonies venues are executed correctly and on time.
  • Oversee or support venue-level operations related to accreditation, catering, parking, sanitation, cleaning, barricades, site equipment, tenting, fueling, and site restoration.
  • Assist in organizing and coordinating rehearsals, load-in/load-out, and all logistics for ceremonies and stadium entertainment.
Dissolution, Load Out & Legacy
  • Oversee or support the dissolution process for all ceremonies assets, ensuring efficient and safe load out after events.
  • Ensure dissolution plans are updated, and all items are prepped, packaged, and logged for load out.
  • Liaise with Logistics FAs for truck movements, load out crew confirmation, equipment needs, and timing.
  • Coordinate with venue management, EMC, and FAs to ensure a smooth, compliant load out process.
  • Promote sustainable practices during dissolution and load out, including waste reduction and responsible materials handling.
  • Contribute to close-out, TOK, and legacy handover reporting and documentation.
PMC, Talent & Volunteer-Centered Operations
  • Serve as or support the liaison between FIFA Volunteers, Talent, and EET Operations, particularly for Pre-Match Ceremonies (PMC).
  • Support volunteer welfare and ensure a positive and safe environment during ceremonies activities.
  • Coordinate with Talent Managers on the integration of talent into ceremonies and PMC operations.
  • Gather and communicate updates to production schedules for PMC-related activities to EET leads and management.
Site Management & Back-of-House Operations
  • Assist with setup, operation, and breakdown of backstage areas including dressing rooms, green rooms, BOH corridors, compounds, and storage areas.
  • Maintain BOH schedules and monitor housekeeping and working standards.
  • Provide on-site support to cast, crew, and staff, addressing needs and concerns promptly.
  • Communicate stadium site operations plans, daily run sheets, schedules, and venue documentation to relevant ceremonies departments.
  • Address venue issues promptly and assist with implementation of contingency plans for stadium operations.
Stakeholder Liaison & Cross-Functional Integration
  • Act as a key liaison with venue operations staff, EET internal departments, EMCs, stadium management, and other FWC26 Functional Areas.
  • Facilitate cross-functional coordination within ceremonies sub-divisions, suppliers, and external stakeholders.
  • Support vendors and suppliers, ensuring clear instructions and alignment with production and operations plans.
  • Represent ceremonies operations/production at venue-level or project-level meetings as required.
Documentation, Reporting & Governance
  • Develop and maintain user-friendly filing and archiving systems for production, technical, scenic, systems, and operational documentation.
  • Prepare or support high-level reporting, such as WIP reports, Executive reports, project updates, dissolution plans, close-out and legacy handover documents.
  • Maintain detailed records of production, staging, scenic, creative, PMC, site, and operational activities.
  • Ensure the current version of scripts, show documents, creative content, and run sheets is accurate, logged, and distributed.
  • Work in full compliance with FWC26 Policies & Procedures and Safety & Security rules and requirements.
Risk Management, Contingency & Safety
  • Identify potential risks across production, operations, site, and show delivery, and contribute to mitigation strategies and contingency plans.
  • Ensure all activities are carried out in line with safety regulations, security protocols, and operational policies.
  • Support the development and implementation of contingency plans for operational or show-related issues and emergencies.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Live Entertainment Production, Theatre Production, Sports Event Management, Technical Production, or a related field preferred.
  • Equivalent professional experience in ceremonies, large-scale live events, or stadium show production will be considered in lieu of a degree.

Work Experience
  • Extensive experience working on large-scale live events, preferably in stadium environments (sporting events, ceremonies, concerts, or similar).
  • Demonstrated experience in event logistics and operations, working cross-functionally across large event delivery teams.
  • Experience with vendors and suppliers, including negotiation, coordination, and performance oversight.
  • Exposure to or experience with production elements such as lighting, sound, video, rigging, staging, scenic, and broadcast integration.
  • Proven ability to manage schedules, calls, meetings, production times, and operational timelines.
  • Experience handling production documentation (call sheets, production reports, permits, scripts, creative logs, etc.) accurately and efficiently.
  • Track record of identifying risks, developing mitigation strategies, and resolving complex operational or production challenges.
  • Experience leading or supporting teams in high-pressure, fast-paced event environments, including managing multiple projects simultaneously.
  • Excellent communication skills for interacting with producers, directors, talent, crew, partners, and stakeholders at all levels.
  • Strong relationship-building skills with internal teams, external partners, and venue stakeholders.
  • High attention to detail in managing complex projects and ensuring accuracy in deliverables.
  • Strong writing and editing skills for creative briefs, reports, presentations, and operational documentation.
  • Ability to prioritize effectively, manage competing deadlines, and remain calm under pressure.
  • Cultural awareness and respect for diverse audiences, ensuring inclusive and respectful content and operations.
  • A genuine enthusiasm for the entertainment industry and a commitment to creating high-quality, compelling ceremonies.
  • Ability to work independently, show initiative, and influence outcomes with minimal supervision.
  • Flexibility and willingness to work irregular hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, in line with event schedules.

Language
  • Fluent in English (required).
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a strong plus.
  • Additional languages are beneficial given the global nature of the tournament, teams, and ceremonies collaborators.

Technology
  • Familiarity with production and event management tools (e.g., show control systems, scheduling software, cueing tools, or project management platforms).
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams).
  • Ability to work with digital documentation, asset management systems, and reporting tools for production and operations.
  • Comfort using collaboration platforms and shared drives for filing, archiving, and distributing production and operational documentation.

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the FIFA Fan Operations Venue Lead, the Venue Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA26 Fan Operations team, and work in close collaboration with the FIFA Headquarters and other key individuals responsible for delivering a positive fan experience.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:  
  • Serve as a point of contact for the FIFA Fan Operations Venue Lead on all Fan Services Operations 
  • Manage the Gate Supervisors ensuring they are briefed, and have the operational equipment required 
  • Responsible for the deployment, briefing and overall management of the Fan Operations volunteers 
  • Ensure that critical event and venue information is available to fans and staff through multiple channels
  • Coordinate with Audio Descriptive Commentators
  • Oversight of the Restricted Item Storages located across the stadium
  • Work alongside the Accessibility team to ensure all mobility and accessible services are delivered to a high standard
  • Responsible for the maintenance and deployment of company assets and equipment
  • Collaborate with other departments to identify and solve any potential issues 
  • Other duties as assigned by Fan Operations Venue Lead

data.key_responsibilities:
The Stadium Government Lead will be a key member of the FIFA World Cup 26 Government Engagement team and will work in close collaboration with government entities and other key individuals responsible for delivering seamless government engagement and protocol operations for the FIFA World Cup 2026. This position requires passionate, skilled, and committed professionals to deliver extraordinary experiences.  

This role will be responsible for supporting the Director of Government Relations in the delivery of the overall project, including:  
  • Assist the Government Relations team in executing a comprehensive government relations game-day strategy for the tournament, ensuring close coordination with Host City Committees, elected officials, stakeholders, and internal departments. 
  • Serve as the primary point of contact for key high-ranking federal, state, and local dignitaries attending matches at the assigned stadium, ensuring smooth coordination and communication. 
  • Coordinate with the Guest Operations team to implement high-level protocol services for key governmental and diplomatic guests. 
  • Work closely with the Safety & Security team to facilitate government official arrivals, stadium access, and escorted movements, ensuring alignment with security protocols.
  • Oversee logistics and operational planning related to government relations projects at the assigned tournament venue.
  • Other duties as assigned.

data.key_responsibilities:
The Hospitality Management & Venue Operations evergreen role consolidates responsibilities across all hospitality operations roles—including operational coordinators, infrastructure coordinators, stakeholder services assistants, and stakeholder services venue managers—to form one comprehensive position responsible for delivering the full FIFA World Cup 2026™ Hospitality Programme inside stadium venues. The main responsibilities include:

Hospitality Venue Operations
  • Support and manage the delivery of the FIFA Hospitality Programme at the assigned stadium.
  • Ensure Hospitality areas (lounges, suites, VIK areas, Affiliate spaces, infrastructure zones) are fully ready and operational for matchday (MD).
  • Conduct pre-match checks for readiness, cleanliness, signage, catering logistics, and brand compliance.
  • Oversee matchday infrastructure, setup, teardown, and Supplier/On Location coordination.
  • Manage hospitality-related issues during MD operations and escalate as appropriate.

Hospitality Programme Coordination
  • Maintain operational alignment between FIFA, Host Cities, stadium operations, On Location, and all involved Functional Areas.
  • Support development and validation of hospitality operational plans and MD strategies to ensure high-quality guest experience.
  • Coordinate handover and hand-back of Hospitality facilities between the stadium and the Official Hospitality Provider.
  • Support preparation of operational documents, briefings, post-match reports, and end-of-tournament final reports.

Infrastructure Oversight & Technical Services
  • Coordinate with Functional Areas providing infrastructure support:
          -Cleaning & Waste
          -Security
          -Signage
          -Power & IT
          -Transport
          -TV/Broadcast
          -Catering Logistics
  • Validate and oversee on-site direction signage for Hospitality spaces.
  • Support mapping and managing logistics routes, especially for catering and sensitive movements.
  • Ensure all technical and infrastructure issues are addressed promptly to avoid operational disruption.

Stakeholder & Affiliate Hospitality Management
  • Support or lead the delivery of the FIFA Affiliates Hospitality Programme (Commercial Partners, Licensees, Media Partners, Host Cities).
  • Act as main or secondary point of contact for Affiliates within the venue on all Hospitality matters.
  • Conduct familiarization tours for Affiliates and verify operational readiness for their activities.
  • Ensure all Affiliate spaces are set up per guidelines and fully cleared of branding and equipment ahead of each match.
  • Guarantee consistent service quality across all matches for Affiliates Hospitality.

Accreditation, Access & Guest Movement
  • Ensure Affiliate teams, suppliers, and staff are appropriately accredited.
  • Oversee the distribution and management of upgrade passes for Affiliates.
  • Monitor VIP/VVIP movements for Affiliates and coordinate with Guest Operations on routing and access needs.

VIK Management & Brand Compliance
  • Conduct VIK (Value-in-Kind) checks and counts with On Location after each match.
  • Ensure brand exposure and Affiliate commercial rights are implemented according to guidelines and clean-site obligations.

Volunteer & Workforce Management
  • Manage Hospitality volunteers assigned to both general Hospitality and Affiliate operations.
  • Coordinate volunteer deployment, break schedules, readiness checks, and operational equipment distribution.
  • Support inclusion training and operational briefings to ensure service excellence and readiness.

Meetings, Reporting & Cross-Functional Communication
  • Attend Hospitality Team Daily Meetings and Affiliate-specific daily meetings with On Location.
  • Keep minutes, share key updates with the Hospitality HQ, and report operational issues promptly.
  • Support preparation of daily reports to Hospitality Central Team.
  • Prepare and support end-of-tournament reporting, including operational insights, VIK summaries, stakeholder feedback, and recommendations.

Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Hospitality Management, Event Management, Sports Management, Operations, Business Administration, or related field preferred.
  • Equivalent experience in premium hospitality, venue operations, or large-scale event delivery will be considered.

Work Experience
  • Experience in stadium hospitality, premium guest services, or luxury event operations.
  • Experience coordinating with suppliers, hospitality operators, or commercial partners.
  • Background managing volunteers and working in large-scale event settings.
  • Experience handling operational and infrastructure processes in fast-paced environments.

  • Ability to manage digital accreditation platforms, operational dashboards, reporting tools, and venue documentation.
  • Comfortable with communication tools, e-filing systems, and operational templates.

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Head of Event Production, the Stadium Entertainment Production and Content Management evergreen role consolidates responsibilities across all content, production, and systems-integration functions within the FIFA26 Events & Entertainment (EET) team.

These positions support the planning, creation, approval, delivery, and technical integration of stadium entertainment content across all 16 FIFA World Cup 26™ stadiums and the International Broadcast Center (IBC).

This evergreen role group collaborates closely with EET internal departments, HBS, production vendors, technical suppliers, stadium control room teams, and FIFA Functional Areas to ensure high-quality, timely, and accurate content delivery for stadium entertainment, pre-match ceremonies, awards presentations, in-venue show elements, and broadcast-integrated content.

The scope includes management of 45,000+ content files, stadium technical integrations, quality control, scheduling, match-day delivery, and real-time troubleshooting to guarantee a seamless fan and broadcast experience.

Content Coordination & File Management (Content Coordinator / Content Manager)

  • Assist or lead the coordination, tracking, and delivery of:
          -Stadium Entertainment, Pre-Match Ceremony, Trophy & Awards content (~30,000 files).
          -FIFA Functional Area (FA) content for all stadiums (~15,000 files).
  • Manage or maintain content tracking documents for all 16 stadiums.
  • Coordinate content updates, version control, and distribution schedules.
  • Liaise with stadium teams to confirm file reception and completion of match-by-match checklists.
  • Support quality control and approvals across all venue content.
IBC Production Workflow Management (Production & Content Coordinator / Content Producer)
  • Serve as final checkpoint at the IBC before content is dispatched to stadiums.
  • Conduct thorough quality checks, including:
          -Visual QC
          -Audio level corrections
          -Naming & metadata alignment with stadium naming convention
  • Manage approvals with the Content Manager or designated reviewer.
  • Coordinate IBC staffing schedules with HBS.
  • Oversee workflow and productivity of IBC edit suites, editors, associate producers, and graphics specialists.
  • Publish cue sheets and disseminate updates to broader Functional Areas.
  • Ensure all content delivered to stadiums aligns with deadlines and production schedules.

Creative Development & On-Site Production (Content Producer)
  • Produce, edit, and refine motion graphics and video content for stadium displays.
  • Manage creative direction from the Content Manager and lead day-to-day execution with editors.
  • Perform real-time adjustments for live environments and last-minute changes.
  • Guarantee quality control of graphics, animations, and video elements.
Stadium Entertainment Production Oversight (Production Manager)
  • Support stadium-by-stadium production management and planning.
  • Coordinate scheduling for stadium control room teams.
  • Manage Stadium Entertainment production overlays and staffing plans.
  • Develop and distribute Venue Production Briefs.
  • Coordinate production integration with EMCs (Event Management Companies) and Technical Suppliers.
  • Track stadium space allocations, FF&E requests, Talent-related production needs, and additional technical equipment installations (PMC & INF).
  • Support administration of all stadium production schedules and documentation.

Stadium Technical Systems Integration (Stadium Systems Integration Manager)
  • Create INF Stadium Technical Guides.
  • Manage stadium setup for:
          -AV/Staging vendor installation
          -Control room configuration
          -Technical overlays
          -In-stadium comms setup
          -Stage/performance space integration
          -Camera positions
          -JBT installations
          -Gap cabling requirements
  • Coordinate BIM360 updates and TEAP stadium technical requirements.
  • Ensure integration of technical systems between stadiums, IBC, staging suppliers, and performance spaces.
  • Lead planning for talent AV, backline riders, and staging requirements.

  • Bachelor’s degree in Media Production, Film, Communications, Live Event Production, Entertainment Technology, or a related field preferred.

Work Experience
  • Experience in stadium entertainment, large-scale live events, or broadcast production.

data.key_responsibilities:
The Ticketing Systems & Stadium Operations workforce is responsible for delivering all operational, technical, and customer-facing ticketing services across Host Cities and World Cup stadiums.

This posting covers the following baby roles under the Ticketing functional area:
  • Electronic Access Control Specialist
  • Ticketing Stadium Assistant Manager
These roles work in collaboration with Ticketing HQ, Stadium Management, Technology teams, Guest Services, Security (SEC), Venue Management, and other functional areas to ensure seamless delivery of ticketing operations, infrastructure, technology readiness, and premium customer experience for all FIFA World Cup 26™ spectators and stakeholders.

Stadium Ticketing Operations (Assistant Manager)
The Ticketing Stadium Operations workforce is responsible for delivering all operational, technical, and customer-facing ticketing services across Host Cities and World Cup stadiums. The main responsibilities include: 
  • Lead or support the full delivery of stadium ticketing operations at assigned FIFA World Cup 26™ competition venues.
  • Conduct all pre-tournament infrastructure, hardware, overlay, technology, and equipment setup and testing.
  • Validate stadium seating maps vs ticketing system (seat kills, obstructed views, flows, ADA plans, signage accuracy).
  • Execute matchday and non-matchday ticketing operations, including Ticket Offices, Ticket Resolution Points, Guest Service locations, and turnstiles.
  • Manage and deliver training for all ticketing staff, including volunteers, resolution assistants, turnstile operators, and dependent FAs (SEC, FAO, etc.).
  • Provide world-class customer service for all spectators, guests, affiliates, and partners.
  • Complete all pre-match and post-match operational reporting and documentation.
  • Serve as escalation point for matchday ticketing challenges, system issues, and customer matters.
  • Liaise with Ticketing HQ to ensure alignment on operations, reporting, and incident management.
  • Deputize for the Stadium Ticketing Manager (Assistant Manager role).
Electronic Access Control Operations (EAC Specialist)
The Electronic Access Control (EAC) workforce is responsible for ensuring the successful delivery, readiness, and live operation of the access control systems at the respective World Cup stadium. The team supports secure, efficient stadium entry by coordinating with providers, validating system performance, training operational staff, and monitoring live access control during Match Days. The main responsibilities include: 
  • Collaborate with and support the Electronic Access Control provider at the stadium.
  • Validate configuration and operational readiness of all EAC systems (turnstiles, handhelds, ADA units).
  • Conduct MD-1 and MD system testing and oversee troubleshooting of all equipment.
  • Deliver training to staff, operators, and dependent FAs on correct access control procedures.
  • Monitor and validate operational interfaces, data flows, event logs, and reporting outputs.
  • Follow up on test results, ensure outstanding items are resolved, and maintain readiness for matchday.
  • Support general stadium ticketing operations during both Match Days and non-Match Days.
  • Participate in live-operation monitoring during matches to ensure stable access control performance.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Business, IT, Event Management, Operations, Sports Management, or related field preferred.
  • Technical training or certification in access control, event technology, or customer operations is advantageous.
  • Equivalent experience in ticketing, stadium operations, or event delivery accepted.

Work Experience
  • Prior experience in ticketing operations, stadium operations, event logistics, or access control systems preferred.
  • Experience managing customer service operations, technology-enabled systems, or large-scale events.
  • Experience leading teams, volunteers, or operational workforces in high-pressure environments.
  • Experience working with large sporting events, ticketing centers, or high-volume customer operations is a strong plus.

Language
  • Fluent in English required
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
  • For any roles in Mexico, Spanish is required

Technology
  • Strong proficiency in MS Office, Microsoft Teams, and digital operational tools.
  • Familiarity with ticketing software platforms, customer service systems, and operational reporting tools.
  • Technical understanding of EAC systems (turnstiles, handheld scanners, RFID/NFC access systems).
  • Ability to interpret venue maps, seating systems, and digital ticketing workflows.
  • Comfort with troubleshooting hardware, software, connectivity, and system configuration.
data.title: Ticketing Stadium Operation and Ticketing Electronic Access Control – FIFA World Cup 26™ (All Host Cities)

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Sr. Manager of Venue Business Strategy, the Manager, Venue Revenue Management will be a key member of the FIFA26 Business Strategy team, and work in close collaboration with the Venue Business Strategy, Food and Beverage,  Parking and Finance teams and other key individuals responsible for delivering and managing ancillary revenue streams.  

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Manager, Venue Revenue Management for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Oversee Concessions Program relating to F&B financials
  • Establish walk thru schedules with Concessionaire to monitor any issues on match days.
  • Working with the Transport Team to manage parking revenue and on-site spectator parking operational plans.
  • Coordinate data integration with the FIFA26 Business Intelligence teams.
  • Identify operational issues onsite on match day which may affect revenue
  • Assist with financial settlements for both Concessionaire and Spectator Parking partner.
  • Assist FIFA26 Finance Venue Rep with preparation for settlements.
  • Prepare daily match day status reports for direct reports and the larger Venue Business Strategy team.
  • Effective communication with venue representatives and on-site FIFA26 representatives.
  • Assist FIFA26 Partner Rights Delivery as it relates to commitments to FIFA26 partners on food & beverage. 
Location
This role supports operations across multiple FIFA World Cup 26™ Host Cities. Not all roles will be required in every Host City.
Current Host Cities include, but are not limited to:

United States:
Atlanta, Georgia · Boston, Massachusetts · Dallas, Texas · Houston, Texas · Kansas City, Missouri · Philadelphia, Pennsylvania · San Francisco, California · Seattle, Washington

Mexico:
Guadalajara, Jalisco · Monterrey, Nuevo León

Canada:
Toronto, Ontario · Vancouver, British Columbia
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree preferred by not required. 

Work Experience
  • At least 8 years of leadership experience in a management role within a sports & entertainment venue, music festivals and/or Professional Sports team.
  • Experience in managing budgets and analyzing forecasts. 
Language
  • Fluent in English required
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Strong proficiency in Excel, financial tools, and data reporting platforms.
  • Familiarity with POS systems, F&B analytics platforms, and venue revenue dashboards.
  • Ability to work with Business Intelligence systems and integrate multiple data sets.
  • Strong documentation and reporting skills in Microsoft Teams, Word, and Monday.com.

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
The Host City Volunteer Operations role supports the full delivery of the FIFA World Cup 2026™ Volunteer Program across one of the 16 Host Cities. Volunteers serve as the “face” of the tournament, and this position ensures the program is implemented professionally, consistently, and in alignment with FIFA’s global standards. The main responsibilities include:

  • Support Host City-level planning and operations for the full volunteer journey, including recruitment, onboarding, training, scheduling, match-day support, and appreciation initiatives.
  • Assist with the operational readiness of the Host City Volunteer Center, ensuring all spaces, equipment, processes, and technology are functional, prepared, and aligned with FIFA standards.
  • Maintain a consistent presence at the Volunteer Center, managing welcome-desk operations, volunteer flow, wayfinding, candidate guidance, meeting pods, and check-in processes.
  • Provide strong customer service to volunteers, resolving inquiries, assisting with navigation, and ensuring a professional and friendly environment.
  • Collaborate closely with the Host City Volunteer Manager, Host City Manager, and centralized FIFA Volunteer Team to implement local and national Volunteer Program strategies.
  • Maintain the Volunteer Platform system, including availability, preference updates, assigned roles, communication, and scheduling.
  • Coordinate logistics, daily setup, break-area upkeep, refreshments, and overall facility tidiness to ensure a well-managed center environment.
  • Track, escalate, and resolve facility, process, equipment, and operational issues including IT, security, and building services.
  • Build relationships with local stakeholders, Host City leads, community partners, and FIFA functional areas to support recruitment, training, and tournament-time operations.
  • Support the delivery of training sessions, ensuring materials, rooms, schedules, and volunteer resources are properly organized.
  • Contribute to data collection, performance tracking, attendance monitoring, and analysis to identify trends and improve volunteer engagement and operational processes.
  • Assist with volunteer-experience initiatives, including appreciation events, recognition efforts, and communication campaigns.
  • Provide ongoing updates to the Volunteer Contact Centre by compiling and sharing all localized program information and FAQs required to support volunteers.
  • Support tournament-time volunteer management, including operational resourcing, daily staffing needs, welfare management, last-minute changes, and on-site troubleshooting.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Sports Management, Business Administration, Hospitality, Human Resources, Communications, Community Engagement, or a related field preferred. 
  • Equivalent experience supporting large-scale events, community programs, operations, or volunteer management will be considered. 
  • Strong interpersonal skills, organizational capability, and a service mindset are essential.

Work Experience
  • Experience working with volunteers, community programs, large-scale events, sports tournaments, nonprofit or civic engagement programs, customer service roles, or operational environments. 
  • Experience in recruitment, training coordination, event delivery, venue operations, or stakeholder support is highly valuable. 
  • Leadership or supervisory experience is preferred for manager-level responsibilities.

Language
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus

Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
Uniforms, Manager - Description
Reporting organisationally to the Uniforms Senior Manager, the Uniform Distribution Center Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Uniforms team, and work in close collaboration with the Uniforms Central Team, FIFA Teamwear, and other key individuals responsible for delivering FIFA World Cup 2026 uniform distribution.   

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Uniform Distribution Center Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • The UDC Manager oversees the entirety of operations at the Uniform Distribution Center and is responsible for ensuring every FWC26 workforce and volunteer member that attends, has a World Cup-worthy experience.
  • This role ensures all processes run smoothly, maintains quality standards, and leads the team in achieving operational excellence.
  • Drive and manage processes across all operational areas, including check-in, waiting areas, fitting rooms, uniform collection, and check-out stations. 
  • Oversee staff and volunteer coordination, scheduling and day-to-day engagement. 
  • Ensure efficiency, accuracy, and consistency in distribution operations between each guest group (workforce and volunteers).
  • Develop and implement best practices. 
  • Serve as primary point of accountability for UDC performance identifying any risks and issues and escalating as appropriate.
  • Supervise the UDC setup process, from product arrival to UDC opening and ultimate dismantling and dissolution. 
  • Identify and solve issues and risks related to UDC operations – collaborating with the wider Uniforms Team.
  • Train and motivate the UDC operations team on-the-ground and more broadly across the tournament where possible. 
  • Attend daily debriefings and complete daily reports, including the documentation of incidents to Central Team via We-Track and Microsoft Teams. 
Deputy Manager Uniforms - Description
Reporting organisationally to the Uniform Distribution Center Manager, the Deputy Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Uniforms team, and work in close collaboration with the Uniforms Central HQ Team, FIFA Teamwear, and other key individuals responsible for delivering FIFA World Cup 2026 uniform distribution.  

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Uniform Distribution Center Deputy Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • The UDC Deputy Manager supports the Manager in overseeing uniform distribution operations with a focus on product management, logistics, and back-of-house organization. 
  • Manage product flow, inventory, and replenishment. Coordinate logistics to support distribution efficiency. 
  • Oversee back-of-house operations and ensure readiness of supplies.
  • Support UDC Manager in executing operational strategies, training, and day-to-day UDC operations to deliver a positively memorable experience for FWC26 volunteer and workforce collecting their tournament time uniform. 
Uniforms, Coordinator - Description
Reporting organisationally to the Uniform Distribution Center Manager, the Uniform Distribution Center Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA26 Uniforms team, and work in close collaboration with the Uniforms Central HQ Team, FIFA Teamwear, and other key individuals responsible for delivering FIFA World Cup 2026 uniform distribution.   

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Uniform Distribution Center Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • The UDC Coordinator plays a central role in delivering the uniform operations through supporting volunteers, operating system and working collaboratively with the wider team to ensure a smooth experience for all FWC26 Workforce and Volunteers. 
  • For all roles, we seek talented, hardworking people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset: Provide direct support to Uniforms volunteers (UNF VOL). 
  • Assist clients and lead by example to provide a World Cup level of service to all. 
  • Troubleshoot system and process issues. Manage client flow, reassigning UNF VOL to address bottlenecks. 
  • Support check-in challenges and serve as help desk resource.
  • Train and lead volunteers to triage and resolve issues effectively.
  • Support the product re-stock strategy.  
  • Support UDC Manager and Deputy in executing operational strategies, training, and day-to-day UDC operations.

Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Operations, Supply Chain, Event Management, or a related field preferred.
  • Equivalent professional experience in logistics, distribution, event operations, inventory management, or customer service environments will also be considered.
Work Experience
  • Experience supporting or managing high-volume distribution centers, retail operations, logistics, or event operations.
  • Experience coordinating or supervising volunteers, staff, or customer-facing operations in fast-paced environments.
  • For manager-level role: experience leading large teams, managing operational programs, and driving process improvements.
Language
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus

data.key_responsibilities:
Airport Deputy Manager - Job Description
Reporting organisationally to the Airport manager, the Deputy Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Operational Services team and will work in close collaboration with the FIFA Transport team and other key individuals responsible for supporting the Airport Manager in overseeing all day-to-day airport operations, ensuring safety, efficiency, and compliance with aviation regulations and FIFA client service levels. This role acts as a second-in-command, providing leadership across multiple functional areas, including airside and landside operations, terminal management, customer service, and emergency response coordination.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Deputy Airport Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Support and deputize for the Arrivals & Departures Teams Manager, ensuring continuity of leadership and operational decision-making.
  • Lead, train, and motivate a diverse team of paid staff and volunteers to deliver exceptional airport services in line with FIFA World Cup 2026 standards.
  • Oversee daily and shift briefings to align team performance, ensure service consistency, and address operational challenges during extended hours.
  • Manage all operational aspects of arrivals and departures, ensuring alignment with governance structures and coordination with airport stakeholders.
  • Develop and maintain effective relationships with airlines, airport authorities, transport providers, and FIFA functional areas to facilitate seamless client movements.
  • Direct service planning and delivery for all FIFA constituent groups, ensuring compliance with defined service levels (DSLAs) and tournament objectives.
  • Coordinate with transport providers across multiple modes—including bus, fleet, and public transport—to ensure efficient passenger flow and timely connections.
  • Oversee the integration of arrivals and departures operations with other FWC2026 functional areas at venue and site level, including stadiums and non-competition locations.
  • Lead preparation and delivery of client-facing materials, including transport guides, operational updates, and presentations for key stakeholder meetings.
  • Ensure readiness and excellence in service delivery for all pre-tournament test events and operational simulations.
  • Foster a proactive, collaborative, and safety-focused team culture capable of performing under pressure and meeting tight deadlines.
Airport Supervisor - Job Description
Reporting organisationally to the Airport Deputy manager, the Airport Supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Operational Services team. It will work closely with the FIFA Transport team and other key individuals responsible for supporting the Airport Manager to ensure the service level for each client group.
The main responsibilities and oversights of the Airport Supervisor for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Will work closely with the Host City Airport Manager and Deputy Manager
  • Manage the contractor and volunteer team on site
  • Coordinate airport operations on airside and landside
  • Work collaboratively with the team and ensure adherence to the daily operational plan
  • Participate in daily meetings, giving ideas and feedback to improve the operation
  • Ensure that all airport safety and security regulations are followed.·      Ensure that the Site Operational Plan is followed for all operational duties and facilitations.
  • Provide directional guidance for FIFA clients, helping them to solve issues at passport control, customs, and biosecurity.
  • Assist with mishandled baggage cases, being the focal point between the client and the handling company/airline.
  • Oversee the contracted Meet and Greet suppliers' performance, ensuring agreed service levels are delivered.
  • Assist clients with any communication problems at passport control, customs, and biosecurity
  • Welcome the FIFA clients
  • Motivate the team, especially volunteers.
  • Maintain positive working ties with airport employees and government representatives
  • Responsible for the liaison of airside and landside operations
  • Maintain an effective working relationship with airport authorities.
  • Work collaboratively with all airport stakeholders.
  • Ensure all client transport is on-site, either in staging areas or at load zones, at the agreed times.
  • Liaise with contracted transport providers when required.
  • Responsible for the Welcome Desk operations and coordinating clients to the correct transport provided.
  • Work at airport vehicle staging areas and load zones.
  • Reporting to Head Arrivals and Departure Manager, Senior Arrivals and Departures Manager, Arrivals and Departures Manager, Arrivals and Departures Deputy Manager
Arrivals & Departures TROC Supervisor - Job Description
Reporting organisationally to the Head of Arrivals & Departures and the Arrivals & Departures Senior manager, the AND TROC (Transport Operations Center) Supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Operational Services team, and work in close collaboration with the FIFA Transport, and other key individuals responsible for delivering The Arrivals & Departures Transport Operations Center Supervisor oversees the real-time coordination, monitoring, and communication of all airport transport operations during the tournament. This role ensures accurate tracking of arrivals and departures, efficient dispatching of vehicles, meet-and-greet services, and seamless collaboration between airport teams, transport providers, and functional areas to deliver a safe, timely, and high-quality service for all FIFA client groups.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Arrivals & Departures Transport Operations Center Supervisor for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include: 
  • Oversee real-time coordination and monitoring of all airport transport operations to ensure on-time arrivals and departures for all FIFA client groups.
  • Supervise the dispatching and allocation of vehicles, ensuring efficient utilization of resources and adherence to service schedules.
  • Maintain constant communication with airport teams, drivers, and transport providers to manage passenger flow and respond promptly to operational updates or changes.
  • Coordinate meet-and-greet services to ensure a smooth, professional, and welcoming experience for all arriving and departing clients.
  • Track flight movements, service requests, and vehicle activity using transport management systems and monitoring tools.
  • Identify, escalate, and resolve operational issues or delays in collaboration with relevant stakeholders.
  • Support the implementation of FIFA service standards and DSLAs, ensuring all transport operations meet defined quality and timing expectations.
  • Liaise with airport authorities, security services, and functional area leads to maintain safety, compliance, and smooth coordination across operations.
  • Provide clear and timely updates to the Transport Operations Center Manager and airport management regarding ongoing activities, incidents, and performance metrics.
  • Prepare and submit daily operational reports and incident logs, ensuring data accuracy and accountability.
  • Assist in the training, briefing, and supervision of TOC staff and volunteers to ensure operational readiness and consistent service delivery.
  • Contribute to test events, rehearsals, and operational readiness exercises to validate systems, procedures, and communication channels ahead of the tournament.
Airport Locations: 
  • Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport — ATL
  • Boston Logan International Airport — BOS
  • Dallas/Fort Worth International Airport — DFW
  • Fort Lauderdale–Hollywood International Airport — FLL
  • Guadalajara International Airport — GDL
  • William P. Hobby Airport — HOU
  • George Bush Intercontinental Airport — IAH
  • Kansas City International Airport — MCI
  • Los Angeles International Airport — LAX
  • Mexico City International Airport — MEX
  • Monterrey International Airport — MTY
  • John F. Kennedy International Airport — JFK
  • LaGuardia Airport — LGA
  • Oakland International Airport — OAK
  • Philadelphia International Airport — PHL
  • San Francisco International Airport — SFO
  • Norman Y. Mineta San José International Airport — SJC
  • Seattle–Tacoma International Airport — SEA
  • Toluca International Airport — TLC
  • Billy Bishop Toronto City Airport — YTZ
  • Toronto Pearson International Airport — YYZ- FILED
  • Vancouver International Airport — YVR- FILLED
  • Newark Liberty International Airport — EWR
  • Miami International Airport — MIA
  • Felipe Ángeles International Airport — NLU

  • Advanced knowledge of transport operations, airport procedures, and client service protocols; certification in operations or project management is an asset.

data.key_responsibilities:
The Event Transport team plays a critical role in delivering all transportation services for FIFA World Cup 26™ client groups, including PMAs, Referees, Guests (VIP/VVIP/Legends), Workforce, Media, and other tournament stakeholders. This evergreen position supports the planning, coordination, and execution of all transport operations across Host Cities, stadiums, hotels, training sites, airports/FBOs, depots, and the International Broadcast Center. The main responsibilities include:

Client Group Coordination (PMA, Referees, Guests, Workforce, Legends, FT1) 
  • Coordinate all daily transport activities for assigned client groups. 
  • Manage airport arrivals and departures including tarmac operations, FBO procedures, convoy planning, and security alignment. 
  • Confirm all bookings in TMM/IFES and ensure accuracy of vehicle allocation, routes, call times, and schedule changes. 
  • Serve as the primary point of contact at hotels, training sites, stadiums, venue load zones, or HQ. 
  • Conduct driver briefings, vehicle readiness checks, and distribute movement updates. 
  • Ensure strict adherence to bubble-to-bubble operations where required. 
  • Maintain real-time communication with liaison officers (TLO, TSLO, RLO, RSLO), SLO, GOP, Team Services, and security. Monitor live vehicle movements and proactively identify delays or routing issues. 
  • Track VIP/VVIP/Legends/FT1 movements ensuring protocol, confidentiality, and service expectations. 
  • Oversee readiness of load zones, staging areas, signage, cones, and transport infrastructure. 
  • Support last-minute, emergency, or high-priority movement requests. Update internal teams on timing changes, risks, or operational challenges.

Workforce Transport Coordination 
  • Oversee daily workforce shuttle operations between hotels and tournament venues. 
  • Prepare and update workforce transport schedules, route maps, frequency charts, and DSLA documents. 
  • Provide professional guidance to workforce members regarding shuttle usage, pick-up points, and eligibility. 
  • Ensure readiness of hotel load zones including vehicle staging, signage, and staff deployment.

Transport Hotline & Help Desk 
  • Respond to all hotline calls regarding shuttles, routes, airport transfers, delays, Uber policies, and vehicle availability. 
  • Log all calls and actions into the system. 
  • Verify and update booking information in real time. 
  • Escalate complex or urgent issues to TROC Duty Managers or relevant workstreams (Fleet, Bus, Venue, Client Services). 
  • Maintain calm and clear communication during peak volumes or service disruptions.

Fleet & Vehicle Operations (Fleet Coordinator / FT1 / Long-Term Fleet) 
  • Inspect fleet vehicles and maintain documentation on inspections, assignments, and usage. 
  • Coordinate scheduled and unscheduled maintenance, repairs, and manufacturer service programs. 
  • Manage insurance claims and follow-up actions. 
  • Ensure long-term fleet vehicles meet technical, maintenance, and compliance standards. 
  • Update fleet management systems and track all vehicle activity. 
  • Oversee FT1 fleet including VVIP movements, real-time GPS tracking, confidentiality protocols, and post-tournament reporting.

Bus Operations & PMA/Referee Bus System 
  • Assist in readiness and planning of client group bus operations across USA, Canada, and Mexico. 
  • Support implementation of daily bus programs including schedule changes and demand-driven adjustments. 
  • Coordinate with Bus Managers/Supervisors on operations and incident response. 
  • Prepare operational activity reports and escalate deviations. 
  • Support execution of emergency, contingency, or alternative plans. 
  • Assist in preparing training materials for bus drivers and staff.

Venue Transport Operations (Stadiums, Training Sites, IBC) 
  • Work closely with stadium teams, Security, and Venue Transport to align operational plans. 
  • Manage transport information desks, VAPP boards, and venue load zones. 
  • Supervise volunteers and contractors. 
  • Support the setup, maintenance, and dismantling of venue logistics assets. 
  • Coordinate signage, parking areas, traffic flows, and access points. 
  • Oversee parking and shuttle operations at the IBC including 24-hour lot management.

Training Site Operations 
  • Support creation and execution of training site transport plans. 
  • Manage vehicle flows, load zones, and client group parking at training sites. 
  • Coordinate readiness with Security and operations teams. 
  • Support walkthroughs, readiness testing, and test events.

PMA Transport Coordination (Team Hotels, Training Sites, A&D) 
  • Maintain daily communication with Fleet, Bus, A&D, Venue Transport, Security, and Logistics. 
  • Hold daily meetings with TLO/TSLO. 
  • Inspect vehicles, conduct driver briefings, and validate training and route familiarity. 
  • Coordinate all TMM updates, last-minute changes, and driver management. 
  • Ensure availability and readiness of load zones at TBC and VSTH. 
  • Manage convoy planning with TSLO, ACS, FBO, and airport authorities. 
  • Support media access and badging procedures. 
  • Submit daily reports on incidents, risks, and performance.

Referee Transport Operations 
  • Lead or support operations at the Referee Base Camp Hotel and Training Site. 
  • Manage referee-dedicated vehicles, drivers, and schedules. 
  • Ensure vehicle readiness and compliance with referee transport standards. 
  • Support RLO/RSLO communication and daily requirements. 
  • Oversee training site transport infrastructure and client group parking. 
  • Provide daily operational reporting, issue escalation, and risk updates.

Guest, VIP/VVIP, and Legends Transport 
  • Act as main point of contact for hotel and stadium guest transport operations. 
  • Coordinate with GOP, SLO, Fleet, Logistics, and Client Services. 
  • Ensure cleanliness, readiness, fueling, and maintenance compliance of all guest vehicles. 
  • Manage hotel load zones for VVIP/VIP guests. 
  • Supervise transport crew and ensure high service standards. 
  • Maintain real-time visibility of guest movements.

Parking & Permitting (Spectator Parking, VAPP) 
  • Support development of spectator parking strategies and inventory analysis. 
  • Coordinate VAPP implementation including digital and printed permit processes. 
  • Manage database accuracy, PDA scanners, configuration, troubleshooting, and match-day distribution. 
  • Support on-site VAPP operations across stadium parking areas. 
  • Assist with nightly reconciliation and settlement procedures. 
  • Act as liaison between FIFA, JustPark, Stadium Teams, Venue Transport, and IT.

Depot Operations (Supervisors & Coordinators) 
  • Lead daily depot operations including dispatch, return processes, and safety procedures. 
  • Ensure vehicle readiness, fueling, cleaning, technology setup, and compliance. 
  • Supervise depot staff, dispatch assistants, and contractors. 
  • Conduct briefings, assign duties, and manage workforce performance. 
  • Maintain site safety, access control, and emergency procedures. 
  • Ensure infrastructure (signage, utilities, equipment) is operational and compliant. 
  • Support operational logs, KPI reports, handovers, and shift documentation.

Integration & Mobility (City Transport Operations) 
  • Monitor route performance, vehicle movements, and mobility conditions in real time. 
  • Coordinate with Host City authorities, public transport agencies, and Functional Areas. 
  • Escalate disruptions and communicate updates to Venue Transport, Bus, and Fleet teams. 
  • Support mobility operations for VIP and special events. 
  • Record key performance metrics and daily situation reports.

Reporting & Documentation 
  • Maintain accurate shift logs, incident reports, vehicle records, driver rosters, and operational summaries. 
  • Support creation of SOPs, training materials, and operational guides. 
  • Participate in daily briefings, debriefs, walkthroughs, and coordination meetings.

Education & Qualifications
  • Degree in Transportation, Logistics, Event Management, Operations, or related field preferred.
  • Equivalent work experience in large-scale events, transportation management, logistics, or mobility operations accepted.

Work Experience
  • Experience in transport, logistics, event operations, customer service, fleet/bus operations, venue operations, or emergency response coordination.
  • Experience supervising staff, drivers, volunteers, or contractors preferred for supervisor-level roles.
  • Experience working in fast-paced, multi-stakeholder or large event environments is an advantage.

Languages

Technology

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Tournament Headquarters and the Food and Beverage Managers, the F&B Coordinator will ensure the successful coordination of the F&B Concessions Program in the venue.

Main responsibilities of the F&B Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Ensure that services delivered are compliant with the F&B Concessions Operational Plan (number of staff, SKU allocation and sales prices per concession, activation hours, alcohol policy, SFE activations, etc.).
  • Ensure that the commercial and contractual rights of the Commercial Partners involved in the F&B Concessions Program are respected and implemented (brand exposure and product exclusivity, where applicable; use of dedicated equipment; respect of merchandising guidelines such as planograms, uniforms, menu boards, POS tactics, etc.).
  •  On Match Days: coordinate and report on operations and carry out regular quality controls (readiness by stadium gate opening, measure queuing times, monitor stock shortages, observe quality of customer service, etc.).
  •  Provide daily briefings to the local concessionaire and the Commercial Partner teams on site and ensure smooth communication between relevant stakeholders of the F&B Concessions Program.
  •  Liaise daily with the FIFA central team using the designated report template.
  •  Assist the FWC26 Commercial Rights Delivery Team in all matters related to FIFA Partnerships’ objectives and obligations.
  •  Assist the team with administrative responsibilities.
  •  Perform any other duties as required.

  •  Good planning and analytical skills.

  •  Strong commitment to quality, results, and accountability within a team.
  •  Resilience to stress and intensive work, persistence, and commitment to procedures.
  •  Good IT skills and experience using collaborative platforms.
  •  Great attention to detail and ability to produce work of a high standard.
  •  Open-mindedness, flexibility, and adaptability.
  •  Team player capable of working on a project involving many stakeholders.
  •  Result-oriented with an entrepreneurial mindset.
  • Experience in sports event management and, ideally, F&B / catering operations is a plus.
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the FIFA26 Director, Medical Administration, the Tournament Operations Center Medical Care Navigator will be a key member of the FIFA26 Medical Services team, and work in close collaboration with the  Tournament Medical Lead and other key individuals responsible for defining a clear process by which participating teams can access priority care of highest international standards and other key participant groups (VIP Guests, Workforce) can access non-emergency specialist care and primary/family care as defined in the FIFA Medical Handbook and defined in the operational plan.  

The main responsibilities of the Tournament Operations Center Medical Care Navigator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • The Tournament Operations Center Medical Care Navigator is responsible for the coordination of medical services to participating teams at the FIFA World Cup 26™ according to the FIFA Medical Handbook and the Concept of Operations Plans plan under supervision by the Director, Medical Administration, Senior Manager, Medical Services and FIFA Tournament Medical Lead and in close collaboration with the FIFA Venue Medical Officers US.
  • The Tournament Operations Center Medical Care Navigator coordinates the availability of medical staff, transports (ambulances, air rescue etc.), access to local providers (hospitals, emergency wards etc.).
  • Support of the FIFA Venue Medical Officers (VMOs) in all administrative and operational matters.
  • The Tournament Operations Center Medical Care Navigator collaborates closely with the Senior Medical Services Managers Canada, Mexico and USA. Tasks and Responsibilities:
Support of the standards described in the FIFA Medical Handbook as per the operational plan, with a focus on participating teams and referees:
  1. Supports the 24/7 medical coverage program for players and team officials at all venues (Team Base Camps; and Team Hotels) in collaboration with Host City Medical Leads
  2. Communicates & coordinates with designated hospitals and providers to support the FIFA VMOs, Tournament Medical Lead and Director Medical Administration General tasks and role functions
  • Capable of working a variety of shifts in the Tournament Operations Center that begin at 07:00 and conclude at 01:00
  • Represents first point of contact at the Medical Desk Tournament Operations Center
  • Anticipates and analyses problematic situations and occurrences and provide solutions to ensure optimal support to team doctors at all times
  • Cooperates with other stakeholders and functional areas within FIFA26 to meet the FIFA medical requirements
  • Builds trust relations with external key partners and stakeholders

data.key_responsibilities:
These positions are an essential part of the Licensing & Retail structure for the FIFA World Cup 26™, responsible for delivering a seamless, safe, enjoyable, and accessible experience across all official FIFA offsite retail operations & stadium retail operation. 
  • Offsite Retail Manager 
  • Retail Venue Manager 
The roles will be the key point of contact for all internal functions relating to licensing and the official retail operations including the Host City committees, Brand Protection and FIFA Commercial Affiliates (sponsors). 

Offsite Retail Manager Key Details 
The Offsite Retail Manager will be a key member of the team delivering the FIFA World Cup 26™ Retail project. In close collaboration with the central operations team based in the Headquarters for CAN,  MEX & US headquarters, the Offsite Retail Manager will be responsible for overseeing all official FIFA retail operations in the Host City apart from the stadium (this may include the official FIFA Fan Festival, airports, shopping malls and city landmarks). They will manage the day-to-day relationship with the appointed retail partners and will be integral in delivering operational retail excellence for the FIFA World Cup 26™. 
  • Day to day management of the appointed official FWC26 retail partner including the development and execution of key operational timelines and deliverables 
  • Ensure regular communication of FWC26 tournament information to all relevant internal and external Licensing and Retail stakeholders 
  • Attend daily Venue Meetings during Tournament 
  • Ensure all FIFA Stores are operating smoothly at optimum capacity with adequate staff numbers, product replenishment and POS system 
  • Provide any necessary status, sales reporting and analytics as and when required 

Retail Venue Manager Key Details 
The Retail Venue Manager will be a key member of the team delivering the FIFA World Cup 26™ Onsite Retail project. In close collaboration with the central operations team based in the Headquarters for  CAN, MEX & US headquarters, the Retail Venue Manager will be responsible for overseeing the Stadium Retail in the Host City operation. They will manage the day-to-day relationship with the appointed retail partner and will be integral in delivering operational retail excellence for the FIFA World Cup 26™.  
  • Day to day management of the appointed official FWC26 retail partner including the development and execution of key operational timelines and deliverables 
  • Ensure regular communication of FWC26 tournament information to all relevant internal and external Licensing and Retail stakeholders 
  • Attend daily Venue Meetings in stadium during Tournament 
  • Ensure FIFA Store match readiness at every Match Day for all nominated retail outlets at their venue 
  • Ensure all FIFA Stores are operating smoothly at optimum capacity with adequate staff numbers, product replenishment, POS system and management of queues 
  • Provide any necessary status, sales reporting and analytics as and when required  

Location
This role supports operations across multiple FIFA World Cup 26™ Host Cities. Not all roles will be required in every Host City.
Current Host Cities include, but are not limited to:

United States:
Atlanta, Georgia · Boston, Massachusetts · Dallas, Texas · Houston, Texas · Kansas City, Missouri · Los Angeles, California · Miami, Florida · Philadelphia, Pennsylvania · San Francisco, California · Seattle, Washington

Mexico:
Guadalajara, Jalisco · Mexico City · Monterrey, Nuevo León

Canada:
All positions have been filled.

data.description: At FIFA World Cup 2026, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest, most innovative, and inclusive sporting event of all time. The 2026 World Cup will mark the first time that the tournament will be hosted by three countries: the United States, Canada, and Mexico. This new format redefines excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement from fans and players. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
The Broadcast Operations Stadium Manager will be responsible for the planning, coordination, and execution of all broadcast operations and non-technical services at an assigned FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium. This position plays a key role in ensuring that all broadcast facilities, services, and activities are delivered to the highest international standards.

Key Responsibilities

Planning and Coordination
  • Oversee the planning and implementation of all stadium broadcast facilities and services in line with FIFA broadcast and technical requirements.
  • Collaborate closely with FIFA World Cup 2026 functional areas, stadium management, and the Host Broadcaster to align construction, logistics, services, and stadium operations with broadcast needs.
  • Act as a primary point of contact for the stadium broadcast team, facilitating smooth communication between all broadcast related stakeholders.
  • Maintain an overview of all Broadcast operations at the stadium
Operational Management
  • Monitor the progress of broadcast facility management implementation, proactively managing risks, issues and dependencies, and escalating urgent matters promptly.
  • Ensure full compliance and operational readiness of all broadcast infrastructure, including commentary positions, camera platforms, compound areas and connectivity.
  • Oversee seamless broadcast operations during the event period.
  • Co-ordinate the implementation of non-technical services for broadcast (e.g. Security, Cleaning & waste, etc.)
Leadership & Stakeholder Management
  • Support on-site broadcast teams, fostering collaboration and ensuring clear task allocation and accountability.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with FIFA departments, and local authorities to ensure effective collaboration and timely problem-solving.
  • Represent FIFA’s broadcast interests at venue-level coordination meetings and briefings.
Reporting and Evaluation
  • Prepare detailed progress reports and readiness updates throughout planning and delivery phases.
  • Daily reporting to broadcast HQ during tournament operation on all stadium broadcast operations status. 
  • Conduct post-event assessments, evaluating broadcast operations, identifying opportunities for successes and process improvement.
  • Ensure all broadcast operations uphold FIFA’s sustainability, safety and accessibility standards.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar level of higher education 
Work Experience
  • Minimum 6 years of experience in event management, preferably in a broadcast environment 
  • Strong interpersonal skills to effectively liaise with diverse stakeholders
  • Strong project management skills, with experience of managing complex projects across preparation, planning, and delivery
  • Experience in major sports events, FIFA World Cup or other FIFA events will be an asset
  • Proven track record of performing under pressure in a dynamic, multicultural and time-critical environment
  • Familiarity and working experience with the stadium and city assigned are a plus. 
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

We will only consider CVs submitted in English.
data.title: Broadcast Operations Venue Manager, FIFA World Cup 2026 - Monterrey
