data.benefits:
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.

Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
  • Health and fitness: Regular football sessions for all abilities, plus on-site gym.
  • Home office: Up to two days of home office per week if work permits and home office allowance for all.
  • Language courses: Offered to all staff to aid personal and professional growth.
  • Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
  • Pension: Flexible pension benefits with generous employer contributions.
  • Relocation assistance: A dedicated team will be on hand to support your relocation.
  • Sustainability: We support all employees using public transport.
  • Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.
  • Volunteering: Make an impact beyond your regular role and bond with colleagues by volunteering with one of our partners.
data.description:
As the organiser of some of the biggest and most iconic sport events in the world, we lead the way in the sport and event industry.

As an  AI & Innovative Product Manager, you will be responsible for establishing and enforcing governance frameworks that ensure secure, compliant, and standardized adoption of emerging technologies. This role will oversee lifecycle management, risk controls, and operational standards for AI models and agents, and blockchain platforms. You will enable innovation while safeguarding compliance, security, and transparency across all initiatives.

Join the team and support us on our mission.
data.key_responsibilities:
These are your key tasks:
  • Define and maintain governance policies, standards, and procedures for AI, and blockchain.
  • Accelerate AI-driven innovation by shaping a clear long-term vision, roadmap, and strategy for AI related products and emerging technologies in collaboration with executives, business stakeholders, and key partners.
  • Establish and maintain trusted relationships with business stakeholders, foster cross-organizational alignment, and build internal and external ecosystems across AI products, vendors, research institutes, regulators, and industry bodies.
  • Establish a world-class AI  products strategy aligned with enterprise objectives, including product awareness, cross-product roadmap coordination, goal setting, and performance measurement.
  • Define operating models and delivery capabilities to engineer, deliver, operate, and plan releases for AI initiatives and products, ensuring scalable and seamless integration across the organization.
  • Ensure compliance with data privacy, security, and regulatory requirements across all platforms (together with the data protection team).
  • Oversee lifecycle management for AI models, AI agents, blockchain applications, and products in scope, including backlog prioritization, delivery, release planning, deployment, updates, operations, and retirement.
  • Establish standardized frameworks for risk assessment and mitigation, identify product and emerging technology risks, and propose appropriate treatment strategies.
  • Monitor and report on governance KPIs, compliance status, AI product performance, budget utilization, and technology adoption metrics.
  • Facilitate governance forums, decision-making bodies, and the engagement of IT and non-IT capabilities involved throughout the AI products lifecycle, including architecture, security, legal, procurement, branding, and business teams.
  • Lead  AI training and governance ensuring continuous business engagement and acceptance throughout the delivery lifecycle.
  • Collaborate with IT security, legal, procurement, architecture, branding, and business units to understand requirements, propose innovative solutions, and ensure a high-quality user experience aligned with organizational objectives and regulatory obligations.
  • Chair the AI Governance Board and support the definition and efficient management of product budgets while evolving responsible AI policies to address regulatory and ethical requirements such as data usage, model explainability, fairness, and privacy.
  • Cultivate organization-wide literacy, change management, and transformation capabilities to support the adoption, governance, and long-term success of AI-enabled products and emerging technologies.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • University degree or similar higher education in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field.
  • 3-5 years of experience in a similar role.
  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in Technology Product manager.
  • Minimum of 3 year of experience in Technology  governance.
  • Strong understanding, experience and knowledge of AI technologies and blockchain platforms.
  • Expertise in governance frameworks, risk management, and compliance standards (e.g., GDPR, ISO, NIST, EU AI Act, etc).
  • Experience in defining policies and operational standards for emerging technologies.
  • Ability to manage cross-functional collaboration between technology, compliance, and business teams.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, including experience engaging with executives.
  • Familiarity with lifecycle management tools and governance dashboards.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
  • Ability to define strategic goals, give clear directions and drive standardization.
  • Ability to manage different stakeholder expectations.
  • Ability to change the status quo and defend the proposed changes.
  • Ability to act as an effective facilitator.
  • Good knowledge in PowerBI, Monday.com and ServiceNow are an advantag.
  • Deep knowledge in AI and Blockchain technologies, others are an advantage (eg.Quantum).
data.title: AI & Innovative Tech Governance Manager

data.key_responsibilities:
Support the Manager, Transport Client Services – Referees by acting as the operational deputy for all referee transport at the Referees Base Camp Hotel (RBCH). Support the Senior Coordinators, Coordinators, and transport crew, ensuring full coverage, clear tasking, and daily readiness. 

Support all referee-dedicated vehicles and drivers, including briefings, schedules, fuelling, cleaning, compliance, and issue resolution. Maintain constant line-of-sight on daily movements, ensuring vehicles are prepared and positioned well ahead of departures. 

Manage transport operations at the training site, including vehicle flow, parking coordination for all client groups, and upkeep of signage and infrastructure. Resolve operational issues promptly and escalate major incidents immediately. 

Provide daily reporting to the Manager, capturing movements, delays, incidents, and key risks. Ensure accurate communication flow, operational discipline, and delivery of services to FIFA standards. 

The key responsibilities and oversights of the Coordinator, Transport Client Services Referee for FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
Leadership and Team Oversight:
  • Supporting the Manager, Transport Client Services – Referees, ensuring continuity of leadership in daily operations and during periods when the Manager is offsite. 
  • Lead a team of Senior Coordinators and Coordinators at the Referees Base Camp Hotel RBCH. 
  • Provide ongoing coaching, tasking, and performance oversight to all transport crew assigned to referee operations. 
  • Manage daily schedules, rotations, breaks, and welfare for all assigned staff, maintaining constant operational coverage. 
Strategic and Operational Delivery:
  • Implement the Transport Client Services strategic plan and DSLA expectations at the hotel and training site. 
  • Oversee all referee-dedicated vehicles and drivers, ensuring line-of-sight across readiness, daily allocation, vehicle flows, and operational discipline. 
  • Coordinate closely with Fleet, Bus Operations, AND, Venue Operations, Security, and other functional areas to ensure seamless execution of referee movements. 
Cross-FA Coordination:
  • Act as a core contact for the Referees Liaison Officer (RLO), Referees Security Liaison Officer (RSLO), and TSV logistics counterparts. 
  • Align with the RLO and RSLO on daily requirements, priorities, contingencies, and emerging requests. 
  • Support bubble-to-bubble protocols and RSA procedures through close coordination with RSLO and Fleet. 
  • Ensure referee load zones at all sites always remain protected and available. 
Vehicle and Driver Management:
  • Lead daily driver briefings covering movement timings, route plans, expectations, and operational updates. 
  • Ensure all drivers are briefed, prepared, and on standby well ahead of departures. 
  • Monitor compliance with all referee transport policies, procedures, and vehicle standards. 
  • Liaising with Fleet/Bus management to resolve any maintenance issues quickly. 
  • Support drivers with any queries or operational challenges throughout the day. 
Training Site Oversight:
  • Oversee the full transport operation at the RBCTS, including load zone management, vehicle flow, client group parking oversight, and stakeholder coordination. 
  • Ensure the transport infrastructure, signage, and traffic-flow measures are in place before first use and maintained throughout the tournament. 
  • Manage and resolve all transport-related issues or incidents at the training site.
Operational Reporting & Escalations:
  • Provide concise daily reporting to the Manager, Transport Client Services – Referees, including key movements, issues, delays, and escalations. 
  • Immediately escalate any major incidents, safety concerns, or service impacts to the Manager and relevant FAs. 
  • Support data tracking and operational logs as required to align with tournament DSLA and post-event reporting. 
Availability

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Support the Senior Coordinators, Coordinators, and transport crew, ensuring full coverage, clear tasking, and daily readiness. 

Support all referee-dedicated vehicles and drivers, including briefings, schedules, fuelling, cleaning, compliance, and issue resolution. Maintain constant line-of-sight on daily movements, ensuring vehicles are prepared and positioned well ahead of departures. 

Manage transport operations at the training site, including vehicle flow, parking coordination for all client groups, and upkeep of signage and infrastructure. Resolve operational issues promptly and escalate major incidents immediately. 

Provide daily reporting to the Manager, capturing movements, delays, incidents, and key risks. Ensure accurate communication flow, operational discipline, and delivery of services to FIFA standards. 

The key responsibilities and oversights of the Coordinator, Transport Client Services Referee for FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
Leadership and Team Oversight:
  • Supporting the Manager, Transport Client Services – Referees, ensuring continuity of leadership in daily operations and during periods when the Manager is offsite. 
  • Lead a team of Senior Coordinators and Coordinators at the Referees Base Camp Hotel RBCH. 
  • Provide ongoing coaching, tasking, and performance oversight to all transport crew assigned to referee operations. 
  • Manage daily schedules, rotations, breaks, and welfare for all assigned staff, maintaining constant operational coverage. 
Strategic and Operational Delivery:
  • Implement the Transport Client Services strategic plan and DSLA expectations at the hotel and training site. 
  • Oversee all referee-dedicated vehicles and drivers, ensuring line-of-sight across readiness, daily allocation, vehicle flows, and operational discipline. 
  • Coordinate closely with Fleet, Bus Operations, AND, Venue Operations, Security, and other functional areas to ensure seamless execution of referee movements. 
Cross-FA Coordination:
  • Act as a core contact for the Referees Liaison Officer (RLO), Referees Security Liaison Officer (RSLO), and TSV logistics counterparts. 
  • Align with the RLO and RSLO on daily requirements, priorities, contingencies, and emerging requests. 
  • Support bubble-to-bubble protocols and RSA procedures through close coordination with RSLO and Fleet. 
  • Ensure referee load zones at all sites always remain protected and available. 
Vehicle and Driver Management:
  • Lead daily driver briefings covering movement timings, route plans, expectations, and operational updates. 
  • Ensure all drivers are briefed, prepared, and on standby well ahead of departures. 
  • Monitor compliance with all referee transport policies, procedures, and vehicle standards. 
  • Liaising with Fleet/Bus management to resolve any maintenance issues quickly. 
  • Support drivers with any queries or operational challenges throughout the day. 
Training Site Oversight:
  • Oversee the full transport operation at the RBCTS, including load zone management, vehicle flow, client group parking oversight, and stakeholder coordination. 
  • Ensure the transport infrastructure, signage, and traffic-flow measures are in place before first use and maintained throughout the tournament. 
  • Manage and resolve all transport-related issues or incidents at the training site.
Operational Reporting & Escalations:
  • Provide concise daily reporting to the Manager, Transport Client Services – Referees, including key movements, issues, delays, and escalations. 
  • Immediately escalate any major incidents, safety concerns, or service impacts to the Manager and relevant FAs. 
  • Support data tracking and operational logs as required to align with tournament DSLA and post-event reporting. 
Availability
Transport Client Services Referee Coordinators must be available for the following contractual period:
  • Approximately January through July 24, 2026

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organizationally to the Head of FIFA Fan Festival™ – [Host Country Specific], the FIFA Fan Festival™ Venue Manager will play a key role in the delivery of the FIFA Fan Festival™ at the assigned Host City venue. Working in close collaboration with the FIFA Fan Festival™ Core Project Team, key stakeholders in Zurich, and local operational leads, this role will ensure the event is implemented in full alignment with FIFA standards, requirements, and the FIFA Fan Festival™ Event Manual.

The FIFA Fan Festival™ Venue Manager is a senior event specialist with extensive experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder live events. The role requires broad operational understanding and proven experience across the following key areas of responsibility:
Areas of Responsibility:
  • General Event Oversight
  • Venue Set-Up and Infrastructure
  • Marketing, Branding, and Communications
  • Entertainment and Stage Programming
  • Food & Beverage (F&B)
  • FIFA Assets and Partner Integration
  • Reporting and Information Flow
Task & Duties:
The main responsibilities and oversights of the FIFA Fan Festival™ Venue Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Represent FIFA on-site as the lead point of contact for the FIFA Fan Festival™ venue
  • Oversee and ensure compliance with all rights, obligations, and requirements defined in the Host City Agreement, FIFA Fan Festival™ Event Manual, and associated documentation
  • Monitor, report, and support the Host City organizing team in all operational matters throughout assembly, event, and dismantling phases.
  • Facilitate integration of FIFA assets, branding, entertainment, content production, and Commercial Partner activities.
  • Ensure clear and timely communication between FIFA, the Host City, service providers, and FIFA’s Commercial Partners and Licensees.
  • Participate in daily coordination meetings with all relevant stakeholders, including Host City representatives, agencies, and partner teams.
  • Identify, escalate, and assist in resolving on-site operational issues in coordination with the FIFA Fan Festival™ Project Team.
  • Ensure consistency in visitor experience, brand application, and stakeholder integration across all event areas.
  • Oversee and coordinate the work of any assigned FIFA Fan Festival™ Deputy Venue Managers or Coordinators.
  • Deliver comprehensive daily reporting and updates to the FIFA Fan Festival™ Head of Country and Core Team.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or equivalent higher education in Event Management, Live Entertainment, Production, or a related field. 
Work Experience
  • Minimum 8 years of experience managing large-scale live entertainment events, festivals, or concerts, ideally at an international level.
  • Proven ability to operate in complex stakeholder environments involving government, sponsors, agencies, and media.
  • Demonstrated experience in multi-disciplinary event oversight (production, logistics, broadcast, partnerships rights delivery, fan experience).
  • Familiarity with large temporary venue build-outs and multi-day programming structures.
  • Strong technical understanding of event infrastructure (staging, lighting, sound, screens, broadcast integration).
  • Experience leading operational teams through high-pressure, time-critical environments.
  • The role prioritizes operational excellence and hands-on experience in delivering large-scale public events with focus on entertainment, culture and lifestyle. 
Languages 
  • Fluent English (written and spoken) required. (understanding of French & Spanish considered an asset)
  • Fluent in Spanish when applying for Mexico (proficient also in English) 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organizationally to the Head of Recruiting, the Recruitment Manager will play a critical and high-impact role in driving large-scale workforce hiring and operational readiness for the FIFA World Cup 2026™. This is an extremely high-volume and high-intensity position — not a traditional recruitment role — requiring exceptional organization, resilience, and the ability to manage multiple complex projects simultaneously. 

The Recruitment Manager will not only facilitate full-cycle recruitment but also lead and execute various workforce-related initiatives that involve filling hundreds of positions across multiple workforce types, each with unique processes, systems, and compliance requirements. This role demands strong project management capabilities, meticulous attention to detail, and the ability to maintain accuracy and efficiency while operating under significant pressure and evolving deadlines. 

We are seeking self-starters and proactive leaders who can navigate challenges, adapt quickly, and push critical objectives to completion on time. The ideal candidate will thrive in ambiguity, possess strong leadership and problem-solving skills, and demonstrate the ability to build, refine, and scale processes to meet the operational needs of a global event. 

This is a dynamic, hands-on role that requires balancing high-volume recruitment with strategic workforce execution — managing competing priorities, coordinating across functions, and ensuring delivery excellence for one of the largest sporting events in history. 

This will be a 6-month temporary contract role.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Recruitment Specialist for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Lead end-to-end recruitment processes, from initial talent sourcing and screening to selection and offer negotiation, ensuring a seamless experience for both candidates and hiring managers.  
  • Must be comfortable with a high-volume requisition load (150-250 roles at a time). 
  • Strong knowledge of modern Project Management tools, Applicant Tracking Systems, and HR technologies used to attract, nurture and manage a pipeline of talent and Onboarding of all candidates. 
  • Demonstrated ability to project manage, prioritize effectively, and work independently. 
  • Monitor recruitment metrics (e.g., candidate quality, source effectiveness, time to fill) and use data to drive continuous improvement  
  • Execute recruitment strategies for high-volume, tournament-time roles and niche skill sets, building a strong pipeline of candidates for specialized roles in an ever-evolving environment.  
  • Transition seamlessly from recruitment responsibilities to operational support during tournament events, assisting with staff mobilization and management as required.  
  • Collaborate with HR and workforce teams to adapt recruitment processes to meet high-intensity, time-sensitive tournament requirements, ensuring rapid deployment of personnel across venues. 
  • Coordinate with other operational teams to ensure that recruitment aligns with logistical and staffing needs during tournament phases, providing a proactive approach to personnel planning.

  • Demonstrated ability to project manage, prioritize effectively, manage deadlines, and work independently.

data.key_responsibilities:
This is an evergreen position supporting FIFA World Cup 26™ stadium Guest Operations across official Host Cities in the United States, Mexico, and Canada.

Final assignment location(s) will be determined based on operational needs, candidate availability, and tournament requirements. Candidates may be considered for one or multiple Host Cities.

Guest Operations Stadium Manager - Role Description

Reporting organizationally to the Central Stadium Operations team, the Guest Operations Stadium Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Guest Operations team. This role works in close collaboration with Safety & Security, Venue Management, Hospitality, Guest Relations, and other key stakeholders responsible for delivering VVIP/VIP operations at the assigned stadium.

The main responsibilities and oversight of the Guest Operations Stadium Manager include:
  • Oversee and implement all VVIP/VIP Guest Operations at the stadium during tournament time, maintaining full visibility of daily activities
  • Establish and implement Guest Operations policies and procedures, ensuring compliance with national protocols for Heads of State and government officials, where applicable
  • Lead the onsite Guest Operations Program (GOP) team through training, guidance, escalation, and reporting to GOP Central
  • Act as the local Guest Operations representative, coordinating operational decisions with other functional areas onsite (e.g., security, access control, hospitality, transport, signage, IT, cleaning & waste)
  • Provide operational leadership and decision-making authority for all Guest Operations personnel, including tournament staff, venue staff, volunteers, and suppliers, in alignment with FIFA standards
  • Ensure readiness of Guest Operations areas (lounges, access control points, signage, wayfinding) prior to the tournament and on a match-by-match basis
  • Lead authority pre-visits alongside the Protocol Officer, gathering requirements and coordinating operational needs (e.g., parking, accreditations, entourage passes)
  • Implement and follow the Site Operations Plan (SOP) in collaboration with FIFA Guest Operations, communicating details and exceptions to the onsite team as needed
  • Communicate anticipated VVIP/VIP guest volumes and high-profile attendees (e.g., legends, dignitaries, celebrities) ahead of each match to Venue Management and Security
  • Coordinate logistical requirements across all relevant stakeholders
  • Support agreed guest management and protocol service levels across all operational partners
  • Monitor, escalate, and support resolution of risks or issues impacting VVIP/VIP operations
  • Prepare and deliver daily operational reports to the Central Guest Operations team
  • Support post-tournament reporting, debriefs, and after-action reviews, ensuring feedback is captured to support continuous improvement
VVIP Lounge Manager - Role Description

Reporting organizationally to the Guest Operations Stadium Manager, the VVIP Lounge Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Guest Operations team. This role works closely with Hospitality, Guest Relations, Venue Management, and other key stakeholders responsible for delivering a world-class VVIP/VIP lounge experience at the assigned stadium.

The main responsibilities and oversight of the VVIP Lounge Manager include:
  • Lead the on-site execution of the V/VIP lounge guest experience, including branding, entertainment, staffing, and decor.  
  • Oversee guest management and ensure protocol service levels are met a agreed between all stakeholders.  
  • Ensure the delivery of exceptional, world-class hospitality service to all guests.  
  • Monitor hospitality suppliers to ensure quality, budget compliance, and risk management.  
  • Address and escalate queries related to stadium infrastructure and technical aspects of guest areas.  
  • Manage VIK sponsor needs and ensure implementation aligns with agreed plans.  
  • Support world-class training programs focused on delivering exceptional customer service.  
  • Participate in planning meetings, provide updates, and prepare documents to support guest experience initiatives.  
  • Fill in the required reports and escalate necessary issues to the central team  
  • Ensure successful knowledge sharing and post-event debriefing for Guest Operations
Location
This role supports stadium operations across multiple FIFA World Cup 26™ Host Cities. Not all roles will be required in every Host City.

Current Host Cities include, but are not limited to:
United States: Atlanta, Georgia · Boston, Massachusetts · Dallas, Texas · Houston, Texas · Kansas City, Missouri · Los Angeles, California · Miami, Florida · New York / New Jersey Area · Philadelphia, Pennsylvania · San Francisco, California · Seattle, Washington

Mexico: Guadalajara, Jalisco · Mexico City · Monterrey, Nuevo León

Canada: All positions have been filled.

data.key_responsibilities:
These evergreen roles support VIP Hotel, FIFA Club, Guest Operations, and Leisure Program delivery for the FIFA World Cup 26™ across official Host Cities in the United States, Mexico, and Canada.

Final assignment(s) will be determined based on operational needs and candidate availability.

FIFA Club Assistant Manager - Role Overview
Reporting to the FIFA Club Manager, the FIFA Club Assistant Manager supports daily FIFA Club operations at assigned VIP Hotels, ensuring consistent service delivery, access control, and an exceptional guest experience.
Key Responsibilities
  • Support all FIFA Club operations, including desk services, F&B, entertainment, staffing, and access control
  • Implement Guest Management policies and procedures
  • Coordinate guest needs with Guest Operations, Guest Relations, and Executive Office
  • Maintain FIFA Club presentation and readiness throughout operations
  • Prepare daily operational reports and maintain accurate records

FIFA Club Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the VIP Hotel & Leisure Program Manager, the FIFA Club Coordinator supports frontline FIFA Club operations and guest services.
Key Responsibilities
  • Assist with guest greetings, access control, opening/closing duties, and services
  • Maintain awareness of FIFA Club offerings and daily schedules
  • Support discreet security awareness and guest familiarity
  • Coordinate guest needs with Transport, Accommodations, and Guest Relations
  • Adapt to changing schedules and operational requirements

Guest Hotel GOP Assistant Manager - Role Overview
Reporting to the Guest Hotel Manager, the Guest Hotel GOP Assistant Manager supports hotel-based Guest Operations and VIP hospitality delivery during the tournament.
Key Responsibilities
  • Support Guest Hotel operations and integrated event delivery
  • Assist with implementation of V/VIP guest experience concepts
  • Act as secondary contact for infrastructure and guest experience issues
  • Support training programs for staff and volunteers
  • Coordinate suppliers, reporting, and operational planning

Guest Hotel GOP Manager - Role Overview
Reporting to Central Hotel Operations, the Guest Hotel GOP Manager leads Guest Operations delivery at assigned Guest Hotels.
Key Responsibilities
  • Oversee Guest Hotel operations and VIP hospitality programs
  • Lead implementation of guest experience concepts and service standards
  • Serve as primary escalation point for infrastructure and guest issues
  • Manage suppliers, budgets, timelines, and risk
  • Maintain strategic oversight and reporting throughout the tournament

Leisure Program Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the VIP Hotel & Leisure Program Manager, the Leisure Program Coordinator supports premium leisure activities for priority guest groups.
Key Responsibilities
  • Maintain oversight of leisure offerings, schedules, capacities, and itineraries
  • Enter and manage bookings within leisure systems
  • Coordinate with program providers on logistics and guest needs
  • Ensure service delivery aligns with Guest Operations standards
  • Adapt to schedule changes and guest expectations

VIP Hotel Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the VIP Hotel & Leisure Program Manager, the VIP Hotel Coordinator supports daily VIP Hotel guest services and operations.
Key Responsibilities
  • Support welcome desk and FIFA Club guest services
  • Coordinate arrivals, departures, gifts, tickets, and package deliveries
  • Maintain awareness of hotel offerings and schedules
  • Coordinate guest needs with Transport and Accommodations
  • Ensure hotel presentation and service standards are maintained

Welcome Desk Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the VIP Hotel & Leisure Program Manager, the Welcome Desk Coordinator supports frontline guest engagement at VIP Hotels.
Key Responsibilities
  • Assist with guest arrivals, departures, ticket requests, and services
  • Maintain awareness of daily hotel operations and offerings
  • Ensure compliance with Guest Operations policies
  • Coordinate guest needs with Transport and Accommodations
  • Deliver a consistent, welcoming guest experience

Locations : Assignments span multiple Host Cities. Not all roles are required in every location.

United States: Atlanta, Georgia · Boston, Massachusetts · Dallas, Texas · Houston, Texas · Kansas City, Missouri · Los Angeles, California · Miami, Florida · New York / New Jersey Area · Philadelphia, Pennsylvania · San Francisco, California · Seattle, Washington

Mexico: Guadalajara, Jalisco · Mexico City · Monterrey, Nuevo León

Canada: Toronto, Ontario

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Senior Manager Central Logistics the Logistics Administration Specialist will be a key member of the FIFA26 Logistics team, and work in close collaboration with FIFA Zurich and other key individuals responsible for delivering the Material Logistics operations across Canada, Mexico and the USA.

This role will be responsible for supporting the central logistical operations during the planning and delivery phases to provide a seamless logistics operation throughout the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 & FIFA World Cup 2026. Further, this role will support throughout the planning and delivery stages coordinating logistics activities across the end-to-end supply chain including customs, warehouse, distribution, materials and venue.

The primary responsibilities of the Coordinator Logistics Administration Specialist for the FIFA World Cup 2026 are to:
  • Support Central Logistics operations for the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 & FIFA World Cup 2026., including program management, development, implementation and support of Logistics Coordination Centre (LCC)
  • Support with contract management of the Official Logistics Service Provider (LSP) and other Logistics equipment suppliers to ensure adherence to contractual KPIs and service levels
  • Support on the overall planning, implementation, operational process across Central Logistics and LCC operations to achieve a seamless supply chain
  • Coordinate with various stakeholders, including Host Cities, FIFA Client Groups, suppliers, and service providers, to capture requirements and align with logistics operations
  • Assist with programme management of logistics teams (materials, venues, customs and freight forwarding, warehousing, PMA and distribution) to ensure alignment on logistics requirements, timelines, and expectations
  • Assist with maintaining the Risks & Issues Register, aligning contingency plans to address unforeseen circumstances that may arise during the tournament
  • Assist with drafting and tracking of policies and procedures
  • Assist in analyzing supply chain data to identify trends, gaps, and opportunities for optimization, providing actionable insights to the Logistics team
  • Generate regular and ad hoc reports on Logistics planning progress, budgets, and logistics to support management decisions, including daily/weekly logistics situational reporting
  • Support with implementation of technological solutions and digital platforms to streamline logistical processes, enhance tracking, and improve real-time communication
  • Help create and maintain user guides, step-by-step instructions, and training materials for the Logistics team
  • Contribute to the development of presentations and visual aids for stakeholder communication on Logistics planning processes and updates.
  • Assist with monitoring and managing the Central Logistics budget 
  • Support in the organization of meetings with internal stakeholders to review logistics needs, address issues, and provide updates.
  • Manage stakeholder logistics requests by facilitating logistics change requests communicating changes, and ensuring all updates are accurately tracked.
  • Monitor logistics schedules, updating stakeholders on any reported delays or issues
  • Be available to work shifts during tournament times, within the Logistics Coordination Centre as required.
  • Document best practices and procedures to ensure clear tracking and transfer of knowledge Participate in outreach programs, as required, intended to educate stakeholders on tournament time operations
  • Support on-ground logistics operations if required, possibly travelling to other locations
This role requires strong attention to detail and a customer-oriented approach to support stakeholders and contribute to successfully executing the FIFA Club World Cup 2025, FIFA World Cup 2026, and other related events.

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
Sitting within the Operational Services Department, Logistics (along with Guest Operations, Language Services, Accommodation, Transport, and Travel) aims to deliver the most engaging, innovative, and accessible World Cup experience for all athletes, fans, partners, and the community.   

Reporting to the Procurement and Dissolution Logistics Manager, the Manager Asset Control & Dissolution will support the asset management, documentation, and dissolution processes for FIFA World Cup 2026, and other related FIFA events.

This role focuses on ensuring all assets are accurately tracked, documented, and prepared for efficient and sustainable dissolution, while supporting procurement and other departments in maintaining full asset visibility. 

The Manager works closely with the Procurement and Dissolution Logistics Manager to ensure asset registers are complete, documentation is up to date, WMS and Asset Tracking Systems are correctly maintained, and dissolution activities are executed in compliance with FIFA standards. 

The primary responsibilities of the Manager Asset Control & Dissolution for the FIFA World Cup 2026 include: 
Asset Tracking & Registration
  • Maintain and continuously update the Asset Register for all FF&E and other assets across all events.
  • Work in the Warehouse Management System (WMS) and Asset Tracking Systems to ensure accurate asset statuses, locations, descriptions, and codes.
  • Verify that all assets have complete metadata, including technical specifications, manuals, warranty documentation, handling instructions, and photos.
  • Track asset movements across warehouses, venues, suppliers, and dissolution sites.
  • Assist in reconciling physical inventories with system inventories and identifying discrepancies. 
Asset Control for Assets Procured by Other Departments
  • Support the tracking of assets procured by other FIFA departments (IT, Broadcast, Commercial, Marketing, Competition Management, etc.).
  • Ensure these assets are integrated into the central Asset Register and contain all required documentation.
  • Coordinate with departments to collect missing specifications, certifications, or technical documentation. ·       Follow up on asset updates, changes, or corrections and ensure the Asset Control processes are followed consistently.
  • Assist in preparing these assets for dissolution, ensuring processes follow departmental and FIFA guidelines. 
Documentation & Archiving 
  • Collect, verify, and archive all procurement-related documentation, including POs, contracts, delivery notes, warranty certificates, manuals, and technical sheets.
  • Organize documentation in accordance with internal controls, ensuring materials are version-controlled and easily accessible to internal stakeholders.
  • Ensure document completeness before assets enter operational use or dissolution processes.
  • Support audits, reconciliations, and data-quality reviews by providing required documentation packages.
Support to Procurement & Dissolution Manager
  • Assist in developing dissolution documentation, asset lists, preparation forms, and return instructions.
  • Prepare reports and summaries of asset statuses, documentation completeness, and dissolution readiness.
  • Support communication with suppliers, warehouses, and disposal partners to align on timelines, requirements, and return workflows.
  • Assist in monitoring and coordinating dissolution activities during post-tournament phases. 
Supplier and Vendor Coordination
  • Liaise with suppliers and vendors to obtain missing documentation (specifications, warranty papers, manuals, disposal guidelines).
  • Support communication regarding asset returns, collections, damages, discrepancies, and dissolution requirements.
  • Assist with verifying documentation and aligning supplier lists with FIFA asset data. 
Operational and Tournament-Time Responsibilities
  • Support Venue Logistics teams during operations, including asset checks, documentation collection, and system updates.
  • Assist with stock checks, asset labeling, on-site inspections, and inventory verification at venues or warehouses.
  • Support damage reporting workflow by collecting information, photos, and documentation.
  • Be ready to work on shifts at the Logistics Coordination Center to support daily asset-related service requests and issue resolution.
  • Participate in site visits, vendor visits, and physical asset inspections as required. 
Overall Responsibilities
  • Demonstrate strong organization skills, attention to detail, and reliability in managing large volumes of data and documentation.
  • Maintain professional communication with internal and external stakeholders.
  • Support a collaborative and inclusive team environment, ensuring smooth coordination with Warehousing, Distribution, Venue Logistics, and other Logistics workstreams.
  • Show a customer-oriented approach, ensuring the needs of Functional Areas are understood and supported.
  • Support the Procurement and Dissolution Logistics Manager and Senior Manager, Materials, in tasks such as reporting, presentations, training, document development, and administrative coordination.
Continuously seek opportunities to improve documentation workflows, asset accuracy, and system data quality.

data.key_responsibilities:
The Training Site Venue Transport Supervisor will play a pivotal role within the FIFA26 Transport team, collaborating closely with the FIFA26 Team Services team. This position is responsible for assisting with the conceptualization and operational planning of all transportation operations provided to FIFA constituents at Venue Specific Training Sites and Team Base Camp Training Sites. We are looking for a self-starter and multitasker who thrives under pressure and is committed to following through on tasks.

Key responsibilities and oversights of the Training Site Venue Transport Supervisor for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Ensure cost-effective and efficient operations at training sites.
  • Assist the Manager in developing plans and budgets while managing all operational activities for Venue Specific Training Sites and Team Base Camp Training Sites.
  • Create comprehensive Transport Plans for all FIFA constituents at Venue Specific Training Sites and Team Base Camp Training Sites.
  • Help develop interim access plans for FIFA Club World Cup Training Sites.
  • Coordinate with key stakeholders to secure necessary agreements and approvals.
  • Collaborate with all FIFA26 Functional Areas and transport systems to align plans with overall transport proposals.
  • Assist managing contracted staff at each Training Site
  • Coordinate with Security to ensure transport plans are integrated with security measures at all Venue Specific Training Sites and Team Base Camp Training Sites.
  • Create walkthroughs, testing, and readiness exercise plans.
  • Support the delivery of transport services for all test events leading up to the FIFA World Cup 2026

data.description: At FIFA World Cup 2026, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest, most innovative, and inclusive sporting event of all time. The 2026 World Cup will mark the first time that the tournament will be hosted by three countries: the United States, Canada, and Mexico. This new format redefines excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement from fans and players. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
Volunteers are at the heart of all FIFA events, impacting all that experience the Tournaments, and represents a critical component to the successful delivery of FIFA World Cup 26TM. As football unites the world, the FIFA World Cup 2026™ Volunteer Program provides a unique opportunity to unite and connect with individuals and communities across North America. Like in all FIFA tournaments, volunteers will become the “face” of the FIFA World Cup 2026™ enhancing the profile, reputation and appeal of football, FIFA, and Host Cities on a global stage. The Volunteer Program will be a driver to help achieve strategic tournament objectives alongside acting as a vehicle for positive social change, by helping strengthen social connections, increase in civic pride, skills, and creating a movement to sustain and grow volunteering across Host Countries and Host Cities.

The Position 
Reporting organizationally to FIFA’s Host City Manager, the Volunteer Coordinator for San Francisco will help serve Volunteer efforts for the FIFA World Cup 2026TM that for the first time ever, is hosting 48 teams and 1,200+ players across 3 countries and 16 cities.

FIFA is seeking a reliable, proactive, and service-oriented Host City Coordinator to support the day-to-day delivery of the Volunteer Program. This role is essential to ensuring the smooth and professional operation of the volunteer center, while creating a memorable experience for all attending candidates and volunteers supporting the operations. The coordinator will focus on customer service, operational excellence, and database management, aligning with the specific needs and strategic planning of each city. The position contributes to building an integrated program across host cities, with a strong emphasis on achieving local recruitment objectives.  

The main responsibilities of the Host City Volunteer Coordinator include:
  • Logistics & Operational Support: Work with the Host City Volunteer Manager to ensure a seamless operational readiness of the Volunteer Center, including logistical setup and coordination, and operations through the recruitment phase of the Center. Assist in general upkeep of back-of-house areas, including office and break spaces.  Assist with Volunteer Platform system maintenance. Coordinate with security, IT, and facility management as required to ensure a safe and functional venue. 
  • Recruitment Operations: Assist with the overall operations of the Volunteer Center. Manage the welcome desk and ensure positive first impressions for all arriving volunteers. Provide wayfinding assistance throughout the Volunteer Center, guiding attendees through various zones (e.g. check-in, video room, meeting pods, legacy zone). Respond to general inquiries from volunteers and staff in a professional and informed manner.
  • Facilities & Hospitality: Monitor and restock food and beverage areas as needed for volunteers and staff. Support maintaining a clean and tidy environment to ensure a welcoming space for all attendees throughout the day. Report and resolve any process, facility, tech or equipment issues in a timely manner.
  • Volunteer Experience: Ensure all spaces remain welcoming, functional, and aligned with FIFA's standards of experience. Be an on-site ambassador for the FIFA Volunteer Program, helping to create a positive and memorable journey for every participant.

* This role is a fixed term role with the opportunity for extension through July 2026, based on performance. Additional phases of the program would include, selection and role offer, training and scheduling, tournament operations at the stadium and different city locations.
In accordance with California' s Equal Pay and Opportunity Act, the salary range for this position is $60,000 - $70,000. This estimate is based on this position being performed in California, and the range may not apply to similar positions in other locations. In determining actual base pay, FIFA 26 considers candidate education, experience, and other job-related factors allowed by law. An employee’s position within the salary range will be determined by various factors, including relevant education, experience, qualifications, certifications, skills, geographic location, performance metrics, shift, travel requirements, collective bargaining agreements, and business needs.
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.key_responsibilities:
These roles support FIFA World Cup 26™ Protocol and Seating operations across official Host Cities in the United States, Mexico, and Canada. 

Final assignments will be determined based on operational needs, candidate availability, and tournament requirements.

Protocol & Seating Lead Support (Central) - Role Overview
Reporting to the Protocol & Seating Leads, this centrally based role supports protocol and seating operations across matches and regions.
Key Responsibilities
  • Support VVIP/VIP seating changes and updates in BSMManage executive suite inventory
  • Monitor guest and ticket volumes per match
  • Coordinate closely with Ticketing, Hospitality, and venue Protocol Officers
  • Support reporting, guest lists, and ad-hoc operational needs
Seating & Ticketing Coordinator (Venue-Based) - Role Overview
Reporting to the Protocol Officer, this role supports on-site VVIP/VIP seating, ticketing, and protocol delivery at the stadium.
Key Responsibilities
  • Coordinate VVIP/VIP seating and ticket resolution on match days
  • Support Protocol Officers with seating, ticketing, and name tags
  • Manage VIP wristbands and on-site ticket distribution
  • Coordinate guest journey plans and transportation needs
  • Ensure exceptional service delivery in all VIP/VVIP areas

Seating & Ticketing Support (Central ) - Role Overview
Reporting to the Regional Seating Manager, this centrally based role supports VVIP/VIP seating and ticketing operations across assigned regions.
Key Responsibilities
  • Support regional seating and ticketing delivery
  • Manage ticket distribution and issue resolution
  • Maintain BSM data and reporting
  • Monitor guest volumes and match allocations
  • Coordinate with Protocol Officers and functional teams

VVIP Seating Manager (Central / Regional)Role Overview 
Reporting to the Protocol & Seating Lead, the VVIP Seating Manager oversees seating and protocol operations across assigned regions. This is a centrally based role.
Key Responsibilities
  • Lead VVIP/VIP seating and protocol delivery across matches
  • Act as escalation point for complex or sensitive issues
  • Manage seating and ticketing in BSM
  • Prepare guest lists and seating plans for approval
  • Maintain continuous coordination with venue Protocol Officers and functional areas
Locations : Assignments span multiple Host Cities. Not all roles are required in every location.

United States: Atlanta, Georgia · Boston, Massachusetts · Dallas, Texas · Houston, Texas · Kansas City, Missouri · Los Angeles, California · Miami, Florida · New York / New Jersey Area · Philadelphia, Pennsylvania · San Francisco, California · Seattle, Washington

Mexico: Guadalajara, Jalisco · Mexico City · Monterrey, Nuevo León

Canada: All positions have been filled.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

data.key_responsibilities:
These evergreen roles support FIFA World Cup 26™ Guest Relations across official Host Cities in the United States, Mexico, and Canada.

Final assignments will be determined based on operational needs, candidate availability, and tournament requirements.

Guest Hotline Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the Guest Relations Manager, the Guest Hotline Coordinator supports centralized guest services by managing the FIFA guest support hotline and ensuring timely, accurate, and professional assistance across all guest groups.
Key Responsibilities
  • Manage and operate the central guest support hotline
  • Log, track, and escalate guest inquiries and requests in coordination with service owners (travel, transport, accommodation, etc.
  • Support implementation of hotline procedures, service standards, and communication protocols
  • Ensure VIP/VVIP interactions are handled with discretion and professionalism
  • Perform additional duties as assigned

Guest Liaison Agent (Football Concierge) - Role Overview
Reporting to the Guest Liaison Lead, the Guest Liaison Agent delivers tailored, high-touch services to FIFA Senior Officials and Council members, working closely with Guest Relations, Protocol, and venue teams.
Key Responsibilities
  • Maintain daily reporting for VVIP/VIP transport and match attendance
  • Manage guest profiles, itineraries, and registrations in IFES
  • Build trusted relationships with FIFA Council members and entourages
  • Coordinate gifts, uniforms, and match-day movements
  • Support seating arrangements in collaboration with Protocol Officers
  • Maintain continuous cross-functional communication

Guest Relations Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the Guest Relations Lead, the Guest Relations Coordinator maintains a strategic overview of guest services delivery and supports integrated event services for FIFA guest groups.
Key Responsibilities
  • Coordinate guest invitation concepts and guest lists with stakeholders
  • Maintain guest data in IFES (profiles, travel, accommodation, tickets)
  • Support ticketing and guest request management processes
  • Coordinate use of guest IT tools and applications
  • Ensure consistent service quality across all guest touchpoints
  • Prepare progress reports and support continuous improvement

Team Protocol Liaison Officer - Role Overview
Reporting to the Team Protocol Liaison Lead, this role serves as the primary point of contact for participating teams’ protocol representatives, ensuring a seamless guest experience throughout the tournament.
Key Responsibilities
  • Act as the central liaison for team guest-related matters
  • Maintain daily communication with team protocol contacts
  • Anticipate and resolve guest issues proactively
  • Update ticketing and guest management systems
  • Coordinate additional ticket and parking requests
  • Collaborate with Team Services, Transport, Accommodation, Ticketing, and Protocol teams

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the TOC Support - Services Integration Duty Manager, the TOC Support – Services Technology Systems Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Services Integration Area team, and work in close collaboration with the Central Services and other key individuals responsible for delivering Technical support role to monitor performance of all Services Systems and tools, acting as main liaison with ITCC, triaging issues reported from the venue and TOC administration levels, and registering Service Desk tickets with technical recommendations for priority action and resolution.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the TOC Support – Services Technology Systems Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Managing the Services Technology Products help desk communications and associated hotline and inbox established for Services Teams to seek advice on their systems related issues.  
  • Manage a detailed request log with correct data, triaging all reported issues and incidents, either in WeTrack or Service Desk that are impacting services business continuity as required, to maintain a centralized view of all bugs / issues / risks for action/resolution.
  • Provide first line resolution and advice to Services support requests reducing need to log a tickets if resolution can be obtained. 
  • Submit system access requests to Service Desk for all Services related apps and tools 
  • Enhances the existing data analysis and reporting capacity of the TOC Support – Services Integration team, providing dynamic resolution to unforeseen operational business challenges, analyzing system processes, providing hot fix solutions and/or recommendations for backlog developments post event.

data.key_responsibilities:
The Transport Mobility Operations team plays a critical role in delivering all transportation services for FIFA World Cup 26™ client groups, including PMAs, Referees, Guests (VIP/VVIP/Legends), Workforce, Media, and other tournament stakeholders. 

This evergreen position supports the planning, coordination, and execution of all transport operations across Host Cities, stadiums, hotels, training sites, airports/FBOs, depots, and the International Broadcast Center. This job posting supports multiple Venue Transport & Mobility roles, including:
  • Transport Mobility Supervisor
  • Transport Side Events Supervisor
Transport Mobility Supervisor – Key Responsibilities
  • Support live transport operations from the Host City Mobility Operations Center, monitoring vehicle movements, travel times, and route conditions in real time.
  • Coordinate with Host City partners, public transport authorities, and FIFA Functional Areas to align city mobility plans with FIFA operational requirements.
  • Identify, escalate, and support mitigation of transport disruptions, delays, and incidents through established communication channels.
  • Monitor the Tournament Route Network, shuttle systems, and public transport services to ensure reliable transport for FIFA clients and workforce.
  • Distribute accurate transport updates to Venue Transport Managers, Bus Operations, and Fleet Coordination teams.
  • Collaborate with Security, Spectator Services, and Local Authorities to support access and egress between transport hubs and venue zones.
  • Record operational data and contribute to daily situation reports to support performance tracking and continuous improvement.
  • Support FIFA VIP and Special Events transport operations as required.
Transport Side Events Supervisor – Key Responsibilities
  • Oversee on-site transport operations for side events, including load zones, parking, shuttle staging, and driver coordination.
  • Serve as the primary on-the-ground transport point of contact for side events within the Host City during tournament time.
  • Develop and execute short-notice transport plans for special or unplanned events, coordinating routes, staging areas, vehicles, and staffing.
  • Liaise with Security, Guest Operations, Venue Transport, Fleet, and City Transport Control Centres to align access, logistics, and operational needs.
  • Communicate directly with event leads, VIP guest managers, and Functional Areas to confirm requirements and translate them into transport plans.
  • Coordinate with Fleet Operations and Dispatch to secure vehicles, drivers, and escorts as required.
  • Maintain event logs, route plans, and incident reports to support debriefs and continuous improvement.
  • Support access control measures and temporary road closures in coordination with local authorities.

Location
This role supports operations across multiple FIFA World Cup 26™ Host Cities. Not all roles will be required in every Host City. Current Host Cities include, but are not limited to:

United States:
Atlanta, Georgia · Boston, Massachusetts · Dallas, Texas · Houston, Texas · Kansas City, Missouri · Los Angeles, California · New York / New Jersey Area · Philadelphia, Pennsylvania · San Francisco, California · Seattle, Washington

Mexico:
Guadalajara, Jalisco · Mexico City · Monterrey, Nuevo León

Canada:
Please apply to the "Venue Transport Operations - FIFA World Cup 26™ | All Host Cities" posting.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor's degree in Transport, Project Management, Event Management, Major Sporting Events, or a combination of education and experience relevant to the position.  
Work Experience
  • 2 years’ work experience: ideally within the football, sport, or event industry. 
  • Proven experience managing rapid-response operations or short-turnaround projects. 
  • Demonstrated ability to work effectively with multiple stakeholders under tight timelines. 
  • Strong interpersonal skills, proactive communication, and confidence in decision-making. 
  • Able to effectively present information and respond to questions from support staff, managers, and clients.  
  • Demonstrates the highest levels of integrity, professionalism, and commitment.  
  • Positive attitude, patience, and persistence in problem-solving.  
  • Strong cultural awareness and the ability to work in an international environment. 
  • Client facing communication and guest services experience is a plus. 
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a strong advantage given the tri-host nature of the tournament (USA–Canada–Mexico).
  • Additional languages spoken by global client groups are a plus.
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project).
  • Comfortable working with planning software, mobility dashboards, and online collaboration tools.
  • Ability to quickly learn and operate specialized tournament transport systems such as the Transport Management Module
  • Experience with ticketing, scheduling, dispatching, or CRM-style tools is an advantage.
data.title: Transport Mobility Operations - FIFA World Cup 26™ | All Host Cities

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting to Executive Director of Legacy and Stakeholder Affairs, the FWWC2027 Legacy Manager, under the guidance of the Executive Director of Legacy and Stakeholder Affairs, will oversee all matters related to the development, implementation and reporting of the FWWC 2027 Legacy Programme in close collaboration and with strategic guidance from the Women’s Football Division in FIFA.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Oversee and implement the FWWC 2027 Legacy Programme with the objective of delivering together with all relevant stakeholders a coordinated, long-lasting and tangible impact on the host country and region after hosting the event.
  • Coordinate the legacy activities of the CBF, FIFA and any other stakeholders related to the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027.
  • Bring to life the FWWC 2027 legacy framework consisting of three core pillars:
    1. Women’s Football Legacy: work with CBF, host cities and relevant stakeholders to build a positive legacy on the women’s football landscape in Brazil and the region.
    2. Social Impact Legacy: work with CBF, the Brazilian Federal Government, State Governments, NGO’s and other relevant stakeholders to identify, build and implement a lasting social impact leveraging the FWWC 2027 with a particular focus on gender-based violence and improving the position of women in Brazilian society.
    3. Infrastructure Legacy: work with relevant stakeholders to develop, record and report on infrastructure legacy opportunities and projects that have arisen due to hosting of the FWWC 2027.
  • Prepare and finalise a report to be released post-tournament which outlines the tangible impacts of the FWWC Legacy Programme including measures of both qualitative and quantitative impact.
  • Support the Women’s Football Division on any other matters related to the FWWC 2027 Legacy programme as and when required.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Experience in Major Sports Events and Brazilian based organisations:
  •  Experience in a similar role within a Brazilian based organisation, with a clear track record of developing and implementing Legacy programmes and activities linked to major sporting events.
  •  Proven track record of working in women’s football in Brazil (and the wider Region) with clear links and networks to key women’s football stakeholders.
Legacy Development 
  • Proven track record of developing and implementing legacy strategies. 
  • Experience in Project Management including planning, organizing, and delivering complex projects and campaigns.
  • Experience in delivering post-event reporting.   
Communication & Stakeholder Relations: 
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills to liaise with high-level stakeholders, including government officials, commercial partners and media (when necessary). 
  • Experience in managing relationships with diverse international stakeholders, including commercial partners, local authorities, and FIFA officials.
Cultural Competency & Multinational Experience: 
  • Experience working in multinational environments and understanding cultural nuances when coordinating across countries.
  • Fluent in English and Portuguese (Brazilian). 
  • Right to work in Brazil and ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
data.title: Legacy Manager

data.description: Reporting organisationally to the Catering Manager, the Senior Catering Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA25 and FIFA26 Catering Management team, and will be responsible for assisting in planning, executing, and overseeing catering services, ensuring exceptional dining experiences for Teams, Youth Programme, Media, Broadcasting, Workforce and Volunteers.
data.key_responsibilities:
The main responsibilities and oversights of the Senior Catering Coordinator for the FIFA Club World Cup 2025™ and FIFA World Cup 26™ include:  
  • Assist the Catering Manager with procurement, supplier agreements, Requests for Menu Proposals, Statements of Work (SOWs), and related processes in coordination with the procurement team.
  • Assist in authoring, creating and implementing standardized policies and procedures for catering operations, ensuring compliance with food safety regulations and addressing specific requirements across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico.
  • Support staff training and orientation to maintain consistent service quality and adherence to catering guidelines.
  • Facilitate the exchange of essential documents and information between central and on-site teams.
  • Provide support for rate card services tailored to media and team catering needs.
  • Manage meal voucher distribution and reconciliation.
  • Assist with contractor and Functional Area (FA) liaison to ensure smooth coordination.
  • Meal Forecasting and Planning: Compile and analyze catering plans to inform budget allocation.
  • Collaborate closely with the Accreditation and Security teams to ensure a seamless experience for catering providers.
  • Support the development of an operational plan for on-site staff.
  • Track key data points related to catering operations and maintain organized records.
  • Work closely with the Look & Feel and Signage teams to provide content for wayfinding signage in workforce restaurants.
  • Collaborate on sustainability efforts, promoting eco-friendly practices such as responsible sourcing and waste reduction in catering services.
  • Assist in post-event evaluations, gathering feedback and contributing insights to enhance catering operations during 2025 and 2026 tournaments as well as for future tournaments.

data.key_responsibilities:
The Content Services Stadium Manager (CSSM) will be responsible for the coordination of all non-live filming operations at an assigned FIFA World Cup 2026™ stadium. This position plays a key role in ensuring that the myriad film and TV crews and FIFA photographers present at every match, along with the ever-growing number of digital content creators are serviced to the highest professional standards. Also fundamental to the role is the communication of this activity to the relevant functional areas in the assigned stadium.

Key Responsibilities
Project Definition – Pre-Tournament 
  • Undertake a multi-day online training module, at the end of which all CSSM must be fully confident of the requirements of this role (dates TBC, expected April 2026) 
Planning and Coordination – Pre-Match 
  • Along with the core FIFA Content Services team at the IBC, look ahead to each FIFA World Cup™ match taking place in the relevant stadium 
  • Collaborate closely with the FIFA Content Services core team, competition management, stadium management, and the Host Broadcaster to ensure all filming requests are viable and clearly communicated to all relevant areas  
  • Act as a primary point of contact between FIFA Content Services, competitions management, the stadium management team and non-live filming crews and FIFA photographers, facilitating smooth processes in the planning phase of filming 
Operational Management – Matchday 
  • Maintain focus on the daily non-live content run-sheet, created in collaboration with the core FIFA Content Services team at the IBC  
  • Be the primary point of contact for, and coordinate a pre-match briefing with all non-live content creators. These can range from high-end film crews, to photographers, through to individual digital content creators filming on mobile phones 
  • Where necessary, escort crews to the correct filming positions. Ensure all relevant operational staff in these areas are aware of each crew’s filming and photography plans  
  • Ensure all content creators comply with the previously communicated filming instructions, pre, during and post-match 
  • Ensure any filming and photography in competition areas during half-time and post match periods has been clearly communicated to all relevant stadium staff in particular the Match Director, FIFA Competition Management, FIFA Security and FIFA Media Operations. Should filming in these areas be required, the CSSM is required to maintain close control of each relevant crew  
  • In the event that content needs to be transferred post-match to the IBC, the CSSM will coordinate this with the relevant crew and the core Content Services team at the IBC 
  • Once all filming operations are complete, the CSSM must ensure that all crews and content creators leave the stadium as promptly as possible 
Reporting and Evaluation 
  • Provide a short post-match report containing details of significant events – good and bad 
  • Provide further updates in short, scheduled calls with the core Content Services Team to help drive operational effectiveness 
  • Conduct a longer post-event assessment, evaluating non-live operations, identifying opportunities for successes and process improvement
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar level of higher education a benefit but a proven track record in similar roles is the most important factor 
Work Experience
  • Minimum 5 years of experience and success in a highly pressured organisational role, preferably in a broadcast environment  
  • Strong interpersonal skills to effectively liaise with diverse stakeholders 
  • Proven track record of performing under pressure in a dynamic, multicultural and time-critical environment 
  • Experience in major sports events, FIFA World Cup or other FIFA events will be an asset 
  • Familiarity and working experience with the stadium and city assigned are a plus
Language
  • Candidates must be fluent in English. Other language skills, particularly Spanish, highly valued 
Technology
  • Must be proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint). Familiarity with other planning software, particularly Monday.com will be a major plus point
data.title: Content Services Stadium Manager – FIFA World Cup 26™ | All Host Cities

data.key_responsibilities:
The Depot Manager is responsible for the day-to-day management and readiness of a FIFA fleet/bus depot, ensuring safe, efficient, and professional operations supporting tournament transport services. This role oversees vehicle staging, dispatch and return processes, driver management, site compliance, facilities coordination, and stakeholder communication, acting as the operational leader within the depot environment.
Operational Leadership 
  • Lead day-to-day depot operations, ensuring safe, on-time fleet deployment 
  • Oversee dispatch & return processes for all bus and vehicle categories 
  • Manage depot setup, readiness, and daily operational planning 
  • Coordinate closely with Transport Command Center and Bus Operations teams 
People & Workforce Coordination 
  • Supervise depot staff, supervisors, dispatchers, and contractors 
  • Conduct briefings, assign duties, monitor performance, and support welfare 
  • Ensure compliance with FIFA Code of Conduct and workforce standards 
Vehicle & Driver Management 
  • Oversee vehicle positioning, fueling, cleanliness, and maintenance requests 
  • Validate driver credentials, technology setup (tablets, apps)
  • Manage incident reporting and fleet issue escalation processes
Safety & Compliance 
  • Enforce site safety, vehicle movement control, RFID/VAPP protocols 
  • Conduct risk assessments and ensure industry-standard HSE practices 
  • Support emergency response and incident management procedures 
Facilities & Logistics 
  • Coordinate depot utilities, cleaning, waste services, and security requirements 
  • Liaise with vendors, landlords, and overlay teams for site upkeep 
  • Ensure signage, equipment, and site infrastructure meet FIFA standards 
Reporting & Communication 
  • Maintain accurate operational logs, KPI reports, and shift notes 
  • Provide real-time updates to Bus Operations Center (BOC) and Fleet Operations Center (FCC) 
  • Participate in daily operational briefings and debriefs
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Transport Operations, Logistics, or Hospitality, or equivalent experience. 
  • Knowledge of transport operations, guest services, or event logistics is an asset. 
Work Experience
  • 3-5 years of experience in transport, hospitality, or event operations. 
  • Experience working in a supervisory role within large-scale or high-profile events preferred. 
  • Proven ability to manage small teams, coordinate logistics, and deliver service excellence under pressure. 
  • Experience collaborating with security teams, hotels, and transport providers is advantageous. 
  • Strong interpersonal skills, proactive communication, and confidence in decision-making. 
  • Demonstrates the highest levels of integrity, professionalism, and commitment.  
  • Positive attitude, patience, and persistence in problem-solving.  
  • Strong cultural awareness and the ability to work in an international environment. 
  • Client facing communication and guest services experience is a plus. 
Language
  • Fluent in English (required)
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
  • Additional language skills beneficial due to international broadcast teams.
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Fleet Depot Operations Manager – FIFA World Cup 26™ | All Host Cities
