data.key_responsibilities:
The Junior Graphic designer at FWWC27 LTDA, which is FIFA’s  Brazilian subsidiary responsible for the delivery of FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027 Brazil (TM), will play a pivotal role in shaping the expression of the FIFA brand across global platforms and live event environments. 

You will support the creative development of Brand assets, ensuring consistent, world-class creative output aligned with FIFA’s strategic brand objectives. 

This role is critical to the success of the first ever FIFA Women’s World Cup to be held in South America with a vision to unite the world through an unprecedented experience that celebrates and elevates women’s football.      

Purpose of the Role: This role combines world class graphic design excellence, strong project management skills, and a deep understanding of global audiences, especially in the realm of sports and entertainment. This role reports to the Global head of Brand, marketing & promotions and acts as an extension to the Brand team based in Zurich with dual reporting lines.  Experience in Graphic Design in brand development context is mandatory

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Graphic Design & Brand Expression
  • Supports creative process from concept to execution across channels ensuring high-quality outcomes aligned with FIFA’s brand vision.
  • Designs & produce creative content across brand graphics, event branding, venue dressing, broadcast environments, digital experiences, motion design, and video content.
  • Develop key visuals, brand toolkits, motion graphics packages, environmental branding concepts, and digital-first creative assets. 
  • Ensure strict adherence to FIFA’s visual identity standards, while pushing creative boundaries within those frameworks.
Creative Expertise
  • Graphic Design experience in the context of Brand creation and brand assets and collaterals.
  • Deep visual sensibility and an understanding of brand identity systems across static, dynamic, and environmental media.
  • High-level conceptual thinker with a passion for visual storytelling, motion language, and digital engagement.
  • Hands-on graphic skills, capable of leading from the front while mentoring junior creatives.
Executional Skills
  • Skilled at balancing creative ambition with operational precision and production feasibility.
  • Experienced in managing multiple concurrent projects under tight timelines, with consistent attention to detail and creative quality.
  • Familiar with rights management, usage permissions, and protection of brand and design IP.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Qualifications & Experience
  •  Degree or diploma in Graphic Design, Visual Communication, or a related creative discipline.
  • Proven HANDS -ON expertise in Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, After Effects, Premiere Pro).
  • Experience working with large-scale brand projects, live events, from concepting event look & feel to developing venue branding systems.
  • Proficient in Word / PowerPoint /  Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign, After Effects, Premiere Pro
  • Minimum 3-5 years of experience in graphic design roles within agency, entertainment, FMCG, or sports-related industries.
Requirements
  • Fluent in Portuguese and English
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Client Services Workforce Manager, the Transport Hotline Supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, and work in close collaboration with the Client Services Team, Guest Operations Team, Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering Transport Operations. 

The Transport Hotline Supervisor serves as the first point of contact for all transport-related inquiries received through the Transport Hotline during tournament operations. This role ensures the prompt, accurate, and professional handling of requests from workforce members, client group representatives, and guests — including airport transfers, shuttle schedules, Uber inquiries, and pool-car bookings. 

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Supervisor, Transport Hotline for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Answer all incoming hotline calls professionally and efficiently. 
  • Provide accurate information on airport transfers, shuttle schedules, workforce routes, hotel/stadium transfers, and Uber usage. 
  • Log all calls and actions in the system to ensure traceability. 
  • Maintain calm, clear communication even during peak call periods or service disruptions. 
  • Access, verify, and edit booking data in the Transport Management Module (TMM) in real time. 
  • Liaise with the Fleet Team to confirm vehicle allocations and booking changes. 
  • Escalate complex cases to the TROC Duty Manager or relevant workstreams (Fleet, Bus, Venue, CSMs) when needed. 
  • Remain fully briefed on the Workforce Transport DSLA, service frequencies, and hotel-venue shuttle policies. 
  • Provide guidance to workforce members on shuttle pick-ups, routes, and eligibility. 
  • Explain Uber policy and eligibility for approved client groups; direct out-of-scope requests to management. 
  • Stay familiar with official hotels, stadiums, non-competition sites, and key transport routes to assist inquiries accurately.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Transport Operations, Customer Service, Communications, Logistics, or Hospitality, or equivalent experience. 
Work Experience 
  • For the FIFA26 Pitch-In Program the Transport Client Services Team is open to partnering with diverse candidates across FIFA26 regardless of Education and Work Experience.  Experience in Client services or event transport is an asset. 
  • 3 years of experience in transport, hospitality, or event operations. 
  • Experience working in a client-facing role  
  • Proven ability to coordinate logistics and deliver service excellence under pressure. 
  • Strong interpersonal skills, proactive communication, and confidence in decision-making. 
  • Demonstrates the highest levels of integrity, professionalism, and commitment.  
  • Positive attitude, patience, and persistence in problem-solving.  
  • Strong cultural awareness and the ability to work in an international environment. 
  • Client facing communication and guest services experience is a plus.   
  • Familiarity with call center operations, helpdesk systems, or live event coordination. 
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Transport Hotline Coordinator, FIFA World Cup 26™ | Miami

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Airport manager, the Deputy Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Operational Services team and will work in close collaboration with the FIFA Transport team and other key individuals responsible for supporting the Airport Manager in overseeing all day-to-day airport operations, ensuring safety, efficiency, and compliance with aviation regulations and FIFA client service levels. This role acts as a second-in-command, providing leadership across multiple functional areas, including airside and landside operations, terminal management, customer service, and emergency response coordination.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Deputy Airport Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:  
  • Support and deputize for the Arrivals & Departures Teams Manager, ensuring continuity of leadership and operational decision-making.
  • Lead, train, and motivate a diverse team of paid staff and volunteers to deliver exceptional airport services in line with FIFA World Cup 2026 standards.
  • Oversee daily and shift briefings to align team performance, ensure service consistency, and address operational challenges during extended hours.
  • Manage all operational aspects of arrivals and departures, ensuring alignment with governance structures and coordination with airport stakeholders.
  • Develop and maintain effective relationships with airlines, airport authorities, transport providers, and FIFA functional areas to facilitate seamless client movements.
  • Direct service planning and delivery for all FIFA constituent groups, ensuring compliance with defined service levels (DSLAs) and tournament objectives.
  • Coordinate with transport providers across multiple modes—including bus, fleet, and public transport—to ensure efficient passenger flow and timely connections.
  • Oversee the integration of arrivals and departures operations with other FWC2026 functional areas at venue and site level, including stadiums and non-competition locations.
  • Lead preparation and delivery of client-facing materials, including transport guides, operational updates, and presentations for key stakeholder meetings.
  • Ensure readiness and excellence in service delivery for all pre-tournament test events and operational simulations.
  • Foster a proactive, collaborative, and safety-focused team culture capable of performing under pressure and meeting tight deadlines.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Project Management, Event Management, Major Sports Events, or a related field; an equivalent combination of education and relevant professional experience may be considered.
  • A master’s degree in a related discipline is considered an asset.
  • Proven experience in large-scale event operations, airport management, or logistics coordination within a complex stakeholder environment preferred.
  • Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills with the ability to perform under pressure and meet strict deadlines.
Work Experience
  • Minimum of 3 years of airport transport operations experience, ideally within major sports, football, or event environments.
  • Proven track record in international mega-event operations, preferably across multiple world-class tournaments (e.g., FIFA World Cup, Olympic Games, or regional championships).
  • Demonstrated success working in multi-functional, fast-paced event organizations, managing complex logistics and tight timelines.
  • Experienced in team leadership, project delivery, and coordinating large-scale operations.
  • Skilled in building and maintaining partnerships with public and private sector stakeholders in collaborative environments.
  • Strong understanding of airport transport operations, service flow, and event-specific service levels.
  • Proficient in administration, planning, and project management principles.
  • Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and presentation abilities.
  • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple priorities under pressure.
  • Strong interpersonal and facilitation skills, with the ability to motivate diverse teams toward common goals.
  • Adaptive thinker able to balance client needs, authority requirements, and FIFA standards.
  • Comfortable working both independently and collaboratively within corporate and project-based structures.
  • Demonstrates flexibility, resilience, and professionalism in dynamic or changing environments.
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Airport Supervisor, FIFA World Cup 26™ | Felipe NLU (AIFA)

data.description: Reporting organizationally to the Head of Planning & Administration for Safety & Security, the Specialist, Business Operations - Finance will be a key member of the FIFA26 Safety and Security (SEC) Planning & Administration team. This Specialist will work in close collaboration with the many functional areas across the FIFA™ enterprise to include key individuals responsible for delivering on Safety and Security programs across all venues (and respective sites) for the FIFA World Cup 2026™ and its test events. In addition, this role will support the execution of overall planning and management of security vendor services to be provided across all venues (and respective sites) for the FIFA World Cup 2026 ™ and its test events. This role will implement and execute the finance and procurement-related business operations for the FIFA World Cup Safety and Security Department.
data.key_responsibilities:
The main responsibilities and oversight responsibilities of the Specialist, Business Operations - Finance for the FIFA World Cup 26™ SEC include:  

• Serve as the FIFA26™ Inc. liaison with vendor partners and service providers who will support FIFA and public safety partners with overall World Cup security planning and coordination. Own and manage the procurement and finance workflow for all SEC business lines.
• Create and manage security contracts with FIFA26™ external vendors and service providers, including site owners.
• Serve as first review for compliance with contractual obligations and standards, regularly reviewing agreements for accuracy and relevance, as per the tournament strategy and concept.
• Manage and maintain the relationships with key partners in the procurement and finance cycles with legal, finance, invoicing, and procurement teams.
• Create SEC Planning and Administration dashboards and processes for scaling tracking of procurement and finance lifecycles. 
• Assist with the financial organization of security-related events and workshops to engage FWC 2026™ stakeholders and disseminate key security information. 
• Provide solutions to and implement an audit strategy to assess the effectiveness and compliance of SEC Planning and admin financial cycle obligations. 
• Coordinate payment processing related to security contracts, ensuring accurate and timely payments to vendors and service providers.
• Create solutions to assist with the management and oversight of financial and budgetary matters related to the FIFA26™ Safety and Security Department.
• Prepare regular reports on the status of safety and security finance and procurement status and create recommendations for Exec. Director of Safety and Security and the Chief Safety and Security Officer visibility.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
• Bachelor’s Degree, or equivalent relevant experience, in accounting, finance, business administration or management, sports management or related fields.
• At least 2 years of demonstrated proficiency in procurement lifecycle, invoice lifecycle, and budget planning and execution lifecycle management.
• At least one year of experience in vendor and contract management. International contracts or sports related contracts experience is a plus. 
• Demonstrated ability to work effectively with stakeholders at all levels.
• Knowledge of sport event safety and security planning a plus.
• A candidate will thrive if they enjoy working in a fast-paced start up culture, is not easily frustrated with not knowing the path forward and is excited to think of and execute creative solutions that work for the needs of the team and the tournament. 
• Sees constraints as creative problems to solve. 
• The successful candidate will support teammates and contribute to team goals, enjoy creating solutions under imperfect circumstances, enjoy building from the ground up something on a scale that has never been achieved, and influence legacy FIFA sport security planning.
• Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
• Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools.
• Proficiency or experience with Monday.com, Yokoy, Miro, and SAP a plus.
data.title: Specialist, Business Operations - Finance, FIFA World Cup 2026

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Senior Ticketing Operations Manager the Ticketing Stadium Configuration Manager will help building and maintaining stadium seating maps for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. Working in close collaboration with the Configuration and Inventory team in Zurich and other key individuals responsible for the management of ticketing inventory, seating plans and its maintenance in the Ticketing System, ensuring data accuracy throughout the planning phase and during the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Serve as the central liaison to FIFA Zurich, facilitating alignment on strategic priorities, reporting, and operational updates
  • Provide hands-on support and guidance to the local team, ensuring they have the resources, information, and leadership needed to deliver high‑quality operational outcomes
  • Support and build accurate seating plans in the ticketing system for FIFA tournament venues of the host country
  • Manage and oversee the implementation of all necessary seating information and configuration of spectator flows, price categories, seat allocations and carry out required updates
  • Maintain on day-to-day basis seating maps and configurations within the ticketing system throughout all stages of FIFA tournaments and apply changes as required
  • Ensure data integrity and accuracy of all seating information and configuration
  • Evaluate outcomes of quality inspections and proposes corrective measures
  • Carry out the seat allocation and seat assignment process in the ticketing system
  • Assess and analyse maps and plans of FIFA tournament venues to identify gaps and changes to be applied in the seating plans and configurations
  • Plan, prepare and carry out onsite seat inspection visits to fully validate seating plans as built in the ticketing system with the actual venue seating
  • Support the overall management of ticketing inventory by conducting quality assurance measure for both ticket quotas and seat allocations
  • Administer allocations and additional ticket requests during the tournaments
  • Support general ticketing operations for FIFA events
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education / Experience
  • Bachelor degree or similar level of education 
  • Experience working with ticketing systems (preferably Secutix)
  • 5 years of professional experience  
  • 1-3 years of experience in ticketing or similar functional area (first experiences in creating seating maps or floorplans in a software application of advantage)
  • Experience in data analytics and/ or management 
  • Onsite ticketing operations experience 
  • Experience in working with CAD technical drawings
  • Native‑level fluency in Brazilian Portuguese and English; proficiency in additional languages is valued.

Skills & Competencies
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Sharepoint, Teams, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
  • Ability to work independently, managing multiple priorities
  • Ability to coordinate across functional areas such as security, broadcasting, hospitality
  • Strong analytical skills, detail-oriented, technology minded, efficient and accountable team player 
  • Ability to adapt to working with different environment and time zones

Certifications & Requirements
  • Willingness to travel to host cities.
  • Availability to work extended hours during test events and tournament time.
  • Experience working in international, multicultural environments preferred.
  • Fluent in Portuguese and English
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Senior Ticketing Operations Manager the Ticketing Electronic Access Control Manager oversees the Ticketing Electronic Access Control project for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. Working in close collaboration with the Ticketing Operations team in Zurich as well as other key individuals responsible for the delivery of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™. 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Manage the Ticketing Electronic Access Control (TEAC) project for ticket holders in close collaboration with all relevant stakeholders; 
  • Coordinate the definition of access control specification and the implementation of interfaces between the central ticketing system(s) and the access control solutions used at the competition venues; 
  • Manage the TEAC technical requirements, configuration and ticketing system related configurations required for EAC system integration;
  • Development of integration plan and system requirements;
  • Issue access control documentations and organize workshops, tests and inspections with all relevant parties; 
  • Support the definition of flow data for ticket holders and define access rules based on available variables and according to operational concept and established flow data;  
  • Monitor the access control interfaces and facilitate issue resolution with all relevant parties; 
  • Setup the ticketing system to support access control testing (integration, stadium and match day); 
  • Support the mobile ticketing project and provide required support for configuration, deployment, testing and maintenance;  
  • Prepare training materials and deliver trainings in preparation of match day operations; 
  • Oversee and coordinate the operations of several access control venue staff during the tournament; 
  • Monitor the access control interfaces  and data exchange, as well as facilitate issue resolution with relevant parties;  
  • Reporting on data exchange and Match Day operation;
  • Support general ticketing operations for FIFA events
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education / Experience
  • Bachelor degree or similar level of education 
  • Strong analytical skills, detail-oriented, technology minded, efficient and accountable team player 
  • Ability to adapt to working with different environment and time zones
  • Experience in Electronic Access Control, Facial Recognition, IT system, Integrations or Data Exchange projects 
  • 3-5 year in ticketing project for international sporting events with experience in managing Electronic Access Control systems
  • Native‑level fluency in Brazilian Portuguese and English; proficiency in additional languages is valued.

Skills & Competencies
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Sharepoint, Teams, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools 
  • Strong IT proficiency, especially with ticketing systems including understanding of networks and databases
  • Ability to work independently, managing multiple priorities 
  • Ability to coordinate across internal functional areas as well as external stakeholders
  • Strong analytical skills, detail-oriented, technology minded, efficient and accountable team player  
  • Ability to adapt to working with different environment and time zones 

Certifications & Requirements
  • Willingness to travel to host cities. 
  • Availability to work extended hours during test events and tournament time. 
  • Experience working in international, multicultural environments preferred. 
  • Fluent in Portuguese and English
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

data.key_responsibilities:
The Legal Assistant will be under the guidance of FWWC 2027 the Head of Legal FWWC 2027 and will provide general legal support for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ legal team in Brazil. 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


  • Prepare, review and compile PowerPoint presentations and internal documents.
  • Maintain and update legal trackers and databases, including regular updates on Wetrack tool.
  • Provide translation and proof reading of legal and operational documents.
  • Assist with document execution processes, including verification of powers and coordination of signatures. 
  • Support the preparation of egal documents and templates.
  • Assist the legal team in monitoring deadlines and follow-ups related to contracts and legal matters.
  • Support internal and external audits by organizing and compiling requested documentation.
  • Assist in monitoring relevant legislative changes.
  • Demonstrate a team-based approach to achieving the best outcomes for FWWC 2027 in accordance with FIFA’s values.
  • Support interactions with internal and external stakeholders.
  • Ensure that assigned tasks are delivered with a high level of accuracy, timeliness and attention to detail.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Qualifications:
  • Bachelor of Laws
  • Qualified to practice law in Brazil 
  • Fluency in English

Experience:
  • 1 - 2 years of experience in a law firm or legal department, including experience gained through internships or trainee positions.
  • Prior experience in a reputable law firm is considered an advantage.
  • Excellent writing skills.
  • Strong analytical and organizational skills.
Other knowledge and skills:
  • Proficiency in MS Office applications (including Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, MS Teams).
  • High attention to detail, accuracy, and follow-through.
  • Skilled in problem solving, solution-oriented and pragmatic mindset.
  • Team player with the ability to build effective relationships internally and externally.
  • Empathetic, collaborative and patient, with a positive and proactive attitude.
  • Ability to communicate effectively.
  • Experience with sporting or events organisations or commercial rights holders/sponsorship is considered an advantage.
  • Strong identification with the FIFA World Cup and its potential positive impact on the Host Country.
  • Passion for football is a plus.
Requiriments:
  • Right to work in Brazil.
  • Fluency in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluency in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to relocate to Rio de Janeiro.
data.title: Legal Assistant

Reporting organizationally to the Transport Side Events Manager, the Transport Side Events Supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, and work in close collaboration with FIFA26 Events and Entertainment Team, Transport Team, Guest Operations Team, Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering Transport Operations. 

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Transport Supervisor for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:  
  • Oversee transport execution at side event sites—manage load zones, parking, driver coordination, and shuttle staging to ensure smooth flow.
  • Act as the transport primary point of contact on the ground side event in that city during tournament time, ensuring quick response and operational readiness.
  • Develop and implement short-notice transport plans for unplanned or special events, coordinating routes, staging areas, and vehicle allocations.
  • Liaise with Security, Guest Operations, Venue Transport, Fleet, and City Transport Control Centres to align logistics and access needs.
  • Communicate directly with event leads, VIP guest managers, and functional areas to confirm requirements and translate them into operational plans.
  • Maintain clear event logs, route maps, and incident reports for each side event to support debriefs and continuous improvement.
  • Coordinate with Fleet Operations and Dispatch to secure vehicles, drivers, and escorts for special events.
  • Support access control measures and temporary road closures in collaboration with local authorities when required.

data.key_responsibilities:
The PMA Cluster Supervisor be dedicated to the planning of the teams’ transport and, arrivals and departures services and will be responsible for the delivery of the related tournament specific Service Level Agreements (SLAs)

Reporting organisationally to the Manager, Transport Client Services- Teams, the  PMA cluster supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, and work in close collaboration with the Bus, Fleet, Arrivals and Departures (A&D), and Venue Transportation Operations to plan the transport and A&D services required before and during the tournaments to ensure that all teams receive the correct services in accordance with FIFA’s service standards to ensure a smooth participation at the FCWC and FWC tournaments.  This includes on match days and non- match days at all official sites including stadiums, team hotels, training sites, the airport and other official sites.

They will be responsible to ensure all team related transport information and other related documents, communications, presentations and electronic (web based) information are delivered.

The  Manager, Transport Client Services - Teams will act as Event Transport’s central point of contact for the external clients (teams) and internal clients (FIFA/FWC26 functional areas) as it relates to the identification and confirmation of the clients’ required transport services.
The main responsibilities and oversights of the  PMA cluster supervisor FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Coordination with FWC26’s Transport’s Bus, Fleet and Venue Transport and other key work streams in developing the transport service levels for the teams (and other client groups as required in the planning phase)  in accordance with FIFA SLAs. 
  • Coordination with FWC26’s A&D team in developing and implementing the A&D service levels for the teams and other client groups as required in accordance with FIFA SLA’s. 
  • Coordination with the FWC26/FIFA Functional Area (FA) client owners to ensure the proper and complete planning of the client service levels. Always being pro-active and helpful to assist the client owner to resolve any issues and find operational solutions. 
  • Coordination with the transport group Lead - Client Services in managing budgets associated with planning the client transport and A&D service levels. 
  • Development, writing and finalisation of the draft and final versions of the Principle Service Level Agreements (PSLAs) and the Detailed Service Level Agreements (DSLA’s) in accordance with strict project timelines, gaining the necessary sign off and approvals from all involved stakeholders. 
  • Ensure the integration of all teams transport and A&D services with all FWC26/FIFA FAs including transport work streams to ensure that services are implemented and delivered at all sites and locations as per agreed SLAs. 
  • Responsibilities related to the development of all teams information and communications for the pre-tournament team events including the team workshop, draw & team seminar and team inspection visits and any other key presentations/meetings related to the tournament or test events. 
  • Planning and delivery of any transport services required for the draw/team seminar, team workshop and other related teams events/meetings. 
  • Development of all policies and procedures, liaising with other involved functional areas and gaining final agreement and approvals relative to teams transport and A&D services. 
  • Work with the transport group Lead - Client Services to identify key risks related to teams/clients’ transport and A&D services and develop mitigation strategies. 
  • At tournament time, acting as the single point of contact for Team Services for any issues relating to teams transport and A&D services and ensuring that problems are fixed in an effective, timely and professional manner, and escalation is carried out where necessary. 
  • Assist with the planning and operational delivery of FIFA client transport and A&D services for all test events prior to the FWC 2026. 
  • Assist with other FIFA client’s (Referees, Media/Broadcast, Guests, Workforce) transport service planning as required during the planning phase including the development of the PSLAs and DSLAs as required. 
  • Manage other teams transport coordinators and staff as required. 
  • Support the transport group Lead - Client Services with other client services related tasks as required.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Education – Bachelor’s Degree in Transport Planning, Sports Management, Hotel Management, Project Management, Event Management, Major Sport events or a combination of education and experience relating to position. 

Work Experience
  • Minimum of two (2) years transport work experience: preferably within the major football or sport event; ideally more than one world class sport event (e.g. FIFA World Cup and / or Olympic Games, regional football tournaments, etc.). 
  • Experience of working with Teams/PMAs, or similar client groups, with a good understanding of their needs and requirements and the criticality of ensuring the transport services operate seamlessly.
  • Experience working in multi-functional event organisations and in dynamic and fast paced environments.  
  • Experience of event transport planning including bus operations.  
  • Experience leading a team and proven project delivery experience. 
  • Experience and knowledge of Workforce transport requirements is an advantage. 
Skills
  • Strong interpersonal skills essential. An ability to work effectively as part of an existing team and communicate effectively with both internal and external parties. 
  • Ability to operate in a dynamic working environment, handling multiple tasks and deadlines and adapting to the developing needs and demands of the organisation. 
  • Strong ability to build working relationships with other departments with robust communication, negotiation and stakeholder management skills. 
  • Excellent project management skills with an ability to prioritise tasks and co-ordinate activities. 
  • Detail orientated and solution focused. 
  • Excellent written English skills with relevant skills to produce quality, high-standard documents. 
  • Good knowledge of administration and management principles. 
  • Ability to prioritise client’s needs, authority requirements and FIFA regulations. 
  • Highly collaborative and a team player. 
  • Ability to work well under pressure and adapt appropriately in a calm and professional manner. 
  • Flexibility when faced with ambiguity or constant change. 
  • Positive attitude, patience, and persistence. 
  • Inter-cultural competence and sensitivity. 
  • Displays the highest levels of integrity and commitment. 
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
  • Fluent in English.
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a strong advantage given the tri-host nature of the tournament (USA–Canada–Mexico).
  • Additional languages spoken by global client groups are a plus.
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Venue Operations Director, the Venue Operations Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Venue Management team, and work in close collaboration with all functional areas, the stadium team and and other key stakeholders responsible for delivering high quality venue management and stadium operations.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Operations Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include: 
1. Operational & Leadership Support
  • Assist the Venue Operations Director in the preparation and execution of venue operational plans.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for immediate on-the-ground operational issues.
  • Step up as Acting Venue Operations Director in the VOD’s absence, ensuring continuity of decision-making and leadership.
  • Share responsibilities with the VOD by taking ownership of designated operational areas to balance workload and maximize efficiency.
2. Competition & Match-Day Delivery
  • Oversee and coordinate stadium operations on match days, ensuring functional areas (Security, Access Management, Transport, Medical, Media, Hospitality, Broadcast, Catering, Fan Experience and others) are aligned.
  • Manage the Stadium Operations Center (SOC) log on WeTrack, monitoring and recording incidents and operational updates in real time.
  • Support pre-match readiness checks, stadium walk-throughs, and venue readiness reporting.
  • Coordinate team arrivals, officials’ logistics, and V/VIP movements in compliance with competition requirements.
  • Oversee spectator and client group flows from transport hubs and parking areas to seating, ensuring safe and efficient operations.
  • Ensure readiness of the stadium areas (such as Dressing rooms, competition related BOH areas, offices, overlay commodities)
3. Functional Area Coordination
  • Follow up with functional areas on outstanding operational points, ensuring timely resolution.
  • Act as a liaison between functional leads, supporting communication and problem-solving.
  • Monitor ground operations during events and proactively identify risks, providing immediate solutions.
4. Team & Workforce Management
  • Organize and distribute daily run sheets, ensuring clear allocation of responsibilities and timelines.
  • Supervise and support venue operations staff, volunteers, and contractors, ensuring accountability and effective performance.
  • Deliver pre-event training, daily briefings, and debriefings for the venue operations team.
  • Foster a collaborative and positive operational culture aligned with FIFA’s values and standards.
5. Administrative & Communication Support
  • Manage venue operations administrative tasks, ensuring all documentation is accurate and accessible.
  • Prepare meeting agendas, take notes, track action items, and ensure follow-ups are completed.
  • Maintain up-to-date venue contact lists (staff, vendors, stakeholders).
  • Oversee meeting room bookings and provide logistical support for venue meetings.
  • Manage the MS Teams channel for venue operations, ensuring key documents and communications are uploaded and well organized.
6. Readiness & Reporting
  • Support and participate in readiness exercises, simulations, and rehearsals.
  • Compile and submit operational reports to the Venue Operations Director and Headquarters.
  • Document lessons learned and ensure continuous improvement in operational processes.
Dates
April 20, 2026  - July 9, 2026
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s Degree in sports event management, business administration or equivalent.

Work Experience
  • Minimum 3-5 years of experience in event management, preparation and planning, in a venue/stadium environment.
  • Strong interpersonal skills to effectively liaise with diverse stakeholders. 
  • Strong project management and organizational skills with the ability to multitask and prioritize tasks effectively, ensuring smooth operations and timely completion of duties in a dynamic work setting.
  • Demonstrable ability to manage multiple projects concurrently and under pressure.
  • Team player with a strong customer-service focus, comfortable across all levels of communication, ability to remain flexible, and a desire to go the extra mile.
  • Previous experience with radio communication including use of hand held radios in an event environment.
  • Experience in planning of major sports events, FIFA World Cup or other FIFA events will be an asset.
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Venue Operations Manager  – FIFA World Cup 26™ | San Francisco

data.benefits: In accordance with California' s Equal Pay and Opportunity Act, the pay range for this position is $28 per hour. This estimate is based on this position being performed in California, and the range may not apply to similar positions in other locations. In determining actual base pay, FIFA 26 considers candidate education, experience, and other job-related factors allowed by law. An employee’s position within the salary range will be determined by various factors, including relevant education, experience, qualifications, certifications, skills, geographic location, performance metrics, shift, travel requirements, collective bargaining agreements, and business needs.

data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day-only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting to the Head of Catering, the Venue Catering Coordinator will be responsible for overseeing and managing on-site catering operations to ensure seamless food and beverage service delivery for all key Functional Areas (FAs) during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025.  

This role focuses on tournament-time execution, and oversight of catering services, ensuring exceptional dining experiences for Teams, Referees, Youth Programme, Media, Broadcasting, Workforce, Volunteers, FIFA Partners, and Suppliers. 

The role requires close collaboration with stadium management, caterers, infrastructure teams, venue operations directors, accreditation, security, IT, Marketing, and other stakeholders to guarantee efficient and high-quality service execution throughout the tournament. 

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Catering Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Work closely with the Venue Catering Manager to coordinate catering logistics across all functional areas within the venue, ensuring smooth communication with vendors and internal teams. 
  • Help maintain accurate records of meal counts and catering requests to support timely service and effective reporting by assisting check in process. 
  • Participate in on-site service delivery by checking setups, observing guest flow, and promptly communicating any operational issues to the Venue Catering Manager. 
  • Compile and organize daily catering performance data, including meal numbers, feedback logs, and incident reports for submission to the Tournament Operations Center(TOC). 
  • Contribute to post-service reviews by gathering feedback from functional area representatives and summarizing key takeaways for the Venue Catering Manager. 
  • Participate in training sessions or briefings led by the Venue Catering Manager to ensure catering staff are aligned with standards and procedures. 
  • Help compile photo documentation of service setups, signage, and sustainability efforts for reporting and post-event analysis. 
  • Monitor guest flow patterns throughout meal service periods to identify congestion points or service delays, communicating adjustments to the Venue Catering Manager to improve efficiency and guest satisfaction.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.  

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.  

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.  

INITIATIVE: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority / decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.  

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.

Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar higher education in Hospitality Management, Events Management, Culinary Arts, or Food Service Management.  
  • Team Player, capable of working autonomously and meeting tight deadlines. 
  • Solution oriented with a strong attention to detail. 
  • Exceptional communication and organisational skills. 
  • Problem solving and conflict resolution skills. 
  • Positive attitude, patience and persistence. 
  • International, cross-cultural work experiences preferred. 
Work Experience
  • Minimum 3 years of experience of project management processes or a similar role.  
  • Strong understanding of logistics, supplier coordination, and on-site service execution. 
  • Ability to manage multiple zones and lead operational delivery under tight tournament schedules. 
  • Experience building or evaluating innovative solutions. 
  • Knowledge of international football (soccer), Hospitality or Catering in USA Stadiums and FIFA World Cups or Large-Scale sports Events. 
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with an emphasis on clarity, follow-up, and teamwork. 
  •  Competence in data collection, reconciliation, and reporting to support operational decision-making. 
  • Experience contributing to sustainability or waste management programs within food service or events. 
  • Experience supporting operational planning through documentation, scheduling, and coordination of service timelines.
Languages
  • Fluent in English.  Spanish and French are a bonus. 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.title: Catering Coordinator, Match Day Only

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day-only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting to the Head of Catering, the Venue Catering Coordinator will be responsible for overseeing and managing on-site catering operations to ensure seamless food and beverage service delivery for all key Functional Areas (FAs) during the FIFA Club World Cup 2025.  

This role focuses on tournament-time execution, and oversight of catering services, ensuring exceptional dining experiences for Teams, Referees, Youth Programme, Media, Broadcasting, Workforce, Volunteers, FIFA Partners, and Suppliers. 

The role requires close collaboration with stadium management, caterers, infrastructure teams, venue operations directors, accreditation, security, IT, Marketing, and other stakeholders to guarantee efficient and high-quality service execution throughout the tournament. 

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Catering Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Work closely with the Venue Catering Manager to coordinate catering logistics across all functional areas within the venue, ensuring smooth communication with vendors and internal teams. 
  • Help maintain accurate records of meal counts and catering requests to support timely service and effective reporting by assisting check in process. 
  • Participate in on-site service delivery by checking setups, observing guest flow, and promptly communicating any operational issues to the Venue Catering Manager. 
  • Compile and organize daily catering performance data, including meal numbers, feedback logs, and incident reports for submission to the Tournament Operations Center(TOC). 
  • Contribute to post-service reviews by gathering feedback from functional area representatives and summarizing key takeaways for the Venue Catering Manager. 
  • Participate in training sessions or briefings led by the Venue Catering Manager to ensure catering staff are aligned with standards and procedures. 
  • Help compile photo documentation of service setups, signage, and sustainability efforts for reporting and post-event analysis. 
  • Monitor guest flow patterns throughout meal service periods to identify congestion points or service delays, communicating adjustments to the Venue Catering Manager to improve efficiency and guest satisfaction.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.  

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.  

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.  

INITIATIVE: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority / decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.  

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.

Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar higher education in Hospitality Management, Events Management, Culinary Arts, or Food Service Management.  
  • Team Player, capable of working autonomously and meeting tight deadlines. 
  • Solution oriented with a strong attention to detail. 
  • Exceptional communication and organisational skills. 
  • Problem solving and conflict resolution skills. 
  • Positive attitude, patience and persistence. 
  • International, cross-cultural work experiences preferred. 
Work Experience
  • Minimum 3 years of experience of project management processes or a similar role.  
  • Strong understanding of logistics, supplier coordination, and on-site service execution. 
  • Ability to manage multiple zones and lead operational delivery under tight tournament schedules. 
  • Experience building or evaluating innovative solutions. 
  • Knowledge of international football (soccer), Hospitality or Catering in USA Stadiums and FIFA World Cups or Large-Scale sports Events. 
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with an emphasis on clarity, follow-up, and teamwork. 
  •  Competence in data collection, reconciliation, and reporting to support operational decision-making. 
  • Experience contributing to sustainability or waste management programs within food service or events. 
  • Experience supporting operational planning through documentation, scheduling, and coordination of service timelines.
Languages
  • Fluent in English.  Spanish and French are a bonus. 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.title: Catering Coordinator, Match Day Only

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organizationally to the Fan Operations Venue Lead, the Gate Supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Fan Operations team, and work in close collaboration with other key individuals responsible for delivering a seamless fan experience from the Last Mile to the Stadium. 
 
The main responsibilities and oversights of the Gate Supervisor for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Responsible for the match day management of assigned Fan Operations Volunteers including being the first point of contact for questions, coordinating deployments and breaks, ensuring their welfare as well as collecting and providing feedback 
  • Provide regular reports and updates to Fan Operations Coordinator regarding the performance and status of the gate operations and volunteers 
  • Observe any crowd flow issues and raise accordingly. Communicate necessary action to volunteers.  
  • Monitor gate operations to ensure adherence to security and access control procedures and raise concerns accordingly to Fan Operations Venue Lead 
  • Collaborate with security, operations and management teams to improve processes 
  • Ensure compliance with company policies, safety regulations, and legal requirements 
  • Communicate effectively with customers, vendors, and other stakeholders to resolve any issues and maintain positive relationships 
  • Provide clear information and wayfinding to fans entering the stadium and assist with issue resolution
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.  

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.  

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority / decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.  

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.

Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree 
  • Must be available for all matches at the venue 
  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills 
  • Strong organizational and time management skills 
  • Familiarity with security regulations and best practices 
  • Ability to make sound decisions under pressure and handle challenging situations with tact and professionalism 
Work Experience
  • 2+ years of experience in a supervisory role within a gate services or related department 
  • Proven track record of effectively leading and managing teams, experience managing volunteers is an asset  
  • Experience working and collaborating with large teams 
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools. 
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.
data.title: FIFA Fan Operations Gate Supervisor - Match Day Only

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day-only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting organizationally to the FIFA Fan Operations Venue Lead, the Venue Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA26 Fan Operations team, and work in close collaboration with the FIFA Headquarters and other key individuals responsible for delivering a positive fan experience. 
 
The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Serve as a point of contact for the FIFA Fan Operations Venue Lead on all Fan Services Operations  
  • Manage the Gate Supervisors ensuring they are briefed, and have the operational equipment required  
  • Responsible for the deployment, briefing and overall management of the Fan Operations volunteers  
  • Ensure that critical event and venue information is available to fans and staff through multiple channels 
  • Coordinate with Audio Descriptive Commentators 
  • Oversight of the Restricted Item Storages located across the stadium 
  • Work alongside the Accessibility team to ensure all mobility and accessible services are delivered to a high standard 
  • Responsible for the maintenance and deployment of company assets and equipment 
  • Collaborate with other departments to identify and solve any potential issues  
  • Other duties as assigned by Fan Operations Venue Lead

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.

Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree  
  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to make decisions quickly and willing to address potential issues proactively 
  • Ability to use complex communication pathways to quickly access resources 
  • Effective interpersonal skills with the ability to build and maintain professional relationships across diverse teams and partners 
  • Ability to pivot your work in response to dynamic environment with rapidly changing situations 
Work Experience
  • 3+ years of experience in event management and/or operations roles 
  • Experience working and collaborating with large teams 
  • Public speaking experience 
  • Leadership experience, specifically with part time staff and/or volunteers a plus 
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools. 
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.title: FIFA Fan Operations Venue Coordinator - Match Day Only

data.benefits: In accordance with California' s Equal Pay and Opportunity Act, the pay range for this position is $28 per hour. This estimate is based on this position being performed in California, and the range may not apply to similar positions in other locations. In determining actual base pay, FIFA 26 considers candidate education, experience, and other job-related factors allowed by law. An employee’s position within the salary range will be determined by various factors, including relevant education, experience, qualifications, certifications, skills, geographic location, performance metrics, shift, travel requirements, collective bargaining agreements, and business needs.

  • Responsible for the deployment, briefing and overall management of the Fan Operations volunteers  
  • Ensure that critical event and venue information is available to fans and staff through multiple channels 
  • Coordinate with Audio Descriptive Commentators 
  • Oversight of the Restricted Item Storages located across the stadium 
  • Work alongside the Accessibility team to ensure all mobility and accessible services are delivered to a high standard 
  • Responsible for the maintenance and deployment of company assets and equipment 
  • Collaborate with other departments to identify and solve any potential issues  
  • Other duties as assigned by Fan Operations Venue Lead
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.  

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.  

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.  

INITIATIVE: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority / decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.  

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.

Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree  
  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to make decisions quickly and willing to address potential issues proactively 
  • Ability to use complex communication pathways to quickly access resources 
  • Effective interpersonal skills with the ability to build and maintain professional relationships across diverse teams and partners 
  • Ability to pivot your work in response to dynamic environment with rapidly changing situations 
Work Experience
  • 3+ years of experience in event management and/or operations roles 
  • Experience working and collaborating with large teams 
  • Public speaking experience 
  • Leadership experience, specifically with part time staff and/or volunteers a plus 
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology

Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.key_responsibilities:

The main responsibilities and oversights of the VIP Lounge Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Lead the on-site execution of the VIP lounge guest experience, including branding, entertainment, staffing, and decor. 
  • Ensure the delivery of exceptional, world-class hospitality service to all guests. 
  • Monitor hospitality suppliers to ensure quality, budget compliance, and risk management.  
  • Address and escalate queries related to stadium infrastructure and technical aspects of guest areas.  
  • Manage VIK sponsor needs and ensure implementation aligns with agreed plans.  
  • Support world-class training programs focused on delivering exceptional customer service.  
  • Participate in planning meetings, provide updates, and prepare documents to support guest experience initiatives. 
  • Fill in the required reports and escalate necessary issues to the central team 
  • Ensure successful knowledge sharing and post-event debriefing for Guest Operations.

  • Proactively troubleshoot issues on-site and report incidents promptly to the Guest Operations Stadium Manager.

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day–only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting operationally to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager, the Stadium Exterior Security Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing FIFA’s engagement with the Last Mile operations, the Private Security Outer Perimeter security operations, operational FIFA sites outside of the stadium walls, and all screening activities conducted at PSA and VSA locations during Match Day operations. 

The role ensures that Private Security personnel assigned to exterior security at the outer perimeter operate in accordance with approved FIFA policies and procedures, the Venue Safety & Security Plan (VSSP), Dot Maps, and Match Day directives. 

The Stadium Exterior Security Operations Manager directly oversees the Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist, and Crowd Management Specialist and maintains operational control of FIFA’s exterior security architecture from the Last Mile through the Outer Perimeter up to controlled stadium entry points. 

Key Responsibilities and Oversight 

Operational Delivery – Last Mile & Outer Perimeter 
  • Oversee Private Security personnel deployed across: 
  • FIFA infrastructure in the Last Mile (Accreditation Center and Stadium Ticketing Center) 
  • Outer Perimeter access control points 
  • Pedestrian flow control corridors 
  • Vehicle mitigation zones  
  • Positioning of Hostile Vehicle Mitigation (HVM) structures 
  • Security Operations at Parking Lots 
  • Protest monitoring and public gathering areas (where applicable) 
  • Other FIFA infrastructure outside of the stadium walls 
  • Ensure deployment aligns with approved Dot Maps and VSSP zoning. 
  • Maintain operational integrity of the Stadium Outer Perimeter and prevent unauthorized access to controlled zones. 

Oversight of PSA/VSA Operations 
  • Directly manage and supervise the Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist and Crowd Management Specialist. 
  • Ensure screening operations at PSA and VSA locations are fully integrated within the broader exterior security architecture. 
  • Maintain alignment between screening throughput, crowd flow, and perimeter security posture. 
  • Identify operational gaps between Last Mile, Outer Perimeter, and screening points and implement corrective measures. 

Crowd Flow & Perimeter Integrity 
  • Monitor spectator approach routes and density conditions within the Last Mile footprint. 
  • Support mitigation of congestion and bottlenecks prior to entry screening. 
  • Ensure controlled transition from public domain to secure perimeter. 
  • Coordinate tactical adjustments to deployment when required. 

Compliance & Standards 
  • Ensure Private Security personnel enforce perimeter control and screening measures in accordance with FIFA operational directives. 
  • Confirm proper installation and positioning of barriers, fencing, signage, and vehicle mitigation measures. 
  • Monitor adherence to approved security zoning and escalation protocols. 
  • Identify deviations from approved operational planning and implement corrective actions. 

Incident Management & SOC Coordination 
  • Monitor, document, and report exterior security incidents occurring within Last Mile, Outer Perimeter, PSA, and VSA zones. 
  • Immediately escalate significant issues to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager and SOC. 
  • Support coordinated incident response in alignment with FIFA Security directives. 
  • Provide timely and accurate situational updates through established reporting channels. 
  • Participate in post-incident reviews where applicable. 

Stakeholder Liaison 
  • Coordinate operationally with: 
  • Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist 
  • Crowd Management Specialist 
  • Private Security supervisors 
  • Stadium Operations 
  • Perform as a FWC2026 SEC liaison with Law Enforcement agencies 
  • City or Host City representatives responsible for public domain security 
  • Ensure clarity of roles, responsibilities, and escalation pathways across all exterior security layers. 

Reporting & Administration 
  • Maintain accurate operational records relating to exterior deployment, perimeter control, and incident management. 
  • Submit Match Day updates and post-match summaries as required. 
  • Support compliance verification, audits, and operational validation processes.

Education & Qualifications
  • Relevant professional certifications in event operations or safety management are an advantage 
Work Experience
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in security operations, preferably in major events, public domain security, or large-scale crowd environments. 
  • Experience supervising or coordinating Private Security services. 
  • Strong understanding of layered security architecture (Last Mile, Outer Perimeter, Screening). 
  • Experience working in coordination with Law Enforcement and public agencies. 
  • Major event or international tournament experience is an advantage. 
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools 
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

  • Submit Match Day updates and post-match summaries as required. 
  • Support compliance verification, audits, and operational validation processes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.  

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.  

INITIATIVE: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.
