data.title: Specialist, Stadium Crowd Management - Match Day Only

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day–only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting operationally to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager, the Stadium Exterior Security Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing FIFA’s engagement with the Last Mile operations, the Private Security Outer Perimeter security operations, operational FIFA sites outside of the stadium walls, and all screening activities conducted at PSA and VSA locations during Match Day operations. 

The role ensures that Private Security personnel assigned to exterior security at the outer perimeter operate in accordance with approved FIFA policies and procedures, the Venue Safety & Security Plan (VSSP), Dot Maps, and Match Day directives. 

The Stadium Exterior Security Operations Manager directly oversees the Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist, and Crowd Management Specialist and maintains operational control of FIFA’s exterior security architecture from the Last Mile through the Outer Perimeter up to controlled stadium entry points. 

Key Responsibilities and Oversight 

Operational Delivery – Last Mile & Outer Perimeter 
  • Oversee Private Security personnel deployed across: 
  • FIFA infrastructure in the Last Mile (Accreditation Center and Stadium Ticketing Center) 
  • Outer Perimeter access control points 
  • Pedestrian flow control corridors 
  • Vehicle mitigation zones  
  • Positioning of Hostile Vehicle Mitigation (HVM) structures 
  • Security Operations at Parking Lots 
  • Protest monitoring and public gathering areas (where applicable) 
  • Other FIFA infrastructure outside of the stadium walls 
  • Ensure deployment aligns with approved Dot Maps and VSSP zoning. 
  • Maintain operational integrity of the Stadium Outer Perimeter and prevent unauthorized access to controlled zones. 

Oversight of PSA/VSA Operations 
  • Directly manage and supervise the Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist and Crowd Management Specialist. 
  • Ensure screening operations at PSA and VSA locations are fully integrated within the broader exterior security architecture. 
  • Maintain alignment between screening throughput, crowd flow, and perimeter security posture. 
  • Identify operational gaps between Last Mile, Outer Perimeter, and screening points and implement corrective measures. 

Crowd Flow & Perimeter Integrity 
  • Monitor spectator approach routes and density conditions within the Last Mile footprint. 
  • Support mitigation of congestion and bottlenecks prior to entry screening. 
  • Ensure controlled transition from public domain to secure perimeter. 
  • Coordinate tactical adjustments to deployment when required. 

Compliance & Standards 
  • Ensure Private Security personnel enforce perimeter control and screening measures in accordance with FIFA operational directives. 
  • Confirm proper installation and positioning of barriers, fencing, signage, and vehicle mitigation measures. 
  • Monitor adherence to approved security zoning and escalation protocols. 
  • Identify deviations from approved operational planning and implement corrective actions. 

Incident Management & SOC Coordination 
  • Monitor, document, and report exterior security incidents occurring within Last Mile, Outer Perimeter, PSA, and VSA zones. 
  • Immediately escalate significant issues to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager and SOC. 
  • Support coordinated incident response in alignment with FIFA Security directives. 
  • Provide timely and accurate situational updates through established reporting channels. 
  • Participate in post-incident reviews where applicable. 

Stakeholder Liaison 
  • Coordinate operationally with: 
  • Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist 
  • Crowd Management Specialist 
  • Private Security supervisors 
  • Stadium Operations 
  • Perform as a FWC2026 SEC liaison with Law Enforcement agencies 
  • City or Host City representatives responsible for public domain security 
  • Ensure clarity of roles, responsibilities, and escalation pathways across all exterior security layers. 

Reporting & Administration 
  • Maintain accurate operational records relating to exterior deployment, perimeter control, and incident management. 
  • Submit Match Day updates and post-match summaries as required. 
  • Support compliance verification, audits, and operational validation processes.

Education & Qualifications
  • Relevant professional certifications in event operations or safety management are an advantage 
Work Experience
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in security operations, preferably in major events, public domain security, or large-scale crowd environments. 
  • Experience supervising or coordinating Private Security services. 
  • Strong understanding of layered security architecture (Last Mile, Outer Perimeter, Screening). 
  • Experience working in coordination with Law Enforcement and public agencies. 
  • Major event or international tournament experience is an advantage. 
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools 
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.title: Manager, Stadium Exterior Security - Match Day Only

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day–only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting operationally to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager, the Stadium Exterior Security Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing FIFA’s engagement with the Last Mile operations, the Private Security Outer Perimeter security operations, operational FIFA sites outside of the stadium walls, and all screening activities conducted at PSA and VSA locations during Match Day operations. 

The role ensures that Private Security personnel assigned to exterior security at the outer perimeter operate in accordance with approved FIFA policies and procedures, the Venue Safety & Security Plan (VSSP), Dot Maps, and Match Day directives. 

The Stadium Exterior Security Operations Manager directly oversees the Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist, and Crowd Management Specialist and maintains operational control of FIFA’s exterior security architecture from the Last Mile through the Outer Perimeter up to controlled stadium entry points. 

Key Responsibilities and Oversight 

Operational Delivery – Last Mile & Outer Perimeter 
  • Oversee Private Security personnel deployed across: 
  • FIFA infrastructure in the Last Mile (Accreditation Center and Stadium Ticketing Center) 
  • Outer Perimeter access control points 
  • Pedestrian flow control corridors 
  • Vehicle mitigation zones  
  • Positioning of Hostile Vehicle Mitigation (HVM) structures 
  • Security Operations at Parking Lots 
  • Protest monitoring and public gathering areas (where applicable) 
  • Other FIFA infrastructure outside of the stadium walls 
  • Ensure deployment aligns with approved Dot Maps and VSSP zoning. 
  • Maintain operational integrity of the Stadium Outer Perimeter and prevent unauthorized access to controlled zones. 

Oversight of PSA/VSA Operations 
  • Directly manage and supervise the Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist and Crowd Management Specialist. 
  • Ensure screening operations at PSA and VSA locations are fully integrated within the broader exterior security architecture. 
  • Maintain alignment between screening throughput, crowd flow, and perimeter security posture. 
  • Identify operational gaps between Last Mile, Outer Perimeter, and screening points and implement corrective measures. 

Crowd Flow & Perimeter Integrity 
  • Monitor spectator approach routes and density conditions within the Last Mile footprint. 
  • Support mitigation of congestion and bottlenecks prior to entry screening. 
  • Ensure controlled transition from public domain to secure perimeter. 
  • Coordinate tactical adjustments to deployment when required. 

Compliance & Standards 
  • Ensure Private Security personnel enforce perimeter control and screening measures in accordance with FIFA operational directives. 
  • Confirm proper installation and positioning of barriers, fencing, signage, and vehicle mitigation measures. 
  • Monitor adherence to approved security zoning and escalation protocols. 
  • Identify deviations from approved operational planning and implement corrective actions. 

Incident Management & SOC Coordination 
  • Monitor, document, and report exterior security incidents occurring within Last Mile, Outer Perimeter, PSA, and VSA zones. 
  • Immediately escalate significant issues to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager and SOC. 
  • Support coordinated incident response in alignment with FIFA Security directives. 
  • Provide timely and accurate situational updates through established reporting channels. 
  • Participate in post-incident reviews where applicable. 

Stakeholder Liaison 
  • Coordinate operationally with: 
  • Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist 
  • Crowd Management Specialist 
  • Private Security supervisors 
  • Stadium Operations 
  • Perform as a FWC2026 SEC liaison with Law Enforcement agencies 
  • City or Host City representatives responsible for public domain security 
  • Ensure clarity of roles, responsibilities, and escalation pathways across all exterior security layers. 

Reporting & Administration 
  • Maintain accurate operational records relating to exterior deployment, perimeter control, and incident management. 
  • Submit Match Day updates and post-match summaries as required. 
  • Support compliance verification, audits, and operational validation processes.

data.title: Manager, Stadium Exterior Security - Match Day Only

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day–only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting operationally to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager, the Stadium Security Hospitality Specialist is responsible for overseeing the Private Security resources deployed within Hospitality, VIP, and VVIP controlled environments during Match Day stadium operations. 

The role ensures that Private Security personnel assigned to premium access points, lounges, suites, controlled hospitality compounds, and VVIP circulation routes operate in accordance with approved FIFA policies and procedures, the Venue Safety & Security Plan (VSSP), Dot Maps, and Match Day directives. 

The EVS Hospitality supports controlled access, zoning integrity, and discreet operational management in high-profile environments. 

Key Responsibilities and Oversight 

Operational Delivery – Hospitality & Premium Zones 
  • Oversee Private Security personnel deployed across: 
  • Hospitality lounges and suites 
  • VIP and VVIP access control points 
  • Premium seating circulation routes 
  • Controlled hospitality compounds 
  • Dedicated VVIP arrival and holding areas (where applicable) 
  • Ensure deployment aligns with approved Dot Maps and VSSP zoning. 
  • Maintain operational integrity of access control between public and premium-restricted areas. 

Access Control & Zoning Integrity 
  • Confirm only properly accredited or ticketed individuals access controlled hospitality areas. 
  • Ensure Private Security enforces access controls in accordance with approved post orders and credential boards. 
  • Monitor unauthorized access attempts and escalate as required. 
  • Support discreet management of high-profile guests. 

Compliance & Standards 
  • Monitor adherence to FIFA operational directives relating to VIP/VVIP protection and hospitality zoning. 
  • Ensure Private Security personnel maintain professional conduct appropriate to premium environments. 
  • Identify and escalate deviations from approved operational planning. 

Incident Management & SOC Coordination 
  • Monitor, document, and report incidents occurring within Hospitality, VIP, and VVIP areas. 
  • Immediately escalate significant incidents to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager and Stadium Operations Center (SOC). 
  • Support coordinated response in alignment with FIFA Security directives. 
  • Provide timely situational updates through established reporting channels. 
  • Participate in post-incident reviews where applicable. 

Stakeholder Liaison 
  • Coordinate operationally with: 
  • Private Security supervisors 
  • Hospitality Operations 
  • FIFA Guest Operations 
  • Stadium Operations 
  • Perform as a FWC2026 SEC liaison with Law Enforcement when tactical intervention is required 
  • Executive protection teams, as applicable 
  • Ensure clarity of escalation pathways within premium environments. 
  • Maintain discretion and professionalism in sensitive and high-visibility areas. 

Reporting & Administration 
  • Maintain accurate operational records relating to hospitality security deployment and incidents. 
  • Submit Match Day updates and post-match summaries as required. 
  • Support compliance verification and operational validation processes.

  • Minimum 3–5 years of experience in security operations, preferably in major events, hospitality, or VIP environments. 
  • Experience supervising or coordinating Private Security services. 
  • Strong understanding of access control and restricted zoning principles. 
  • Experience operating in high-profile or executive environments. 
  • Major event or international tournament experience is an advantage.  
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

This role is designated as match day–only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting operationally to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager, the Stadium Security Hospitality Specialist is responsible for overseeing the Private Security resources deployed within Hospitality, VIP, and VVIP controlled environments during Match Day stadium operations. 

The role ensures that Private Security personnel assigned to premium access points, lounges, suites, controlled hospitality compounds, and VVIP circulation routes operate in accordance with approved FIFA policies and procedures, the Venue Safety & Security Plan (VSSP), Dot Maps, and Match Day directives. 

The EVS Hospitality supports controlled access, zoning integrity, and discreet operational management in high-profile environments. 

Key Responsibilities and Oversight 

Operational Delivery – Hospitality & Premium Zones 
  • Oversee Private Security personnel deployed across: 
  • Hospitality lounges and suites 
  • VIP and VVIP access control points 
  • Premium seating circulation routes 
  • Controlled hospitality compounds 
  • Dedicated VVIP arrival and holding areas (where applicable) 
  • Ensure deployment aligns with approved Dot Maps and VSSP zoning. 
  • Maintain operational integrity of access control between public and premium-restricted areas. 

Access Control & Zoning Integrity 
  • Confirm only properly accredited or ticketed individuals access controlled hospitality areas. 
  • Ensure Private Security enforces access controls in accordance with approved post orders and credential boards. 
  • Monitor unauthorized access attempts and escalate as required. 
  • Support discreet management of high-profile guests. 

Compliance & Standards 
  • Monitor adherence to FIFA operational directives relating to VIP/VVIP protection and hospitality zoning. 
  • Ensure Private Security personnel maintain professional conduct appropriate to premium environments. 
  • Identify and escalate deviations from approved operational planning. 

Incident Management & SOC Coordination 
  • Monitor, document, and report incidents occurring within Hospitality, VIP, and VVIP areas. 
  • Immediately escalate significant incidents to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager and Stadium Operations Center (SOC). 
  • Support coordinated response in alignment with FIFA Security directives. 
  • Provide timely situational updates through established reporting channels. 
  • Participate in post-incident reviews where applicable. 

Stakeholder Liaison 
  • Coordinate operationally with: 
  • Private Security supervisors 
  • Hospitality Operations 
  • FIFA Guest Operations 
  • Stadium Operations 
  • Perform as a FWC2026 SEC liaison with Law Enforcement when tactical intervention is required 
  • Executive protection teams, as applicable 
  • Ensure clarity of escalation pathways within premium environments. 
  • Maintain discretion and professionalism in sensitive and high-visibility areas. 

Reporting & Administration 
  • Maintain accurate operational records relating to hospitality security deployment and incidents. 
  • Submit Match Day updates and post-match summaries as required. 
  • Support compliance verification and operational validation processes.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.  

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.  

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.  

INITIATIVE: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.  

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority / decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.  

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.

Education & Qualifications
  • Relevant professional certifications in event operations or safety management are an advantage 
Work Experience
  • Minimum 3–5 years of experience in security operations, preferably in major events, hospitality, or VIP environments. 
  • Experience supervising or coordinating Private Security services. 
  • Strong understanding of access control and restricted zoning principles. 
  • Experience operating in high-profile or executive environments. 
  • Major event or international tournament experience is an advantage.  
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools 
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.
data.title: Specialist, Stadium Hospitality Security - Match Day Only

data.description: At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.
data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day–only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting operationally to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager and , the Stadium Security Pitch & Competition Specialist is responsible for overseeing the Private Security resources deployed within Pitch and Competition-related zones during Match Day stadium operations. 

The role ensures that stadium-provided personnel assigned to the Field of Play, pitch perimeter, tunnel access, and competition-controlled areas operate in accordance with approved FIFA policies and procedures, the Venue Safety & Security Plan (VSSP), Dot Planning, and Match Day directives. 

The Stadium Pitch & Competition Specialist supports the protection of the Field of Play and controlled competition environments in coordination with FIFA Security, Competition, and Stadium Operations. 

Key Responsibilities and Oversight 

Operational Delivery – Pitch & Competition Zones 
Oversee EVS personnel deployed across: 
  • Pitch perimeter (including LED line and field protection areas) 
  • Field Intervention Team (FIT) 
  • Training 
  • Communications 
  • Testing 
  • Player tunnel access points 
  • Team bench and technical areas (as applicable) 
  • Competition-restricted circulation routes 
  • Controlled media pitch-side positions 
Ensure deployment aligns with approved Dot Planning and VSSP zoning. 
Maintain operational integrity of access control points into competition-restricted areas. 

Field of Play Protection 
  • Support implementation of anti-pitch incursion measures. 
  • Monitor spectator proximity risks and potential breach points. 
  • Ensure rapid coordination with Private Security and Law Enforcement in the event of attempted or actual pitch incursions. 
  • Support orderly and secure movement of Teams, Match Officials, and Competition personnel. 
  • FWC2026 SEC liaison with public safety and first responder associates during field level response to pyrotechnics deployment and eradication.  

Compliance & Standards 
  • Confirm only properly accredited personnel access controlled competition zones. 
  • Monitor adherence to FIFA operational directives relating to Field of Play protection. 
  • Identify and escalate any deviations from approved operational planning. 

Incident Management & SOC Coordination 
  • Monitor, document, and report incidents occurring within Pitch & Competition zones. 
  • Immediately escalate pitch-related incidents to the EVS Manager and Stadium Operations Center (SOC). 
  • Support incident response coordination in alignment with FIFA Security directives. 
  • Provide timely situational updates through established reporting channels. 
  • Participate in post-incident reviews where applicable. 

Stakeholder Liaison 
  • Coordinate operationally with: 
  • FIFA Competition team 
  • Private Security supervisors 
  • Stadium Operations 
  • Broadcast representatives operating in pitch-side zones 
  • Ensure clarity of escalation pathways for pitch-related incidents. 
  • Maintain professional conduct in high-visibility and broadcast-sensitive environments. 

Reporting & Administration 
  • Maintain accurate operational records relating to pitch-zone staffing and incidents. 
  • Submit Match Day updates and post-match summaries as required. 
  • Support compliance verification and operational validation processes.

  • Familiarity with hotel security operations and transport/logistics environments

  • Ability to work independently, manage multiple sites, and perform under pressure 
  • Major event or international tournament experience is an advantage 
Languages

Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools 
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day–only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting operationally to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager, the Stadium Security PSA/VSA Specialist is responsible for overseeing the Private Security resources deployed to deliver search and screening operations in accordance with FIFA Prohibited Items policy, preventing the introduction of prohibited materials into the stadium perimeter during Match Day operations. 

The role ensures that Private Security personnel assigned to the Pedestrian Screening Areas (PSA), the Vehicle Screening Area (VSA), and controlled compliance zones, operate in accordance with approved FIFA policies and procedures, the Venue Safety & Security Plan (VSSP), Dot Maps, and Match Day directives of the FWC26 Security team. 

The Stadium PSA/VSA Specialist supports consistent implementation of FIFA Prohibited Items policy, Flags & Banners regulations, and Brand Protection measures within the stadium footprint. 

Key Responsibilities and Oversight 

Operational Delivery – PSA & VSA Zones 
Oversee Private Security personnel deployed across: 
  • Pedestrian Screening Areas (PSA) 
  • Vehicle Screening Areas (VSA) 
  • Search lanes and inspection points 
  • Item deposit/storage areas (where applicable) 
  • Adjacent queue management zones 
  • Ensure deployment aligns with approved Dot Planning and VSSP zoning. 
  • Escalate issues/staffing gaps to FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager and Stadium Operations Center (SOC). 
  • Maintain operational integrity of screening lanes, search procedures, and vehicle inspection protocols ensuring only ticketed or accredited people enter the stadium 
  • Escalate PSA system failures as necessary 

Prohibited Items Policy Enforcement 
  • Ensure Private Security personnel consistently enforce FIFA Prohibited Items policy. 
  • Monitor search procedures to confirm proper identification and handling of prohibited materials. 
  • Oversee documentation and storage procedures for retained items (if applicable). 
  • Identify inconsistencies in screening standards and implement corrective measures. 
  • Ensure installation of signage to provide guidance to spectators. 

Flags & Banners Compliance 
  • Monitor inspection and approval procedures for flags and banners entering through PSA locations. 
  • Ensure compliance with approved FIFA Flags & Banners policy. 
  • Identify and escalate non-compliant materials in accordance with established procedures. 
  • Ensure implementation of Flags & Banners check-points, signage installation, distribution of equipment to Private Security Company. 

Brand Protection 
  • Monitor reporting of unauthorized commercial activity or ambush marketing at screening points. 
  • Ensure prohibited promotional materials are identified and confiscated. 
  • Coordinate with Private Security and Law Enforcement when tactical enforcement is required. 
  • Incident Management & SOC Coordination 
  • Monitor, document, and report PSA/VSA-related incidents, disputes, or compliance issues. 
  • Immediately escalate significant issues to the FIFA Stadium Safety & Security Senior Manager and Stadium Operations Center (SOC). 
  • Support coordinated incident response in alignment with FIFA Security directives. 
  • Provide timely and accurate situational updates through established reporting channels. 
  • Participate in post-incident reviews where applicable. 

Stakeholder Liaison 
  • Coordinate operationally with: 
  • Private Security supervisors 
  • Stadium Operations 
  • Guest Services 
  • Perform as a FWC2026 SEC liaison with Law Enforcement when required 
  • Ensure clarity of escalation pathways for screening-related disputes or enforcement actions. 
  • Maintain professional conduct in high-volume and high-pressure entry environments. 

Reporting & Administration 
  • Maintain accurate operational records relating to PSA/VSA deployment, screening performance, and incidents. 
  • Submit Match Day updates and post-match summaries as required. 
  • Support compliance verification and operational validation processes.

data.key_responsibilities:

data.key_responsibilities:
This role is designated as match day-only and includes participation in a select number of required training sessions scheduled ahead of the tournament. Reporting organizationally to the Fan Operations Venue Lead, the Gate Supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Fan Operations team, and work in close collaboration with other key individuals responsible for delivering a seamless fan experience from the Last Mile to the Stadium. 
 
The main responsibilities and oversights of the Gate Supervisor for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Responsible for the match day management of assigned Fan Operations Volunteers including being the first point of contact for questions, coordinating deployments and breaks, ensuring their welfare as well as collecting and providing feedback 
  • Provide regular reports and updates to Fan Operations Coordinator regarding the performance and status of the gate operations and volunteers 
  • Observe any crowd flow issues and raise accordingly. Communicate necessary action to volunteers.  
  • Monitor gate operations to ensure adherence to security and access control procedures and raise concerns accordingly to Fan Operations Venue Lead 
  • Collaborate with security, operations and management teams to improve processes 
  • Ensure compliance with company policies, safety regulations, and legal requirements 
  • Communicate effectively with customers, vendors, and other stakeholders to resolve any issues and maintain positive relationships 
  • Provide clear information and wayfinding to fans entering the stadium and assist with issue resolution

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organizationally to the Accommodation Manager in Rio de Janeiro, the Accommodation Coordinator will help serve Accommodation efforts for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ is hosting 32 teams and 700+ players across Brazil and 8 host cities/ venues. This role will be responsible for coordinating lodging and hotel-related arrangements for the event, ensuring a smooth and comfortable experience for teams, participants and attendees. Further, this role will have a range of responsibilities, including contracting hotels, coordinating inventory, F&B, AV, meetings space, and reconciling and reporting.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Contract hotels ensuring they meet quality standards and core group requirements
  • Ensure hotels comply with all contractual obligations
  • Execute tasks and addendums necessary for adjustments to hotel contracts
  • Coordinate reservations, rooming lists, changes and requests for core groups
  • Provide excellent customer service to official tournament guests
  • Coordinate hotel room blocks, meeting space, F&B, AV, group requirements, hotel communications and reservation management
  • Provide resources and information to guests, create presentation materials
  • Support booking and reservation processes for guests
  • Keep all necessary internal and external reporting up to date
  • Ensure held inventory is in accordance with remaining demand
  • Attend site visits, familiarize with hotels and tour constituent groups
  • Coordinate the reconciliation, billing and closeout at hotels and with groups
  • Support and address unforeseen changes, cancellations, or emergencies in accommodation arrangements
  • Build strong relationships with designated groups to provide excellent experience within the overall accommodation strategy
  • Consider all operational aspects when coordinating hotel arrangements, for example, transportation, logistics, security, etc.
  • Uphold and enforce high standards of customer service, ensuring participants and guests have a memorable and enjoyable experience during their stay
  • Establish relationships with the hotel industry to provide the best overall value for FIFA
  • Ensure records of accommodation bookings, rooming lists, and related information comply with data integrity and protection regulations
  • Coordinate various ancillary projects taking place in the leadup to the Women’s World Cup
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
QUALIFICATIONS
  • Bachelor’s Degree or similar level of education.
  • Minimum of 2 years of work experience in the hospitality industry.
  • Strong communication skills – oral and written.
  • Experience coordinating complex projects.
  • Strong organizational, customer service and attention to detail.
  • Fluent/Native in English and Portuguese are required for this position.
  • Knowledge of other languages is considered a plus.
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools.

Requirements:
  • Must have legal right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to relocate and work from Rio de Janeiro 
  • Required language skills: fluent in English and Portuguese is mandatory, fluent in Spanish is a big plus.

data.key_responsibilities:
The Translation Project Manager of the FIFA27 Language Centre team, and work in close collaboration with FIFA Zurich key collaborators and other key individuals responsible for delivering the most engaging, innovative, multilingual and accessible World Cup experience for all athletes, fans, partners, and the community.


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Managing and supervising the translation services provided by the FWWC27 office to all stakeholders
  • Handling any issues that arise in connection with the translation services provided by the FWWC27 office or in connection with the FWWC27.
  • Planning the language operations for the FWWC27 (pre-tournament and during the competition)
  • Reporting on daily business (e. g. requests processed) to the Head of the Language Centre in Zurich
  • Ensuring a smooth collaboration and efficient processes between FIFA and the FWWC27 office in connection with the translation services provided
  • Identifying and using artificial intelligence (AI) technologies in delivering the language services. Clear focus should be the use of AI to streamline processes and increase productivity
  • Strengthening FIFA’s translation vendor network in the Americas if operations require so
  • Being the first point of contact for translation requests placed by the FWWC27 team and other FIFA stakeholders
  • Handling requests in FIFA’s translation management system
  • Allocating resources
  • Coordinating large multilingual projects comprised of several requests (CMS/CXM, apps, publications), occasionally managing different teams (developers, graphic designers, desktop publishers)
  • Monitoring and controlling translation requests
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
QUALIFICATIONS
  • Education Bachelor’s degree or similar level of education in translation/localization/language technology studies
  • Experience at least five years’ experience as translation/localization project manager working with a state-of-the-art translation management system
  • Team player
  • Ability to set priorities and work under high pressure
  • Openness and interest to learn
  • Experience in the sports and/or entertainment industries is a plus
  • Native speaker of Brazilian Portuguese, full professional competency in English (written and oral), proficiency in French and/or Spanish is a plus
  • At least two years’ experience as a translation/localization project manager working with Trados
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Teams) and experienced in the use of online planning and collaboration tools
  • Experience working in global language teams, with different time zones
Requirements:
  • Must have legal right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to relocate and work from Rio de Janeiro 
  • Required language skills: fluent in English and Portuguese is mandatory, fluent in Spanish is a big plus.

The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. data.key_responsibilities:
This role provides site-level security delivery oversight for Team Base Camp venues that consist of the Hotel and Training Site that a participating national football team will occupy throughout FIFA World Cup 2026TM. Intended to be a home away from home, a Team Base Camp (TBC) refers to a team’s headquarters where they stay and perform necessary functions to compete at the FIFA World Cup 2026TM.

Reporting organizationally to the Safety and Security Senior Manager – Non-Competition Sites, the Team Base Camp Specialist acts as the primary on-site FIFA security representative at Team Base Camps during tournament time.

The role focuses on site readiness, private security supervision, and operational assurance, ensuring that security services are delivered in accordance with approved FIFA standards, post orders, contractual obligations, and operational directives.

Site Readiness & Operational Assurance
• Ensure assigned Team Base Camp sites are operationally ready for security delivery prior to and throughout tournament operations
• Verify that private security personnel are correctly briefed, trained, and deployed in accordance with their assigned duties and post orders
• Confirm that security infrastructure and equipment (access control points, barriers, CCTV, lighting, radios, signage, etc.) are installed (where applicable), functional, and appropriately configured
• Conduct readiness checks and daily operational verifications at assigned Team Base Camp sites
• Validate that access control arrangements and security zoning are implemented as approved
• Liaise with site owners to ensure security measures are in place and that contracted services are delivered
Operational Delivery
• Act as the on-site security focal point for assigned Team Base Camp sites during tournament-time operations, primarily when a Team is in residence
• Oversee daily security operations, including access control, patrols, Team protection measures (where applicable), and response readiness
• Monitor private security performance and address deficiencies in real time
• Coordinate with hotel and training site management, logistics operators, transport teams, and other functional areas to ensure secure operations
• Maintain situational awareness across assigned sites and adjust deployment as operational conditions evolve
Incident Management & SSOC Coordination
• Monitor, document, and report all security incidents, issues, and near misses at assigned Team Base Camp sites
• Initiate and support incident response in coordination with the Safety & Security Operations Center (SSOC) and Safety and FWC26TM Security Senior Manager
• Provide timely and accurate situational updates through established reporting channels
• Support post-incident reviews and corrective actions

Stakeholder Liaison
• Serve as the primary security liaison with:
      o Private security providers
      o FIFA staff and functional area leads
      o Hotel and Training Site management and site operators
      o Local law enforcement and emergency services, as required
• Ensure clarity of roles, responsibilities, and escalation pathways at site level
• Maintain discretion and professionalism in environments involving FIFA staff, Teams, and sensitive operations

Reporting & Administration
• Maintain accurate records of daily operations, incidents, staffing levels, and resource usage
• Submit daily operational reports and ad-hoc updates as required
• Support compliance checks, audits, and performance validation processes
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications:
• Bachelor's degree in a related field associated with safety, security and/or project management is a plus
• All candidates must be able to pass a comprehensive background check
• Must be available for duration of the Team’s residence
• Must be willing to work long hours and be on call 24/7 during the tournament.

Work Experience:
• Minimum 3–5 years of experience in security operations, preferably in major events, hospitality, logistics, or VIP environments
• Experience supervising or coordinating private security services
• Familiarity with hotel security operations and transport/logistics environments
• Strong understanding of access control, incident response, and operational security principles
• Ability to work independently, manage multiple sites, and perform under pressure
• Major event or international tournament experience is an advantage

Languages:
• Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus

Technology:
• Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Specialist, Team Base Camp Security - FIFA World Cup 26™

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Tournament Headquarters, the Stadium Fan Experience Manager will ensure the successful operational implementation and fulfilment of the dedicated stadium fan engagement area. The Stadium Fan Experience (SFE) is comprised of Commercial partner activations and FIFA-led initiatives designed to enhance the overall match day ticket holder experience.

The main responsibilities of the Stadium Fan Experience Manager include:  
  •  Support the SFE operational planning with a strong emphasis on logistical coordination
  •  Serve as the primary on-site point of contact for the production agency, ensuring alignment on timelines, deliverables and issue resolution
  •  Coordinate with key functional areas the seamless installation and operation of all activations
  •  Oversee commercial partner booth load-in and load-out activities, ensuring compliance with venue regulations, approved schedules and health and safety requirements
  •  Manage day-to-day communication with internal stakeholders and external vendors to maintain alignment throughout event build, operations and dismantling phases
  •  Monitor and enforce build schedules, site access protocols and venue rules across all SFE elements and partner activations
  •  Conduct site readiness walkthroughs to confirm all installations meet operational, safety and brand standards prior to event opening
  •  Troubleshoot and resolve on-site issues in real time, coordinating with relevant stakeholders to address technical, operational, or logistical challenges
  •  Maintain comprehensive operational documentation, including run-of-show inputs, contact lists and escalation procedures
  •  Support the onsite Marketing Venue Management team with Commercial Partner activities. 
  •  Complete event evaluations and reporting.
  •  Any other duties that may be required.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar level of education
Work Experience
  •  A minimum experience of 5 years working in sports event management and ideally sponsorship marketing (preferably in soccer).
  •  Proven experience with administrative responsibilities for international events, ideally in the sports industry.
  •  Knowledge of marketing operations in event settings.
  •  Good planning, analytical and conceptual skills.
  •  Service-oriented approach.
  •  Strong commitment to quality, results and accountability to a team.
  •  Resilience to stress and intensive work, persistence and commitment to procedures.
  •  Good IT skills and knowledge of working on sharing platforms.
  •  Ability to adopt a strategic view, take decisions and act promptly.
  •  Great attention to detail and to producing work of a high standard.
  •  Open-mindedness, flexibility, and adaptability.
  • Team Player.
  •  Experience or knowledge of the Host Countries (Canada, Mexico, USA) culture would be an added asset.
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
The Ceremonies Production Integration role is a key position within the FIFA World Cup 26™ Events & Entertainment Team (EET), consolidating the responsibilities of Production Manager, Senior Operations Manager, Senior Show Delivery Manager, Senior Scenic & Systems Manager, Operations Manager, Site Manager,  and Production Support roles across 4th of July ceremonies programs. 

This role supports the full lifecycle of ceremonies – from concept development and technical/production planning, through rehearsals, live show delivery, site and back-of-house operations, and legacy handover. It ensures that all ceremonies-related production, staging, scenic, systems, site operations, and logistics are delivered on time, safely, efficiently, and in alignment with FIFA’s creative vision, operational standards, and safety policies.

The position works in close collaboration with EET internal departments, Event Management Companies (EMCs), stadium management, Logistics, Venue Management, Broadcast, Marketing, IT, Security, Accreditation, Workforce & Volunteers, and other FIFA World Cup 26™ Functional Areas (FAs). The role also plays a central part in managing documentation, project plans, production schedules, call sheets, reports, creative assets, and operational data to support a seamless ceremony delivery.

This is a high-intensity, live-event operations role requiring strong project management, show delivery, technical understanding, stakeholder management, and the ability to adapt quickly to changing circumstances in a stadium environment.

Project & Production Management
  • Support the development, management, and distribution of production, staging, scenic, systems, and ceremonies operational plans from concept to delivery.
  • Assist in the development of ceremonies deliverables, coordinating event details, internal alignment, and approval processes.
  • Work alongside Heads of Operations, Technical, and Resourcing to oversee technical requirements and integration of ceremonies with stadiums and show environments.
  • Develop and implement broadcast, staging, and scenic requirements and processes, in collaboration with EMCs and relevant Functional Areas.
  • Align ceremonies production, staging, and systems plans with venue operating requirements and cross-functional integration (e.g. Stadium Entertainment, INF, Logistics, Venue Operations).
  • Support the creation and maintenance of detailed production schedules and timelines for build, rehearsals, show delivery, dissolution, and load out.
Ceremonies Operations, Site & Show Delivery
  • Manage and/or support ceremonies operations at stadium level, working closely with Stadium Entertainment Producers and Technical/Operations Managers.
  • Set up and manage ceremonies and operational spaces, including field-of-play areas, backstage, compounds, green rooms, dressing rooms, storage, BOH areas, and operational work zones.
  • Assist the Show Operations – Stadium team and Show Delivery team in executing show operations, cast movements, and in-bowl activities according to approved movement plans.
  • Ensure FF&E (what goes where) plans for ceremonies venues are executed correctly and on time.
  • Oversee or support venue-level operations related to accreditation, catering, parking, sanitation, cleaning, barricades, site equipment, tenting, fueling, and site restoration.
  • Assist in organizing and coordinating rehearsals, load-in/load-out, and all logistics for ceremonies and stadium entertainment.

Site Management & Back-of-House Operations
  • Assist with setup, operation, and breakdown of backstage areas including dressing rooms, green rooms, BOH corridors, compounds, and storage areas.
  • Maintain BOH schedules and monitor housekeeping and working standards.
  • Provide on-site support to cast, crew, and staff, addressing needs and concerns promptly.
  • Communicate stadium site operations plans, daily run sheets, schedules, and venue documentation to relevant ceremonies departments.
  • Address venue issues promptly and assist with implementation of contingency plans for stadium operations.
Stakeholder Liaison & Cross-Functional Integration
  • Act as a key liaison with venue operations staff, EET internal departments, EMCs, stadium management, and other FWC26 Functional Areas.
  • Facilitate cross-functional coordination within ceremonies sub-divisions, suppliers, and external stakeholders.
  • Support vendors and suppliers, ensuring clear instructions and alignment with production and operations plans.
  • Represent ceremonies operations/production at venue-level or project-level meetings as required.
Documentation, Reporting & Governance
  • Develop and maintain user-friendly filing and archiving systems for production, technical, scenic, systems, and operational documentation.
  • Prepare or support high-level reporting, such as WIP reports, Executive reports, project updates, dissolution plans, close-out and legacy handover documents.
  • Maintain detailed records of production, staging, scenic, creative, PMC, site, and operational activities.
  • Ensure the current version of scripts, show documents, creative content, and run sheets is accurate, logged, and distributed.
  • Work in full compliance with FWC26 Policies & Procedures and Safety & Security rules and requirements.
Risk Management, Contingency & Safety
  • Identify potential risks across production, operations, site, and show delivery, and contribute to mitigation strategies and contingency plans.
  • Ensure all activities are carried out in line with safety regulations, security protocols, and operational policies.
  • Support the development and implementation of contingency plans for operational or show-related issues and emergencies.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Live Entertainment Production, Theatre Production, Sports Event Management, Technical Production, or a related field preferred.
  • Equivalent professional experience in ceremonies, large-scale live events, or stadium show production will be considered in lieu of a degree.

Work Experience
  • Extensive experience working on large-scale live events, preferably in stadium environments (sporting events, ceremonies, concerts, or similar).
  • Demonstrated experience in event logistics and operations, working cross-functionally across large event delivery teams.
  • Experience with vendors and suppliers, including negotiation, coordination, and performance oversight.
  • Exposure to or experience with production elements such as lighting, sound, video, rigging, staging, scenic, and broadcast integration.
  • Proven ability to manage schedules, calls, meetings, production times, and operational timelines.
  • Experience handling production documentation (call sheets, production reports, permits, scripts, creative logs, etc.) accurately and efficiently.
  • Track record of identifying risks, developing mitigation strategies, and resolving complex operational or production challenges.
  • Experience leading or supporting teams in high-pressure, fast-paced event environments, including managing multiple projects simultaneously.
  • Excellent communication skills for interacting with producers, directors, talent, crew, partners, and stakeholders at all levels.
  • Strong relationship-building skills with internal teams, external partners, and venue stakeholders.
  • High attention to detail in managing complex projects and ensuring accuracy in deliverables.
  • Strong writing and editing skills for creative briefs, reports, presentations, and operational documentation.
  • Ability to prioritize effectively, manage competing deadlines, and remain calm under pressure.
  • Cultural awareness and respect for diverse audiences, ensuring inclusive and respectful content and operations.
  • A genuine enthusiasm for the entertainment industry and a commitment to creating high-quality, compelling ceremonies.
  • Ability to work independently, show initiative, and influence outcomes with minimal supervision.
  • Flexibility and willingness to work irregular hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays, in line with event schedules.

Language
  • Fluent in English (required).
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a strong plus.
  • Additional languages are beneficial given the global nature of the tournament, teams, and ceremonies collaborators.

Technology
  • Familiarity with production and event management tools (e.g., show control systems, scheduling software, cueing tools, or project management platforms).
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams).
  • Ability to work with digital documentation, asset management systems, and reporting tools for production and operations.
  • Comfort using collaboration platforms and shared drives for filing, archiving, and distributing production and operational documentation.
data.title: Ceremonies Production Integration – FIFA World Cup 26™ | Philadelphia

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience. 

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
The Host City Volunteer Manager will be responsible for the delivery of the FIFA Volunteer Programme in-line with the FIFA Volunteer Framework and FIFA’s service excellence standards supported by the local operational concept. This will include full alignment with the Senior Volunteer Manager – FIFA27 and FIFA Zurich on the programme strategy, mission, and vision, safeguarding the FIFA Volunteer brand and its reputation across all the host cities and assigned venues.

Reporting to the Volunteer Management Central Team – FIFA27 and working in close collaboration with the Lead of the project on the city level, the Host City Volunteer Manager will be the face of the FIFA Volunteer Programme for their respective Host City. The role will be responsible for supporting the delivery of the volunteer journey through the FIFA Volunteer Platform and overseeing volunteer operations within their assigned Host City.

The ideal candidate will join a dynamic, multicultural environment and bring both strategic insight and firsthand expertise to one of the most exciting sporting events in the world.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

The following points will apply to all host cities and venues where the competition takes place.
Responsibilities include but are not limited to:

Programme Delivery and Coordination at the Host City level
  • Collaborate with the Volunteer Management Central Team (FIFA27) and FIFA Zurich to support the delivery of the Volunteer Programme and reporting on the project status.
  • Act as the primary point of contact between FIFA27 Volunteer Management Central Team and Host City counterparts on all volunteer related matters, ensuring clear two-way coordination and information flow.
  • Act as the primary point of contact for Host City queries and requests related to volunteer communications and engagement activities and projects, including volunteer-focused events.
  • Establish and maintain strong working relationships with Functional Area managers who engage volunteers or support volunteer management operations.
  • Act as a Point of contact for volunteer related topics with Venue Management when necessary.
  • Work closely with Host City counterparts for pre implementation of promotion and recruitment initiatives within local communities to tournament delivery including but not exclusive to training and uniform distribution
  • Support the promotion initiatives and events of the Volunteer team by:
    - Identifying volunteer profiles for media and/or social media features.
    - Maintaining an archive of photos and videos of Host City volunteers for use at the discretion of the central volunteer management team. Oversee the organization of volunteer-focused events led by the Host City.
    - Ensuring the Host City adheres to brand and communications guidelines at all times.
  • Actively represent the FIFA Volunteer Programme in public settings, including speaking at community events, hosting volunteer information sessions, and supporting recruitment activations.
  • Implement a coordinated approach with all stakeholders across all phases of the volunteer journey, including promotion, recruitment, role offers, training, and the tournament period.
  • Attend daily HQ meetings, Venue meetings and reporting on the status of the Volunteer Programme.
  • Participate in site visits to inspect potential pre-tournament and tournament-time required spaces.
  • Oversee the set-up and daily operations - act as a venue manager - dismantling and handover of the Volunteer Centres and spaces.
  • Align and coordinate with Host City and venue teams on logistics, storage, inventory, and distribution of volunteer recognition items as part of the Recognition Programme. Ensure venues adhere to the gift distribution calendar outlined in the Recognition Plan
  • Communicate with candidates and volunteers via Volunteer Support Channel and the FIFA Volunteer Platform.
Volunteer Management
  •  Recruit, train, manage, and lead Volunteer Coordinators within the assigned Host City, ensuring alignment, clear communication, and effective team collaboration.
  • Manage volunteers’ day-to-day to ensure efficient deployment and the well-being of volunteers on-site during each volunteer journey phase.
  • Ensure the volunteer services and recognition are provided as planned and liaise with local authorities when necessary.
  • Lead the recruitment, training and oversee the operation of the journey volunteers
  • Support with planning and delivery for the Official Draw, Playoffs and other FWWC27 official events, as required.
Volunteer Platform
  • Work extensively on the Volunteer Platform to manage all stages of the volunteer journey in the host city, including but not limited to interview scheduling, role assignment, uploading shifts, shift allocation, trainings, communication, Tournament-time operations and overall communications.
  • Identify and report system-related issues and diligently follow up to ensure their resolution or escalate as necessary to mitigate potential impacts.
Reporting and Feedback
  • Raise risks and concern to the Volunteer Management Central team in a timely manner
  • Prepare and submit daily reports during tournament time
  • Support the knowledge transfer programme to ensure planning and operational learnings are carried to the future events
  • Prepare and deliver the post-tournament report for the assigned Host City.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education
  • Master's in Sports Management or Event Management or Project Management preferred.
Experience
  • 5 years + of working for events, and strong understanding of volunteer management
  • Proven experience in leading and managing volunteer programmes supporting the volunteer journey, including fit for purpose attraction, promotion, recruitment, training, engagement, and tournament operations.
  • Knowledge of project management principles and methodologies, with the ability to plan, coordinate, and deliver multiple workstreams simultaneously.
  • High level of proficiency in Microsoft Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Experience in Volunteer Management System is an advantage. (Rosterfy knowledge is preferrable)
  • Demonstrated ability to develop beneficial working relationships with internal and external stakeholders, as well as with local networks within the Host City, especially among the football community.
  • Experience operating in complex, multi-stakeholder environments, with the flexibility and resilience to adapt to change and remain solution oriented.
  • A collaborative and inclusive team player, capable of working effectively with colleagues both on-site and remotely, while fostering a positive and respectful working environment.
  • Strong public speaking and presentation skills
  • Enthusiasm, and the ability to inspire and motivate volunteers
  • Ability to facilitate workshops, information sessions, and volunteer briefings
  • Community outreach and recruitment engagement skills.
  • Knowledge of the local landscape, community networks, and key stakeholders within the respective Host City is an advantage.
 Requirements
  • Right to work in Brazil
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Intermediate English (spoken and written)
  • Based in one of the listed cities (Belo Horizonte, Brasília, Fortaleza, Porto Alegre, Recife, Rio de Janeiro, Salvador & São Paulo) or able to relocate there independently

    Expected start date: June 2, 2026

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Accommodation Team Account Manager, the Coordinator Team Hotel will be a key member of the FIFA26 Accommodation team, and work in close collaboration with the  Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering  seamless accommodation services for all hotel operations for each team, ensuring services are activated, rooms and function spaces are prepared, reservations are managed, issues are resolved, and all invoices are reconciled, while maintaining FIFA service standards and facilitating smooth check-ins and check-outs.  

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Coordinator Team Hotel for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Brief the hotel on the operations with all involved hotel departments for each team staying at the hotel.  
  • Responsible for the activation of all services reserved by FIFA at each team hotel.  
  • Ensure readiness of the team’s services prior to their arrival, check rooms and function spaces, and prepare all room keys according to the rooming list.  
  • Ensure an efficient and smooth check-in process for all delegates. 
  • Meet and greet the team manager upon arrival, serving as the main point of contact for FIFA Accommodation.  
  • Coordinate and manage all changes to reservations on-site with the team manager, and inform FIFA HQ (account manager) of any changes.  
  • Resolve on-site issues with the hotel and the team as they arise.  
  • Provide daily reporting and attend meetings with FIFA HQ.  
  • Monitor invoices provided by the hotel and check all accounts on a daily basis.  
  • Maintain and ensure that the level of service provided by the hotel meets FIFA standards.  
  • Prepare the team’s departure, ensuring that all extra charges are paid before they leave.  
  • Ensure a seamless and efficient check-out process for the team.  
  • Prepare for the arrival of the next team.  
  • Finalize the reconciliation of all accounts (rooms, function spaces, F&B, laundry, etc.).  
  • Match the final invoice to actualized rooms and ensure credits and complimentary rooms are applied.

Dates
June 9, 2026  to July 7, 2026
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (business administration, management, leadership, entrepreneurship)
  • Postgraduate Education focusing on International Organizations or Sport Management preferred, but not required 
  • Willingness to work outside core working hours (online workshops accommodating different time zones)
Work Experience 
  • 3+ years experience in planning and leading in a guest-related service environment 
  • Proven experience in hotel operations, particularly in coordinating accommodation logistics for large groups or teams, ensuring all services are properly activated and managed.  
  • Strong background in organizing and overseeing team logistics, including room assignments, F&B services, and meeting space preparations, ensuring readiness prior to arrivals.  
  • Experience handling on-site operations, from smooth check-ins to resolving issues swiftly, ensuring seamless service delivery and maintaining high standards throughout. 
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to effectively coordinate between team managers, hotel staff, and FIFA HQ to ensure smooth operations and timely updates. 
  • Experience in monitoring, reconciling, and ensuring the accuracy of invoices, including ensuring correct application of charges, credits, and complimentary room allocations. 
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Accommodation Team Account Manager, the Coordinator Team Hotel will be a key member of the FIFA26 Accommodation team, and work in close collaboration with the  Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering  seamless accommodation services for all hotel operations for each team, ensuring services are activated, rooms and function spaces are prepared, reservations are managed, issues are resolved, and all invoices are reconciled, while maintaining FIFA service standards and facilitating smooth check-ins and check-outs.  

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Coordinator Team Hotel for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Brief the hotel on the operations with all involved hotel departments for each team staying at the hotel.  
  • Responsible for the activation of all services reserved by FIFA at each team hotel.  
  • Ensure readiness of the team’s services prior to their arrival, check rooms and function spaces, and prepare all room keys according to the rooming list.  
  • Ensure an efficient and smooth check-in process for all delegates. 
  • Meet and greet the team manager upon arrival, serving as the main point of contact for FIFA Accommodation.  
  • Coordinate and manage all changes to reservations on-site with the team manager, and inform FIFA HQ (account manager) of any changes.  
  • Resolve on-site issues with the hotel and the team as they arise.  
  • Provide daily reporting and attend meetings with FIFA HQ.  
  • Monitor invoices provided by the hotel and check all accounts on a daily basis.  
  • Maintain and ensure that the level of service provided by the hotel meets FIFA standards.  
  • Prepare the team’s departure, ensuring that all extra charges are paid before they leave.  
  • Ensure a seamless and efficient check-out process for the team.  
  • Prepare for the arrival of the next team.  
  • Finalize the reconciliation of all accounts (rooms, function spaces, F&B, laundry, etc.).  
  • Match the final invoice to actualized rooms and ensure credits and complimentary rooms are applied.

Dates
June 9, 2026  to July 7, 2026
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (business administration, management, leadership, entrepreneurship)
  • Postgraduate Education focusing on International Organizations or Sport Management preferred, but not required 
  • Willingness to work outside core working hours (online workshops accommodating different time zones)
Work Experience 
  • 3+ years experience in planning and leading in a guest-related service environment 
  • Proven experience in hotel operations, particularly in coordinating accommodation logistics for large groups or teams, ensuring all services are properly activated and managed.  
  • Strong background in organizing and overseeing team logistics, including room assignments, F&B services, and meeting space preparations, ensuring readiness prior to arrivals.  
  • Experience handling on-site operations, from smooth check-ins to resolving issues swiftly, ensuring seamless service delivery and maintaining high standards throughout. 
  • Excellent communication skills, with the ability to effectively coordinate between team managers, hotel staff, and FIFA HQ to ensure smooth operations and timely updates. 
  • Experience in monitoring, reconciling, and ensuring the accuracy of invoices, including ensuring correct application of charges, credits, and complimentary room allocations. 
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Team Hotel Coordinator – FIFA World Cup 26™ | San Francisco

data.key_responsibilities:
The Transport Supervisor supports daily transport delivery for assigned client groups during the FIFA World Cup 2026. This role ensures safe, efficient, and professional transport services and supports vehicle dispatch, driver coordination, and client movement.

Client Group Service Coordination 
  • Execute specialized transport support based on client group needs 
  • Manage driver check-in, briefing, and deployment 
  • Ensure accurate vehicle allocation and staging for client movements 
  • Support loading, dispatch, and arrival coordination at designated points 
Driver & Workforce Support 
  • Facilitate driver technology setup (tablets, mobile apps, TMM credentials) 
  • Distribute job cards and ensure VAPP/accreditation compliance 
  • Promote uniform, conduct, and service standards 
Operational Delivery 
  • Ensure vehicles are clean, fueled, and operational prior to dispatch 
  • Coordinate arrival/departure flows to maintain on-time performance 
  • Assist with routing, fleet adjustments, and incident escalation 
Safety & Compliance 
  • Enforce depot and venue safety procedures 
  • Monitor access control, permissions, and safe vehicle movements 
  • Support emergency or contingency response as directed 
Documentation & Communication 
  • Maintain shift logs, attendance sheets, driver rosters, and reports 
  • Participate in briefings, handovers, and operational calls 
  • Provide proactive updates to the Depot Manager and Command Center
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Transport Operations, Logistics, or Hospitality, or equivalent experience. 
  • Knowledge of transport operations, guest services, or event logistics is an asset. 
Work Experience 
  • 3-5 years of experience in transport, hospitality, or event operations. 
  • Experience working in a supervisory role within large-scale or high-profile events preferred. 
  • Proven ability to manage small teams, coordinate logistics, and deliver service excellence under pressure. 
  • Experience collaborating with security teams, hotels, and transport providers is advantageous. 
  • Strong interpersonal skills, proactive communication, and confidence in decision-making. 
  • Demonstrates the highest levels of integrity, professionalism, and commitment.  
  • Positive attitude, patience, and persistence in problem-solving.  
  • Strong cultural awareness and the ability to work in an international environment. 
  • Client facing communication and guest services experience is a plus. 
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
