data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organizationally to the Venue Transport Manager, the Venue Transport Supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, and work in close collaboration with FIFA26 Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering Transport Operations at the FIFA World Cup 2026 stadiums.  
 
The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Transport Supervisor for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Work closely with existing Stadium teams to coordinate FIFA Constituent Group transport provisions and access to/from stadium and venues  
  • Work closely with the Security team to ensure transport plans are coordinated with the appropriate security provisions  
  • Liaise with bus operations staff and fleet operations staff during operational period to ensure a successful transportation operation  
  • Work with chosen suppliers/contractors within the various parking/traffic management/load zone locations  
  • Management of transport information desks  
  • Training and supervising volunteers and contractors  
  • Responsible for management of VAPP boards and transport load zones at each venue  
  • Responsible for placement, control and storage of transport related FF&E
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s Degree in Transport, Civil Engineering, Project management, Event Management, Major Sport events or a combination of education and experience relating to position. 
Work Experience 
  • For the FIFA26 Pitch-In Program the Venue Transport Team is open to partnering with diverse candidates across FIFA26 regardless of Education and Work Experience. The Venue Transport Managers will build a robust training and onboarding program. If candidates have a OneTeam spirit and willingness to learn, they will be a great fit on this team. 
  • 2 years’ work experience: ideally within the football, sport, or event industry. 
  • Able to effectively present information and respond to questions from support staff, managers, and clients.  
  • Must be motivated, capable of working independently, and have excellent written and oral communication skills.  
  • Willingness to learn transit operations involved in moving Client Groups by vehicles, including city layout.
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organizationally to the Client Services Guest Manager, the Supervisors, Guest – VVIP/VIP Hotel will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, and work in close collaboration with the Guest Operations Team, Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering Transport Operations. 
 
The Supervisor, Guest – VVIP/VIP Hotels is responsible for overseeing all transportation operations serving FIFA’s VVIP and VIP client groups at VVIP/VIP hotels during tournament time. This position ensures flawless execution of guest transport services, including coordination of drivers, vehicles, and schedules; maintaining direct communication with client service teams and security; and ensuring on-time, secure, and seamless transport experiences for guests. 
 
The main responsibilities and oversights of the Supervisor, Guest – VVIP/VIP Hotels for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Manage all VVIP/VIP hotel transport operations, ensuring seamless coordination between the hotel load zones, shuttle services, and scheduled fleet movements.  
  • Lead a team of transport coordinators assigned to VVIP/VIP hotels. Schedule shifts, conduct daily briefings, monitor performance, and ensure high service standards. 
  • Act as the primary point of contact for FIFA’s VVIP/VIP guests and their representatives on transport matters. Provide timely assistance, updates, and solutions to ensure exceptional client service.  
  • Liaise daily with the Client Services Manager, Security Liaison Officers (SLOs), Fleet Operations, and Hotel Logistics Teams to align on transport schedules, routes, and protocols. 
  • Track VVIP/VIP vehicle movements in real time to ensure punctuality and security compliance. 
  • Respond promptly to operational issues, vehicle breakdowns, or delays, coordinating with Fleet and Bus Operations to resolve them. 
  • Oversee the setup and maintenance of hotel load zones, signage, and transport infrastructure throughout the tournament. 
  • Maintain daily operational reports, incident logs, and escalate critical issues to the Client Services Guest Manager immediately. 
  • Uphold the highest standards of professionalism, and hospitality for all FIFA VVIP/VIP clients.

data.key_responsibilities:
We are seeking Tournament Operations Talent to support venue readiness, match-day operations, and live event delivery across stadiums and event sites in each Host City. This role plays a key part in executing a world-class global sporting event in a fast-paced, high-performance environment.

Assignments will be based on operational needs and individual experience.

Key Responsibilities
  • Support venue setup, readiness, and daily operations
  • Assist with match-day and event-day execution
  • Coordinate staff, volunteers, vendors, and internal teams
  • Manage workflows within assigned zones or functions
  • Support crowd flow, access control, and entry operations
  • Ensure facilities and infrastructure meet operational standards
  • Deliver high-quality guest and fan experiences
  • Address operational issues and escalate as needed
  • Maintain compliance with safety, security, and event protocols
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure smooth event delivery
Functional Areas
Access, Accreditation & Ticketing
  • Access Management
  • Accreditation
  • Ticketing
Broadcast, Media & Content
  • Broadcast Operations & Services
  • Communications
  • Content Services
  • Language Services
  • Media Operations & Services
Commercial & Partnerships
  • Licensing & Retail
  • Partnership Rights Delivery
  • Venue Business Strategy
Fan, Hospitality & Guest Operations
  • Accommodation
  • Fan Operations (including FIFA Fan Festival)
  • Guest Operations
  • Hospitality
  • Catering
Safety, Security & Government
  • Government Engagement
  • Safety & Security
Team & Competition Services
  • Competition Management
  • Team Services
Transportation & Movement
  • Arrivals & Departures
  • Event Transport
  • Venue Transport & Mobility
  • Fleet Management
  • Client Services
Venue & Event Operations
  • Ceremonies
  • Dressing & Signage
  • Stadium Entertainment
  • Venue Management
Workforce & Volunteer Operations
  • Volunteer Management
  • Workforce Management
Offsite Assignments
Some roles may support off-site operations, including:
  • Airports, hotels, and team base camps
  • Fan zones and public viewing events
  • Accreditation and uniform distribution centers
  • Media and broadcast centers (IBC/MPC)
  • Transport hubs, fleet operations, and warehouses
  • Workforce and volunteer centers
  • VIP and hospitality locations
  • Host city coordination and stakeholder operations
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Qualifications
  • Must be currently authorized to work onsite in Toronto, Canada
  • Previous experience in event operations, logistics, hospitality, or a related field preferred
  • Ability to work in fast-paced, dynamic environments
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail
  • Flexible availability, including evenings, weekends, and event days
  • Ability to stand, walk, and work on-site for extended periods
Work Conditions
  • Based in Toronto, with work across multiple venues and sites
  • Tournament-time role with variable hours based on event schedules
  • May require early mornings, late nights, and extended shifts
data.title: Tournament Operations Event Roles – FIFA World Cup 26™ | Toronto

data.key_responsibilities:
The Non-Competition Site Operations roles are key positions within the FIFA World Cup 26™ Safety and Security team, responsible for ensuring site readiness and delivering site-level security oversight for FIFA and Venue Specific Team Facilities. These facilities consist of, but are not limited to, hotels, training sites, and ticketing centers that a participating national football team or FIFA staff/VIPs will occupy prior to and during tournament time.

This job posting supports multiple Non-Competition Site Operations roles, including: 
  • FIFA Sites Security Specialist
  •  Non-Competition Venue Specific Security Specialist
FIFA Sites Security Specialist - Key Responsibilities
  • Ensure assigned FIFA Sites are operationally ready for security delivery prior to and throughout tournament operations 
  • Verify that private security personnel are correctly briefed, trained, and deployed in accordance with their assigned duties and post orders 
  • Confirm that security infrastructure and equipment (access control points, barriers, CCTV, lighting, radios, signage, etc.) are installed (where applicable), functional, and appropriately configured 
  • Act as the on-site security focal point for assigned FIFA Sites during tournament-time operations 
  • Oversee daily security operations, including access control, patrols, VIP protection measures (where applicable), and response readiness
  • Monitor, document, and report all security incidents, issues, and near misses at assigned FIFA Sites 
  • Initiate and support incident response in coordination with the FWC26TM  Safety & Security Operations Center (SSOC) and Safety and Security Senior Manager 
  • Serve as primary security liaison
  • Submit daily operational reports and ad-hoc updates as required 
Non-Competition Venue Specific Security Specialist - Key Responsibilities
  • Ensure assigned Venue Specific Team Facility sites are operationally ready for security delivery prior to and throughout tournament operations 
  • Verify that private security personnel are correctly briefed, trained, and deployed in accordance with their assigned duties and post orders 
  • Confirm that security infrastructure and equipment are installed (where applicable), functional, and appropriately configured 
  • Act as the on-site security focal point for assigned  Venue Specific Team Facility sites during tournament-time operations, primarily when a Team is in residence 
  • Oversee daily security operations, including access control, patrols, Team protection measures (where applicable), and response readiness 
  • Monitor, document, and report all security incidents, issues, and near misses at assigned  Venue Specific Team Facility sites 
  • Initiate and support incident response in coordination with the Safety & Security Operations Center (SSOC) and Safety and FWC26TM Security Senior Manager 
  • Serve as primary security liaison
  • Submit daily operational reports and ad-hoc updates as required
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor's degree in a related field associated with safety, security and/or project management is a plus  
  • Must be available for duration of the Team’s residence 
  • Must be willing to work long hours and be on call 24/7 during the tournament
Work Experience
  • Minimum 3–5 years of experience in security operations, preferably in major events, hospitality, logistics, or VIP environments 
  • Experience supervising or coordinating private security services 
  • Familiarity with hotel security operations and transport/logistics environments 
  • Strong understanding of access control, incident response, and operational security principles 
  • Ability to work independently, manage multiple sites, and perform under pressure 
  • Major event or international tournament experience is an advantage
  •  Located in: Pachuca, Quintana Roo, Guadalajara, Monterrey, or Mexico City. 
Languages
  • Fluent in english (required)
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a strong plus
  • Additional languages beneficial due to the international nature of FIFA guests.
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Non-Competition Site Security Operations - FIFA World Cup 26™

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Airport Deputy Manager, the Airport Supervisor will be a key member of the FIFA26 Operational Services team and will work in close collaboration with the FIFA Transport team and other key individuals responsible for supporting the Airport Deputy Manager in overseeing all day-to-day airport operations, ensuring safety, efficiency, and compliance with aviation regulations and FIFA client service levels. This role acts as a second-in-command, providing leadership across multiple functional areas, including airside and landside operations, terminal management, customer service, and emergency response coordination. 

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Airport Supervisor for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Support and deputize for the Arrivals & Departures Teams Manager, ensuring continuity of leadership and operational decision-making. 
  • Lead, train, and motivate a diverse team of paid staff and volunteers to deliver exceptional airport services in line with FIFA World Cup 2026 standards. 
  • Oversee daily and shift briefings to align team performance, ensure service consistency, and address operational challenges during extended hours. 
  • Manage all operational aspects of arrivals and departures, ensuring alignment with governance structures and coordination with airport stakeholders. 
  • Develop and maintain effective relationships with airlines, airport authorities, transport providers, and FIFA functional areas to facilitate seamless client movements. 
  • Direct service planning and delivery for all FIFA constituent groups, ensuring compliance with defined service levels (DSLAs) and tournament objectives. 
  • Coordinate with transport providers across multiple modes—including bus, fleet, and public transport—to ensure efficient passenger flow and timely connections. 
  • Oversee the integration of arrivals and departures operations with other FWC2026 functional areas at venue and site level, including stadiums and non-competition locations. 
  • Lead preparation and delivery of client-facing materials, including transport guides, operational updates, and presentations for key stakeholder meetings. 
  • Ensure readiness and excellence in service delivery for all pre-tournament test events and operational simulations. 
  • Foster a proactive, collaborative, and safety-focused team culture capable of performing under pressure and meeting tight deadlines. 
Dates
June 6, 2026  - July 14, 2026

data.benefits: FIFA26 is a proud equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion and prohibits discrimination of any kind on the basis race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.
data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Client Services PMA Cluster Supervisors, the Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, and work in close collaboration with the Team Services Team, Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering Transport Operations. 

The Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator is responsible for the full scope of transport operations at an assigned Team Base Camp Hotel or Venue Specific Hotel, including the
hotel and training site. This role ensures the seamless delivery of bus, fleet, and airport/FBO (Arrivals & Departures) services for participating teams, in strict alignment with FIFA service levels and operational procedures. The coordinator serves as the primary liaison between drivers, suppliers, the Team (Security) Liaison Officer (TLO/TSLO), airport authorities, and FIFA Transport, ensuring bubble-to-bubble operations, client satisfaction, and readiness at all times.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
Communication 
  • Maintain daily communication with Fleet, Bus, A&D, Venue Transport, Security, Logistics, and Client Services functions. 
  • Hold daily face-to-face meetings with TLO/TSLO to align on schedules, procedures, and driver management. 
  • Receive and inspect all vehicles before each shift, conducting driver briefings on daily activities and changes. 
  • Escalate issues immediately to ensure continuity of operations. 
Client Services 
  • Act as the primary point of contact for TLO/TSLO at the TBC or Venue Specific Team Hotel. 
  • Ensure compliance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and all FIFA transport policies. 
  • Coordinate with TLO/TSLO to maintain strict bubble-to-bubble operations. 
  • Ensure load zones are available and maintained for all team activities. 
  • Support drivers in resolving operational issues, escalating when required to depot or FIFA transport. 
  • Liaise with training site and hotel staff, ensuring readiness of signage, cones, and transport infrastructure. 
  • Submit daily reports on concerns, incidents, and operational performance to the Client Services Cluster Supervisor. 
Fleet & Bus Operations 
  • Manage daily driver check-in/out and shift scheduling at the TBCH or VSTH. 
  • Ensure Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are correctly reflected in TMM and followed by staff. 
  • Secure and manage staging areas, load zones, and parking at the TBC and training site or VSTH. 
  • Oversee all aspects of TMM system management, including last-minute schedule or vehicle changes. 
  • Validate/facilitate driver training, conduct test runs, and ensure familiarity with routes. 
  • Supervise drivers during shifts, ensuring compliance with procedures, grooming standards, and readiness. 
  • Ensure all vehicles are properly maintained, fueled, branded, equipped with VAAPs, and operationally ready. 
  • Provide accurate driver and vehicle information as required. 
  • Manage rate card requests in collaboration with TLO/TSLO. 
  • Coordinate with the depot team for vehicle/driver issues, including intercity and convoy movements. 
Arrivals & Departures (A&D) / FBO Operations 
  • Act as the on-site coordinator for all team arrivals and departures via airports and FBOs. 
  • Communicate with FBO teams, ACS, and airport authorities for vehicle staging and tarmac access. 
  • Ensure drivers know approved routes, timings, and positioning for FBO operations. 
  • Guarantee all vehicles are in position at least 60 minutes prior to ETA and properly briefed. 
  • Coordinate security convoys, logistic trucks, and family/friends rate card vehicles when applicable. 
  • Liaise with ACS for live convoy tracking and resolve any discrepancies on site. 
  • Inspect and establish SOPs for first international arrivals and last international departures, training colleagues in all procedures. 
  • Liaise with Bus/Fleet and TSLO for convoy planning on all movements. 
  • Coordinate media access and badging for first arrivals, ensuring compliance with FBO/media protocols. 
  • Stay onsite until after ETD to confirm successful departures or resolve contingencies. 
  • Any other duties that may be required.  
Dates
May 1, 2026  to July 2, 2026

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
The Ceremonies Senior Manager leads the end‑to‑end planning, creative development, and operational delivery of all official ceremonies for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, including the Opening and Closing Ceremonies, Medal and Trophy Ceremonies, Pre‑Match Ceremonies (PMC), the Final Match Half‑Time Show (if applicable), and all additional ceremonial moments.

Reporting to the Head of Entertainment, this role is responsible for overseeing the full lifecycle of each ceremony—from planning and preparation through to live execution—ensuring full alignment with FIFA’s vision and brand standards. The role also manages the implementation of the operational delivery across the entire FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, in accordance with the Ceremonies Concept developed by FIFA.

Working closely with the Entertainment team and key Functional Areas, the Ceremonies Senior Manager acts as the senior operational lead for all ceremony delivery, ensuring creative and operational approvals are met and delivering iconic, culturally resonant moments that celebrate women’s football on its biggest global stage.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Ceremonies Project Leadership 
  • Lead the operational delivery and implementation of the Ceremonies Concept provided by FIFA.
  • Manage the end-to-end planning and delivery of all official ceremonies across the FWWC27 tournament.
  • Oversee ceremonies planning and delivery from concept development through preproduction, execution, dissolution, and legacy handover.
  • Ensure full alignment with the Entertainment team in Zurich across all ceremonial workstreams.
  • Manage the approval process for all ceremony related creative and operational deliverables.
  • Provide senior decision-making support on creative direction, technical feasibility, and operational prioritisation.
  • Lead the development of ceremony operational plans, production schedules, rundowns, and show formats for all ceremonies, including PMC.
  • Coordinate with Venue Management, Broadcast, Safety & Security, and other Functional Areas to integrate ceremonies seamlessly into matchday and event day operations.
  • Act as the senior escalation authority for all ceremony related operational matters.
  • Ensure all ceremonies reflect the approved creative vision, brand standards, cultural sensitivities, and tournament wide entertainment strategy.
Team Leadership
  • Manage and mentor the Ceremonies Team, providing clear direction, performance oversight, and accountability structures.
  • Mentor and support all Ceremonies personnel, including fulltime staff, freelancers, and event time workforce, ensuring consistent standards, development pathways, and operational readiness.
  • Support internal talent development through coaching, guidance, and structured feedback.
  • Build team structures and optimize collaboration across creative, technical, and operational units.
  • Foster strong coordination between venue based ceremonies teams to maintain consistency in delivery standards and operational execution.
  • Foster strong teamwork, knowledge exchange, and operational alignment with the Stadium Entertainment Team to ensure unified delivery across all entertainment and ceremonial workstreams.
Major Ceremonies Oversight
  • Manage planning and delivery management for all major ceremonial events, including: Opening Ceremony, Closing Ceremony, Medal and Trophy Ceremonies, Final Match Half-Time Show (if applicable), Pre-Match Ceremonies throughout the tournament
  • Ensure that each major ceremony reflects the ambition, brand standards, and cultural significance of the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™.
Creative & Concept Development / Creative Concept, Content Production & Management
  • Collaborate with the Entertainment team to develop and refine the ceremonial concept and creative direction for each ceremony.
  • Integrate host country cultural elements into ceremonial programming in a meaningful, authentic, and respectful way.
  • Oversee creative brief development, content commissioning, and pre-production workflows with internal teams and external agencies.
  • Ensure all creative content — including music, graphics, videos, scripts, translations, choreography and performances — is approved and locked in line with operational timelines.
  • Manage stakeholder approval pathways to ensure content is delivered and archived in accordance with operational requirements.
  • Manage IP and copyright compliance for all ceremony content.
Planning Scheduling & Documentation
  • Develop and maintain detailed project plans, WIP reports, and milestone trackers for all ceremony workstreams.
  • Ensure all operational and creative decisions are fully documented and version controlled.
  • Manage multiple suppliers, conduct regular update meetings, coordinate implementation processes, and supervise payments and reporting.
  • Prepare written and oral presentations, high-level briefings, and reports for senior leadership and FIFA counterparts.
  • Organize and participate in Review, Project Team, and Status Update meetings.
  • Lead the creation, management, and archiving of project plans, workflows, venue documentation, and readiness tools across all ceremony workstreams.
  • Manage the development of operational plans, production schedules, rundowns, and rehearsal programmes for all ceremonies.
  • Ensure readiness standards are met at all ceremony venues and oversee execution on the day of each event.
  • Act as the key operational decision-maker to maintain seamless delivery during live ceremonies.
  • Oversee rehearsal planning, scheduling, approvals, and stakeholder coordination for all ceremonies.
  • Direct venue-level pre-production processes including technical surveys, staging assessments, and infrastructure planning.
Supplier & Budget Management
  • Lead early stage budget modelling, scenario planning, and resourcing strategies to support creative and operational planning. 
  • Develop and manage Scopes of Work, Procurement activities, supplier evaluations, contract negotiations, in line with FWWC27 management and procurement policies. Manage procurement activities for stadium entertainment suppliers, including RFP development, evaluation, and contract negotiation. 
  • Maintain full budget oversight including forecasting, cost control, variations, and invoicing. Manage budget oversight for the stadium entertainment workstream, including forecasting, cost control and invoicing. 
  • Ensure contractual deliverables and timelines are aligned with preproduction schedules and operational readiness requirements. 
  • Conduct regular supplier update meetings and coordinate implementation processes. 
  • Supervise payments, deliverables and performance reports from entertainment suppliers.
Stakeholder & Functional Area Liaison
  • Act as the senior liaison between the Ceremonies Team and all relevant Functional Areas, including Venue Operations, Broadcast, Safety & Security, Marketing, and FIFA SPE.
  • Deliver concise briefings, alignment updates, and escalation management across all internal and external stakeholders.
  • Build and maintain relationships with Host Country national bodies, cultural organizations, and local partners contributing to ceremonies.
  • Ensure early phase cross functional integration to support seamless operational delivery.
Venue Readiness & Execution
  • Lead preparation of Site Operational Plans (SOPs), ceremony production schedules, and PMC rundowns.
  • Direct venue level preproduction processes including technical surveys, staging assessments, performer logistics, and infrastructure planning.
  • Ensure readiness standards are met at all ceremony venues and oversee execution on the day of each event.
  • Act as the key operational decision maker to maintain seamless delivery during live ceremonies.
  • Oversee rehearsal planning, scheduling, approvals, and stakeholder coordination.
  • Ensure all creative and technical elements are validated during pre event testing and rehearsal phases.
Other Duties
  • Perform additional responsibilities as required by senior managership aligned with project delivery.
  • Represent the Ceremonies function in cross-functional planning forums and senior managership briefings.
  • Support the Head of Entertainment in strategic planning, operational readiness, and tournament wide entertainment integration.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education:
  • Bachelor's degree or similar level of higher education or equivalent in industry experience.
Experience
  •  7+ years' experience in live event production and ceremonies management, ideally within major international sporting events or large-scale public events.
  • Prior experience in a FIFA tournament or comparable major international sports event is highly desirable.
  • Demonstrated experience managing complex, large-scale ceremonies including opening/closing ceremonies and awards presentations.
  • Proven track record in team managership, creative development, supplier management, and cross-functional coordination.
  • Significant experience managing RFP/procurement processes and overseeing multi-supplier contracts and budgets.
  • Experience integrating cultural programming and working with national cultural bodies and creative agencies.
Skills & Competencies
  • ADMINISTRATIVE SKILLS: Ability to manage office and production documentation including, RFPs, presentations, show rundowns, rehearsal schedules, scripts, and permits accurately.
  • ADAPTABILITY: Ability to adapt to evolving creative direction and operational challenges in high-pressure environments.
  • FLEXIBILITY: Willingness to work irregular hours, including evenings and weekends, across the full tournament period.
  • COMMUNICATION SKILLS: Outstanding written and verbal communication skills; able to manage complex stakeholder relationships at a senior level.
  • ORGANIZATIONAL SKILLS: Exceptional project management skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent ceremony workstreams to tight deadlines.
  • PROBLEM-SOLVING SKILLS: Strategic and creative approach to problem-solving; ability to make clear decisions under pressure.
  • CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLABORATION: Proven ability to work collaboratively across internal teams and diverse functional areas, ensuring alignment and contributing to the successful achievement of delivery goals.
  • INTERPERSONAL SKILLS: Builds effective working relationships across internal and cross-functional teams, applying strong interpersonal skills to support smooth coordination and operational alignment
  • CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: Deep awareness and respect for diverse cultures; committed to ensuring ceremonies are inclusive, authentic, and globally resonant.
  • PASSION FOR ENTERTAINMENT: Genuine passion for live performance, ceremony production, and the power of sport to inspire.
Certifications & Requirements
  • Fluent in English and Brazilian Portuguese.
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning/collaboration tools.
  • Experience of Monday.com an advantage.
  • Knowledge of and previous event / tournament experience across the stadiums in Brazil a plus
  • The right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. data.key_responsibilities:
The Stadium Entertainment Senior Manager is responsible for leading the planning, development, and delivery of all Stadium Entertainment operations across the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™. 

Reporting to the Head of Entertainment, this role will oversee the full lifecycle of stadium entertainment activations across all tournament venues and IBCC, ensuring a consistent, high-quality and globally resonant fan experience.

This role ensures the successful implementation of the FIFA‑developed Stadium Entertainment Concept, overseeing creative, technical, and operational workstreams to deliver world‑class matchday entertainment experiences.

The position provides senior leadership, cross‑functional coordination, and operational oversight to ensure consistency, quality, and readiness.

The Stadium Entertainment Senior Manager will act as the primary point of contact for stadium entertainment matters across the tournament footprint, including the IBCC, working closely with venue teams, broadcast, and the FIFA Special Events & Entertainment (SPE) team to guarantee world-class entertainment delivery at every match.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Stadium Entertainment Project Leadership

·       Lead the operational delivery and implementation of the Stadium Entertainment Concept across all tournament venues in alignment with FIFA Entertainment (EET) in Zurich.
·       Oversee stadium￼specific pre￼production workflows including creative approvals, technical scoping, resource planning, and risk assessments.
·       Main point of contact with stadiums for LED screen specs and content delivery formats
·       Ensure alignment of objectives, timelines, scopes, and approvals for all Stadium Entertainment activities.
·       Provide support on creative direction, technical feasibility, and operational prioritisation for Stadium Entertainment.
·       Lead the operational planning and delivery of all stadium entertainment workstreams across all FWWC27 venues and IBCC.
·       Provide senior‑level oversight and escalation management for all Stadium Entertainment operational issues.
·       Lead the development of stadium entertainment operational plans, rundowns, and show formats for all venues.
·       Oversee implementation of the FIFA‑approved Entertainment Concept across all stadium entertainment touchpoints.
·       Coordinate with Venue Management, Broadcast, FTI, and other Functional Areas to integrate entertainment seamlessly into match‑day operations.
·       Ensure consistency of fan experience and entertainment standards across the full tournament venue footprint.

IBCC Stadium Entertainment Project Leadership

·       Lead the Stadium Entertainment operation at the IBCC, overseeing all entertainment areas, operational structures, and functional setup within that environment.
·       Ensure the IBCC stadium entertainment programme is fully aligned with the broader tournament entertainment concept and operational standards.
·       Coordinate IBCC-specific entertainment requirements with broadcast partners, venue management, and relevant Functional Areas.
·       Act as the primary point of contact for all stadium entertainment matters within the IBCC, managing suppliers and technical crews on site

Team Leadership

·       Manage the Stadium Entertainment Team, providing clear direction, performance oversight, and accountability structures.
·       Mentor and support all Stadium Entertainment personnel, including full‑time staff, freelancers, and event‑time workforce, ensuring consistent standards, development pathways, and operational readiness across all staffing groups.
·       Support internal talent development through coaching and guidance.
·       Build team structures and optimize collaboration across creative, technical, and operational units.
·       Provide leadership and operational oversight to all Stadium Entertainment personnel working within the IBCC environment, ensuring clarity of roles, responsibilities, and communication flows.
·       Foster strong coordination between venue‑based teams and IBCC‑based teams to maintain consistency in delivery standards and operational execution.
·       Foster strong teamwork, knowledge exchange, and operational alignment with the Ceremonies Team to ensure unified delivery across all entertainment and ceremonial workstreams.

Venue Readiness & Execution

·       Lead preparation of Site Operational Plans (SOPs), stadium production schedules, and PMC rundowns for Stadium Entertainment.
·       Oversee pre‑production processes including technical surveys, AV infrastructure assessments, and supplier installation planning.
·       Ensure readiness standards are met and oversee execution during live operations.
·       Act as the key problem‑solver to maintain seamless delivery during matchday entertainment.
·       Oversee rehearsal planning, scheduling, approvals, and stakeholder coordination.
·       Ensure all entertainment elements are validated during pre‑event testing and rehearsal phases.
·       Direct venue‑level pre‑production processes including technical surveys, infrastructure assessments, and installation planning.

Content Production & Management

·       Coordinate delivery, approval, and archival of all entertainment content including music, graphics, video, and scripts.
·       Oversee creative brief development, content commissioning, and preproduction workflows with internal teams and external agencies.
·       Manage stakeholder approval pathways to ensure content is locked in accordance with operational timelines.
·       Work with the Head of Entertainment and FIFA SPE team to align creative execution with brand and communications guidelines.
·       Manage IP and copyright compliance for all entertainment content deployed at venues.

Planning Scheduling & Documentation

·       Oversee development of pre‑production timelines, milestone structures, and cross‑venue coordination processes for Stadium Entertainment.
·       Track and report delivery progress through detailed WIP reports and Project plan. Ensure all operational and creative decisions are documented and version-controlled.
·       Prepare written and oral presentations, high-level briefings and reports for Head of Entertainment, Senior leadership and FIFA counterparts.
·       Organize and participate in regular Review, Project Team, and Status Update meetings.
·       Report on project progress and stakeholder alignment to the Head of Entertainment.
·       Lead the creation, management, and archiving of project plans, workflows, venue documentation, and readiness tools across all Stadium Entertainment workstreams.

Supplier & Budget Management

·       Lead early‑stage budget modelling, scenario planning, and resourcing strategies to support creative and operational planning.
·       Develop and manage Scopes of Work, Procurement activities, supplier evaluations, contract negotiations, in line with FWWC27 management and procurement policies.
·       Maintain full budget oversight including forecasting, cost control, variations, and invoicing. Ensure contractual deliverables and timelines are aligned with pre‑production schedules and operational readiness requirements.
·       Conduct regular supplier update meetings and coordinate implementation processes.

Stakeholder & Functional Area Liaison

·       Act as the senior liaison between the Entertainment Team and all relevant Functional Areas, including Venue Operations, Broadcast, Safety & Security, Marketing, and FIFA SPE.
·       Deliver concise briefings, alignment updates, and escalation management across all internal and external stakeholders.
·       Build and maintain relationships with Host Country national bodies, cultural organizations, and local partners contributing to ceremonies.
·       Ensure early‑phase cross‑functional integration to support seamless operational delivery.

Venue Readiness & Execution

·       Lead preparation of Site Operational Plans (SOPs), stadium production schedules, and PMC rundowns for Stadium Entertainment.
·       Oversee pre‑production processes including technical surveys, AV infrastructure assessments, and supplier installation planning.
·       Ensure readiness standards are met and oversee execution during live operations.
·       Act as the key problem‑solver to maintain seamless delivery during matchday entertainment.
·       Oversee rehearsal planning, scheduling, approvals, and stakeholder coordination.
·       Ensure all entertainment elements are validated during pre‑event testing and rehearsal phases.
·       Direct venue‑level pre‑production processes including technical surveys, infrastructure assessments, and installation planning.

Other Duties

·       Perform additional responsibilities as required by Senior Management aligned with project delivery.
·       Represent the Ceremonies function in cross-functional planning forums and Senior Management briefings.
·       Support the Head of Entertainment in strategic planning, operational readiness, and tournament‑wide entertainment integration.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education:

·       Bachelor's degree or similar level of higher education or equivalent in industry experience.

Experience

·       7+ years' experience in live event production and ceremonies management, ideally within major international sporting events or large-scale public events.
·       Prior experience in a FIFA tournament or comparable major international sports event is highly desirable.
·       Demonstrated experience managing complex, large-scale ceremonies including opening/closing ceremonies and awards presentations.
·       Proven track record in team management, creative development, supplier management, and cross-functional coordination.
·       Significant experience managing RFP/procurement processes and overseeing multi-supplier contracts and budgets.
·       Experience integrating cultural programming and working with national cultural bodies and creative agencies.

Skills & Competencies

·       ADMINISTRATIVE SKILLS: Ability to manage office and production documentation including RFPs, presentations, show rundowns, rehearsal schedules, scripts, and permits accurately.
·       ADAPTABILITY: Ability to adapt to evolving creative direction and operational challenges in high-pressure environments.
·       FLEXIBILITY: Willingness to work irregular hours, including evenings and weekends, across the full tournament period.
·       COMMUNICATION SKILLS: Outstanding written and verbal communication skills; able to manage complex stakeholder relationships at a senior level.
·       ORGANIZATIONAL SKILLS: Exceptional project management skills with the ability to manage multiple concurrent ceremony workstreams to tight deadlines.
·       PROBLEM-SOLVING SKILLS: Strategic and creative approach to problem-solving; ability to make clear decisions under pressure.
·       CROSS-FUNCTIONAL COLABORATION: Proven ability to work collaboratively across internal teams and diverse functional areas, ensuring alignment and contributing to the successful achievement of delivery goals.
·       INTERPERSONAL SKILLS: Builds effective working relationships across internal and cross-functional teams, applying strong interpersonal skills to support smooth coordination and operational alignment.
·       CULTURAL SENSITIVITY: Deep awareness and respect for diverse cultures; committed to ensuring ceremonies are inclusive, authentic, and globally resonant.
·       PASSION FOR ENTERTAINMENT: Genuine passion for live performance, ceremony production, and the power of sport to inspire.

Certifications & Requirements

·       Fluent in English and Brazilian Portuguese.
·       Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning/collaboration tools.
·       Experience of Monday.com an advantage.
·       Knowledge of and previous event / tournament experience across the stadiums in Brazil a plus
·      The right to work in Brazil.
·      Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
data.title: Senior Manager, Stadium Entertainment

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
The Stadium Entertainment Manager will be responsible for the planning, coordination and delivery of all in-stadium entertainment activations across all assigned venues of the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™. 

Reporting to the Head of Entertainment and supporting the Stadium Entertainment Senior Manager, this role ensures that all entertainment elements — including pre‑match programming, music, audio‑visual content, half‑time activations, and fan engagement moments — are delivered to the highest standard. The role supports and coordinates the implementation of the Stadium Entertainment operational delivery across the entire FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, in accordance with the Stadium Entertainment Concept developed by FIFA.

Working closely with venue teams, technical suppliers, and the broader Entertainment department, the Stadium Entertainment Manager will act as the primary entertainment point of contact for their assigned venue(s), managing operational workflows, coordinating suppliers, and overseeing delivery timelines from a central coordination function. This role works in close collaboration with the on-site venue entertainment teams, providing the planning structure, content, and operational direction required for seamless execution on match days.

The Stadium Entertainment Manager is an office-based coordination role suited to an experienced live events professional with strong project management skills, who can drive high-quality entertainment delivery across multiple venues through effective planning, clear communication, and proactive stakeholder management.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Stadium Entertainment Delivery

·       Manage the day-to-day planning and delivery of all entertainment activations at assigned venue(s).
·       Execute the FIFA-approved Entertainment Concept at venue level, maintaining quality and consistency throughout the tournament.
·       Coordinate all entertainment elements on match days, including pre-match shows, half-time activations, music programming and AV content.
·       Act as the primary on-site entertainment point of contact for venue management, technical crews, and Functional Area partners

Team Support

·       Support the Stadium Entertainment Team by contributing to day‑to‑day coordination, operational readiness, and delivery standards across all entertainment activities.
·       Assist with the onboarding and support of Stadium Entertainment personnel—including full‑time staff, freelancers, and event‑time workforce—helping ensure consistent standards and operational preparedness.
·       Contribute to internal talent development through daily guidance, structured feedback, and knowledge sharing within the entertainment workstream.
·       Support effective collaboration across creative, technical, and operational units involved in stadium entertainment delivery.
·       Contribute to coordination between venue‑based entertainment teams to maintain consistency in delivery standards and operational execution across all stadiums.
·       Foster strong teamwork, knowledge exchange, and operational alignment with the Ceremonies Team to ensure unified delivery across entertainment and ceremonial workstreams.

Technical & Production Coordination

·       Liaise with technical suppliers including AV, LED, sound, and lighting providers to ensure correct installation and operation of all entertainment infrastructure.
·       Support pre-event technical surveys, production walkthroughs, and infrastructure assessments at assigned venues.
·       Coordinate rehearsals and technical run-throughs, ensuring all entertainment elements are tested and validated before match day.
·       Manage show rundowns, cue sheets, and entertainment schedules for each match at assigned venue(s).

Venue Readiness & Execution

·       Support the preparation of Site Operational Plans (SOPs), stadium production schedules, and PMC rundowns for Stadium Entertainment.
·       Assist in pre￼production processes, including coordinating technical surveys, gathering stadium LED screen specs, AV infrastructure reviews, and supplier installation planning.
·       Contribute to readiness activities and provide on‑site support during live operations to ensure delivery standards are met.
·       Support problem solving during matchday entertainment, escalating issues to the Senior Manager as needed.
·       Coordinate rehearsal planning, scheduling, documentation, and stakeholder communication in alignment with Senior Manager guidance.
·       Support the validation of entertainment elements during pre‑event testing and rehearsal phases.
·       Assist with venue‑level pre‑production tasks, including gathering technical information, supporting infrastructure assessments, and coordinating installation planning.

Content Production & Management

·       Coordinate the receipt, testing, and deployment of all entertainment content at venue level, including music, video, graphics, and scripts.
·       Ensure that all content is approved, correctly formatted, and delivered on time in line with operational deadlines.
·       Maintain accurate records of content usage and ensure IP/copyright compliance at venue level.

Planning Scheduling & Documentation

·       Prepare match-day reports, schedules, show notes and post-event summaries for the Stadium Entertainment Senior Manager.
·       Maintain up-to-date operational documentation including rundowns, contact lists, and venue entertainment plans.
·       Participate in regular team meetings, briefings, and review sessions.
·       Flag operational risks and escalate issues to the Stadium Entertainment Senior Manager in a timely manner.

Supplier & Budget Management

·       Support procurement processes as directed by the Stadium Entertainment Senior Manager, including supplier briefing and evaluation.
·       Manage day-to-day relationships with entertainment suppliers and contractors at assigned venue(s).
·       Assist with budget management tasks, including forecasting inputs, cost‑tracking, variation documentation, and invoice processing, ensuring accuracy and timely follow‑up.

·       Help monitor contractual deliverables and timelines, ensuring they remain aligned with pre‑production schedules and operational readiness requirements, and escalate risks to the Senior Manager as needed.

Stakeholder & Functional Area Liaison

·       Coordinate with Venue Operations, Broadcast, Protocol, Safety, and other Functional Areas and stakeholders to integrate entertainment seamlessly into match-day operations.
·       Support early‑phase cross‑functional integration to support seamless operational delivery.

Other Duties

·       Perform additional responsibilities as required by line management aligned with project delivery.
·       Support the Head of Entertainment and Senior Stadium Entertainment Manager in planning, operational readiness, and tournament‑wide entertainment integration.
·       Support cross-venue entertainment activities and cover other venues as required during the tournament.

data.description:
As the organiser of some of the biggest and most iconic sport events in the world, we lead the way in the sport and event industry.

Join the team as a freelancer and support us on our mission.
data.key_responsibilities:
These are your key tasks:
  • Manage assigned FIFA retail and eCommerce accounts independently or under supervision
  • Pro-active contribution to the overall FIFA retail strategy providing ongoing strategic input and recommendations to drive sales and revenue, and the consumer journey
  • Account management (incl contractual T&Cs, rights and obligations) of FIFA’s retail operators
  • Plan and manage the product assortment and buy for FIFA retail channels together with retail operators to ensure best in class assortment
  • Identify the product opportunities and drive the product development with licensing managers to ensure event & 365 retail and eCommerce needs are met
  • Work with FIFA cross-functional teams to coordinate product collection releases, promotions, storytelling, marketing campaigns and consumer engagement initiatives
  • Manage and plan the product roll-out calendar and marketing calendar for FIFA retail channels
  • Manage specific licensing & retail projects, including DTR projects as directed
  • Support the licensing & retail team in managing FIFA licensees in relation to FIFA retail opportunities and needs
  • Works closely and proactively with the L&R controller to ensure all projects are measured and tracked to drive data-based decisions and recommendations and financial remunerations are accurately recorded, processed and invoiced
  • Oversee market benchmarking and monitor unauthorised FIFA IP product & retail activities
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • Minimum 5 years experience in a similar or related role
  • Experience in assortment planning or buying across DTC/retail & ecommerce
  • Experience in sports or fashion brands is an asset
  • Project management experience is an asset
  • Demonstrable experience in retail marketing is an asset

  • Fluent in English

  • Advanced Excel skills
  • Experience in working with planning tools and systems is a plus
  • Highly organized and capable of setting priorities
  • Results oriented but understand the bigger picture
  • Solid knowledge of the retail and ecommerce landscape in addition to the sports licensed business and its key players
  • Highly analytical and capable of translating data into useful insights
  • Excellent team player
  • Able to work to hard deadlines
  • Stress resilient and able to work in a very dynamic environment
  • Highly skilled at ‘connecting the dots’ and solid understanding of the dependency between product, distribution and marketing
  • Competent and confident at building solid relationships and working cross-functionally
  • Comfortable working in a multi-cultural and complex stakeholder environment
  • Willingness to travel when required

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
We are seeking Tournament Operations Talent to support venue readiness, match-day operations, and live event delivery across stadiums and event sites in each Host City. This role plays a key part in executing a world-class global sporting event in a fast-paced, high-performance environment.

Assignments will be based on operational needs and individual experience.

Key Responsibilities
  • Support venue setup, readiness, and daily operations
  • Assist with match-day and event-day execution
  • Coordinate staff, volunteers, vendors, and internal teams
  • Manage workflows within assigned zones or functions
  • Support crowd flow, access control, and entry operations
  • Ensure facilities and infrastructure meet operational standards
  • Deliver high-quality guest and fan experiences
  • Address operational issues and escalate as needed
  • Maintain compliance with safety, security, and event protocols
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure smooth event delivery

Airports, hotels, and team base camps

Accreditation and uniform distribution centers

Transport hubs, fleet operations, and warehouses
Workforce and volunteer centers
VIP and hospitality locations
Host city coordination and stakeholder operations

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Client Services PMA Cluster Supervisors, the Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, and work in close collaboration with the Team Services Team, Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering Transport Operations. 

The Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator is responsible for the full scope of transport operations at an assigned Team Base Camp Hotel or Venue Specific Hotel, including the
hotel and training site. This role ensures the seamless delivery of bus, fleet, and airport/FBO (Arrivals & Departures) services for participating teams, in strict alignment with FIFA service levels and operational procedures. The coordinator serves as the primary liaison between drivers, suppliers, the Team (Security) Liaison Officer (TLO/TSLO), airport authorities, and FIFA Transport, ensuring bubble-to-bubble operations, client satisfaction, and readiness at all times.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
Communication 
  • Maintain daily communication with Fleet, Bus, A&D, Venue Transport, Security, Logistics, and Client Services functions. 
  • Hold daily face-to-face meetings with TLO/TSLO to align on schedules, procedures, and driver management. 
  • Receive and inspect all vehicles before each shift, conducting driver briefings on daily activities and changes. 
  • Escalate issues immediately to ensure continuity of operations. 
Client Services 
  • Act as the primary point of contact for TLO/TSLO at the TBC or Venue Specific Team Hotel. 
  • Ensure compliance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and all FIFA transport policies. 
  • Coordinate with TLO/TSLO to maintain strict bubble-to-bubble operations. 
  • Ensure load zones are available and maintained for all team activities. 
  • Support drivers in resolving operational issues, escalating when required to depot or FIFA transport. 
  • Liaise with training site and hotel staff, ensuring readiness of signage, cones, and transport infrastructure. 
  • Submit daily reports on concerns, incidents, and operational performance to the Client Services Cluster Supervisor. 
Fleet & Bus Operations 
  • Manage daily driver check-in/out and shift scheduling at the TBCH or VSTH. 
  • Ensure Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are correctly reflected in TMM and followed by staff. 
  • Secure and manage staging areas, load zones, and parking at the TBC and training site or VSTH. 
  • Oversee all aspects of TMM system management, including last-minute schedule or vehicle changes. 
  • Validate/facilitate driver training, conduct test runs, and ensure familiarity with routes. 
  • Supervise drivers during shifts, ensuring compliance with procedures, grooming standards, and readiness. 
  • Ensure all vehicles are properly maintained, fueled, branded, equipped with VAAPs, and operationally ready. 
  • Provide accurate driver and vehicle information as required. 
  • Manage rate card requests in collaboration with TLO/TSLO. 
  • Coordinate with the depot team for vehicle/driver issues, including intercity and convoy movements. 
Arrivals & Departures (A&D) / FBO Operations 
  • Act as the on-site coordinator for all team arrivals and departures via airports and FBOs. 
  • Communicate with FBO teams, ACS, and airport authorities for vehicle staging and tarmac access. 
  • Ensure drivers know approved routes, timings, and positioning for FBO operations. 
  • Guarantee all vehicles are in position at least 60 minutes prior to ETA and properly briefed. 
  • Coordinate security convoys, logistic trucks, and family/friends rate card vehicles when applicable. 
  • Liaise with ACS for live convoy tracking and resolve any discrepancies on site. 
  • Inspect and establish SOPs for first international arrivals and last international departures, training colleagues in all procedures. 
  • Liaise with Bus/Fleet and TSLO for convoy planning on all movements. 
  • Coordinate media access and badging for first arrivals, ensuring compliance with FBO/media protocols. 
  • Stay onsite until after ETD to confirm successful departures or resolve contingencies. 
  • Any other duties that may be required.  
Dates
May 1, 2026  to July 2, 2026
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Sports Management, Hotel Management, Project Management, Transport Operations, Logistics, or Hospitality, or equivalent experience. 
  • Knowledge of transport operations, guest services, or event logistics is an asset. 
Work Experience 
  • Proven experience in Event transport, logistics, or operations, preferably at large-scale international events. 
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple stakeholders and staff under pressure. 
  • Experience supervising drivers, staff, or suppliers in a dynamic operational environment. 
  • Familiarity with transport systems (e.g., TMM) and airport/FBO procedures is a strong advantage. 
  • Experience working in a client-facing supervisory role within large-scale or high-profile events preferred. 
  • Strong interpersonal skills, proactive communication, and confidence in decision-making. 
  • Demonstrates the highest levels of integrity, professionalism, and commitment.  
  • Positive attitude, patience, and persistence in problem-solving.  
  • Strong cultural awareness and the ability to work in an international environment. 
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Teams Transport Coordinator – FIFA World Cup 26™ | Kansas City

data.benefits:
In accordance with Washington' s Equal Pay and Opportunity Act, the pay for this position is a salary of $1,920/weekly. This estimate is based on this position being performed in Washington, and the range may not apply to similar positions in other locations. In determining actual base pay, FIFA 26 considers candidate education, experience, and other job-related factors allowed by law. An employee’s position within the salary range will be determined by various factors, including relevant education, experience, qualifications, certifications, skills, geographic location, performance metrics, shift, travel requirements, collective bargaining agreements, and business needs.

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Tournament Headquarters, the Stadium Fan Experience Manager will ensure the successful operational implementation and fulfilment of the dedicated stadium fan engagement area. The Stadium Fan Experience (SFE) is comprised of Commercial partner activations and FIFA-led initiatives designed to enhance the overall match day ticket holder experience.

The main responsibilities of the Stadium Fan Experience Manager include:  
  •  Support the SFE operational planning with a strong emphasis on logistical coordination
  •  Serve as the primary on-site point of contact for the production agency, ensuring alignment on timelines, deliverables and issue resolution
  •  Coordinate with key functional areas the seamless installation and operation of all activations
  •  Oversee commercial partner booth load-in and load-out activities, ensuring compliance with venue regulations, approved schedules and health and safety requirements
  •  Manage day-to-day communication with internal stakeholders and external vendors to maintain alignment throughout event build, operations and dismantling phases
  •  Monitor and enforce build schedules, site access protocols and venue rules across all SFE elements and partner activations
  •  Conduct site readiness walkthroughs to confirm all installations meet operational, safety and brand standards prior to event opening
  •  Troubleshoot and resolve on-site issues in real time, coordinating with relevant stakeholders to address technical, operational, or logistical challenges
  •  Maintain comprehensive operational documentation, including run-of-show inputs, contact lists and escalation procedures
  •  Support the onsite Marketing Venue Management team with Commercial Partner activities. 
  •  Complete event evaluations and reporting.
  •  Any other duties that may be required.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar level of education
Work Experience
  •  A minimum experience of 5 years working in sports event management and ideally sponsorship marketing (preferably in soccer).
  •  Proven experience with administrative responsibilities for international events, ideally in the sports industry.
  •  Knowledge of marketing operations in event settings.
  •  Good planning, analytical and conceptual skills.
  •  Service-oriented approach.
  •  Strong commitment to quality, results and accountability to a team.
  •  Resilience to stress and intensive work, persistence and commitment to procedures.
  •  Good IT skills and knowledge of working on sharing platforms.
  •  Ability to adopt a strategic view, take decisions and act promptly.
  •  Great attention to detail and to producing work of a high standard.
  •  Open-mindedness, flexibility, and adaptability.
  • Team Player.
  •  Experience or knowledge of the Host Countries (Canada, Mexico, USA) culture would be an added asset.
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
Under the responsibility of the FIFA27 Head of Finance and in collaboration with the FIFA Finance division in Zurich and the Senior Event Controller, the Event Controller will support in Brazil the budget and forecast process throughout the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ project lifecycle providing finance business partnering and support to the various budget owners and to FIFA27 senior management.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Implementation Phase 
    • Assist with the implementation of the FIFA budgeting and reporting tools and organize hands-on training with the budget owners in Brazil. 
    • Prepare cash flow forecast requirements in connection with the Brazilian operational budget. 
  • Planning & Operational Phase 
    • Support in Brazil the financial planning and budget review processes working closely with the FIFA Senior Event Controller (based in Zurich) and with all budget owners (Zurich and Brazil based). 
    • Work closely with the FIFA27 Senior Event Controller, to implement FIFA Finance processes in Brazil and to support a smooth consolidation of the full tournament operational budgets. 
    • Assist the Presentation of the quarterly forecast exercise as well as recommended corrective actions (when necessary) to the various stakeholders. 
    • Support the accrual process at year-end supporting the FIFA Zurich and FIFA27 Accounting teams and the various budget owners. 
    • Assist the review of monthly cost allocations to various project codes and prepare re-allocation journals when required. 
    • Support the preparation monthly management reports providing insightful commentary for budget owners and senior management. 
    • Monitor and maintain monthly cash flow forecasts, reflecting the Brazilian operational budget, to ensure the liquidity needs of FIFA27 are met. 
  • Perform ad-hoc analysis and prepare additional financial reports as requested by the Head of Finance and senior management.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics, or related field.  
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in accounting and Financial Planning & Analysis. 
  • Experience with budgeting and reporting tools. 
  • Experience in preparing financial statements, preferably in a multi-entity environment. 
  • Experience in an international environment.  
  • Experience in procurement of advantage. 
  • SAP experience preferred. 
  • Well-developed organisational skills with an ability to prioritize your workload and meet strict deadlines. 
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills to facilitate business partnering. 
  • Have a proactive hands-on approach to problem solving - a 'can do' attitude. 
  • Strong attention to detail and a focus on quality and accuracy. 
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office Excel, Power BI Word & PowerPoint presentation 
  • Ability to work independently and with a team in a fast-paced and high-volume environment. 
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Fluent in English (spoken and written)
  • Fluent in Portuguese (spoken and written)
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Tournament Headquarters and the Food and Beverage Managers, the F&B Coordinator will ensure the successful coordination of the F&B Concessions Program in the venue.

Main responsibilities of the F&B Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Ensure that services delivered are compliant with the F&B Concessions Operational Plan (number of staff, SKU allocation and sales prices per concession, activation hours, alcohol policy, SFE activations, etc.).
  • Ensure that the commercial and contractual rights of the Commercial Partners involved in the F&B Concessions Program are respected and implemented (brand exposure and product exclusivity, where applicable; use of dedicated equipment; respect of merchandising guidelines such as planograms, uniforms, menu boards, POS tactics, etc.).
  •  On Match Days: coordinate and report on operations and carry out regular quality controls (readiness by stadium gate opening, measure queuing times, monitor stock shortages, observe quality of customer service, etc.).
  •  Provide daily briefings to the local concessionaire and the Commercial Partner teams on site and ensure smooth communication between relevant stakeholders of the F&B Concessions Program.
  •  Liaise daily with the FIFA central team using the designated report template.
  •  Assist the FWC26 Commercial Rights Delivery Team in all matters related to FIFA Partnerships’ objectives and obligations.
  •  Assist the team with administrative responsibilities.
  •  Perform any other duties as required.

Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

  •  Assist the FWC26 Commercial Rights Delivery Team in all matters related to FIFA Partnerships’ objectives and obligations.
  •  Assist the team with administrative responsibilities.
  •  Perform any other duties as required.

Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.
