data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting to the  Senior Manager VIP/Venue Hotels the Manager, Venue Hotel will be a key member of the  FIFA World Cup 26™  Accommodation team and will work in close collaboration Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering  all FIFA-related operations at the venue hotel, ensuring seamless coordination of services, logistics, rooming lists, and daily operations while acting as the primary on-site liaison between the hotel, FIFA HQ, and other stakeholders. 

The main responsibilities and oversight of the  Manager, Venue Hotel for the  FIFA World Cup 26™  include: 
  • Consolidate the requirements from all FIFA Functional Areas at the Venue Hotel 
  • Brief the hotel on the FIFA workforce standard operations with all involved hotel departments 
  • Responsible for the activation of all services reserved by FIFA at the Venue Hotel (meeting rooms, laundry, F&B arrangements…) 
  • Coordinate and supervise all logistic operations (storage, deliveries…) 
  • Ensure readiness of the meeting rooms and F&B services on daily basis 
  • Daily reporting and meetings with FIFA HQ 
  • On-site issue resolution with the hotel and the workforce staff 
  • Manage and check rooming list check on daily basis, ensuring readiness of the room for the arrivals 
  • On-site point of contact for the Reservation team at the tournament HQ ensuring follow-ups on the room inventory and releases 
  • Support the Team Hotel Manager as necessary and most particularly for overlapping teams' arrivals  
  • Support the VIP Hotel Manager as necessary  
  • Monitor invoices provided by the hotel and check all accounts on daily basis. 
  • Match final invoice to actualized rooms and ensure credits and complimentary rooms are applied 

Dates
May 14, 2026  to July 5, 2026

  • Postgraduate Education focusing on International Organizations or Sport Management preferred, but not required

Work Experience 
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (business administration, management, leadership, entrepreneurship) 
  • Postgraduate Education focusing on International Organizations or Sport Management preferred, but not required 
  • Willingness to work outside core working hours (online workshops accommodating different time zones) 
  • 5 years experience in planning and leading in a guest-related service environment  
  • Strong background in managing high-end or luxury hotel services, ensuring VIP and VVIP guest experiences meet premium standards.  
  • Proven ability to manage high-profile clients, coordinate with hotel management, and ensure seamless guest experiences.  
  • Experience in monitoring invoices, reconciling accounts, and ensuring contract compliance with hotel services. 
  • Ability to liaise with FIFA Functional Areas, hotel staff, and other key stakeholders to implement service levels and operational plans.  
  • Skilled in on-the-spot issue resolution, ensuring smooth operations under pressure.  
  • Understanding of tournament operations and large-scale accommodation management.
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
We are seeking Tournament Operations Talent to support venue readiness, match-day operations, and live event delivery across stadiums and event sites in each Host City. This role plays a key part in executing a world-class global sporting event in a fast-paced, high-performance environment.

Assignments will be based on operational needs and individual experience.

Key Responsibilities
  • Support venue setup, readiness, and daily operations
  • Assist with match-day and event-day execution
  • Coordinate staff, volunteers, vendors, and internal teams
  • Manage workflows within assigned zones or functions
  • Support crowd flow, access control, and entry operations
  • Ensure facilities and infrastructure meet operational standards
  • Deliver high-quality guest and fan experiences
  • Address operational issues and escalate as needed
  • Maintain compliance with safety, security, and event protocols
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure smooth event delivery
Functional Areas
Access, Accreditation & Ticketing
  • Access Management
  • Accreditation
  • Ticketing
Broadcast, Media & Content
  • Broadcast Operations & Services
  • Communications
  • Content Services
  • Language Services
  • Media Operations & Services
Commercial & Partnerships
  • Licensing & Retail
  • Partnership Rights Delivery
  • Venue Business Strategy
Fan, Hospitality & Guest Operations
  • Accommodation
  • Fan Operations (including FIFA Fan Festival)
  • Guest Operations
  • Hospitality
  • Catering
Safety, Security & Government
  • Government Engagement
  • Safety & Security
Team & Competition Services
  • Competition Management
  • Team Services
Transportation & Movement
  • Arrivals & Departures
  • Event Transport
  • Venue Transport & Mobility
  • Fleet Management
  • Client Services
Venue & Event Operations
  • Ceremonies
  • Dressing & Signage
  • Stadium Entertainment
  • Venue Management
Workforce & Volunteer Operations
  • Volunteer Management
  • Workforce Management
Offsite Assignments
Some roles may support off-site operations, including:
Airports, hotels, and team base camps
Fan zones and public viewing events
Accreditation and uniform distribution centers
Media and broadcast centers (IBC/MPC)
Transport hubs, fleet operations, and warehouses
Workforce and volunteer centers
VIP and hospitality locations
Host city coordination and stakeholder operations
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Qualifications
  • Previous experience in event operations, logistics, hospitality, or a related field preferred
  • Ability to work in fast-paced, dynamic environments
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail
  • Flexible availability, including evenings, weekends, and event days
  • Ability to stand, walk, and work on-site for extended periods
Work Conditions
  • Based in Miami, with work across multiple venues and sites
  • Tournament-time role with variable hours based on event schedules
  • May require early mornings, late nights, and extended shifts

At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
We are seeking Tournament Operations Talent to support venue readiness, match-day operations, and live event delivery across stadiums and event sites in each Host City. This role plays a key part in executing a world-class global sporting event in a fast-paced, high-performance environment.

Assignments will be based on operational needs and individual experience.

Key Responsibilities
  • Support venue setup, readiness, and daily operations
  • Assist with match-day and event-day execution
  • Coordinate staff, volunteers, vendors, and internal teams
  • Manage workflows within assigned zones or functions
  • Support crowd flow, access control, and entry operations
  • Ensure facilities and infrastructure meet operational standards
  • Deliver high-quality guest and fan experiences
  • Address operational issues and escalate as needed
  • Maintain compliance with safety, security, and event protocols
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure smooth event delivery
Functional Areas
Access, Accreditation & Ticketing
  • Access Management
  • Accreditation
  • Ticketing
Broadcast, Media & Content
  • Broadcast Operations & Services
  • Communications
  • Content Services
  • Language Services
  • Media Operations & Services
Commercial & Partnerships
  • Licensing & Retail
  • Partnership Rights Delivery
  • Venue Business Strategy
Fan, Hospitality & Guest Operations
  • Accommodation
  • Fan Operations (including FIFA Fan Festival)
  • Guest Operations
  • Hospitality
  • Catering
Safety, Security & Government
  • Government Engagement
  • Safety & Security
Team & Competition Services
  • Competition Management
  • Team Services
Transportation & Movement
  • Arrivals & Departures
  • Event Transport
  • Venue Transport & Mobility
  • Fleet Management
  • Client Services
Venue & Event Operations
  • Ceremonies
  • Dressing & Signage
  • Stadium Entertainment
  • Venue Management
Workforce & Volunteer Operations
  • Volunteer Management
  • Workforce Management
Offsite Assignments
Some roles may support off-site operations, including:
Airports, hotels, and team base camps
Fan zones and public viewing events
Accreditation and uniform distribution centers
Media and broadcast centers (IBC/MPC)
Transport hubs, fleet operations, and warehouses
Workforce and volunteer centers
VIP and hospitality locations
Host city coordination and stakeholder operations
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Qualifications
  • Previous experience in event operations, logistics, hospitality, or a related field preferred
  • Ability to work in fast-paced, dynamic environments
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail
  • Flexible availability, including evenings, weekends, and event days
  • Ability to stand, walk, and work on-site for extended periods
Work Conditions
  • Based in Miami, with work across multiple venues and sites
  • Tournament-time role with variable hours based on event schedules
  • May require early mornings, late nights, and extended shifts
data.title: Tournament Operations Event Roles – FIFA World Cup 26™ | Los Angeles

data.benefits: FIFA26 is a proud equal opportunity employer that is committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion and prohibits discrimination of any kind on the basis race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, pregnancy, age, national origin, disability status, genetic information, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

data.key_responsibilities:
We are seeking Tournament Operations Talent to support venue readiness, match-day operations, and live event delivery across stadiums and event sites in each Host City. This role plays a key part in executing a world-class global sporting event in a fast-paced, high-performance environment.

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Tournament Headquarters and the Food and Beverage Managers, the F&B Coordinator will ensure the successful coordination of the F&B Concessions Program in the venue.

Main responsibilities of the F&B Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Ensure that services delivered are compliant with the F&B Concessions Operational Plan (number of staff, SKU allocation and sales prices per concession, activation hours, alcohol policy, SFE activations, etc.).
  • Ensure that the commercial and contractual rights of the Commercial Partners involved in the F&B Concessions Program are respected and implemented (brand exposure and product exclusivity, where applicable; use of dedicated equipment; respect of merchandising guidelines such as planograms, uniforms, menu boards, POS tactics, etc.).
  •  On Match Days: coordinate and report on operations and carry out regular quality controls (readiness by stadium gate opening, measure queuing times, monitor stock shortages, observe quality of customer service, etc.).
  •  Provide daily briefings to the local concessionaire and the Commercial Partner teams on site and ensure smooth communication between relevant stakeholders of the F&B Concessions Program.
  •  Liaise daily with the FIFA central team using the designated report template.
  •  Assist the FWC26 Commercial Rights Delivery Team in all matters related to FIFA Partnerships’ objectives and obligations.
  •  Assist the team with administrative responsibilities.
  •  Perform any other duties as required.

Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar level of education
  Work Experience
  • Versatile profile with a strong enthusiasm for catering operations at mega-events.
  •  Good planning and analytical skills.
  •  Service-oriented approach (towards Commercial Partners).
  •  Strong commitment to quality, results, and accountability within a team.
  •  Resilience to stress and intensive work, persistence, and commitment to procedures.
  •  Good IT skills and experience using collaborative platforms.
  •  Great attention to detail and ability to produce work of a high standard.
  •  Open-mindedness, flexibility, and adaptability.
  •  Team player capable of working on a project involving many stakeholders.
  •  Result-oriented with an entrepreneurial mindset.
  • Experience in sports event management and, ideally, F&B / catering operations is a plus.
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Tournament Headquarters, the Stadium Fan Experience Manager will ensure the successful operational implementation and fulfilment of the dedicated stadium fan engagement area. The Stadium Fan Experience (SFE) is comprised of Commercial partner activations and FIFA-led initiatives designed to enhance the overall match day ticket holder experience.

The main responsibilities of the Stadium Fan Experience Manager include:  
  •  Support the SFE operational planning with a strong emphasis on logistical coordination
  •  Serve as the primary on-site point of contact for the production agency, ensuring alignment on timelines, deliverables and issue resolution
  •  Coordinate with key functional areas the seamless installation and operation of all activations
  •  Oversee commercial partner booth load-in and load-out activities, ensuring compliance with venue regulations, approved schedules and health and safety requirements
  •  Manage day-to-day communication with internal stakeholders and external vendors to maintain alignment throughout event build, operations and dismantling phases
  •  Monitor and enforce build schedules, site access protocols and venue rules across all SFE elements and partner activations
  •  Conduct site readiness walkthroughs to confirm all installations meet operational, safety and brand standards prior to event opening
  •  Troubleshoot and resolve on-site issues in real time, coordinating with relevant stakeholders to address technical, operational, or logistical challenges
  •  Maintain comprehensive operational documentation, including run-of-show inputs, contact lists and escalation procedures
  •  Support the onsite Marketing Venue Management team with Commercial Partner activities. 
  •  Complete event evaluations and reporting.
  •  Any other duties that may be required.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree or similar level of education
Work Experience
  •  A minimum experience of 5 years working in sports event management and ideally sponsorship marketing (preferably in soccer).
  •  Proven experience with administrative responsibilities for international events, ideally in the sports industry.
  •  Knowledge of marketing operations in event settings.
  •  Good planning, analytical and conceptual skills.
  •  Service-oriented approach.
  •  Strong commitment to quality, results and accountability to a team.
  •  Resilience to stress and intensive work, persistence and commitment to procedures.
  •  Good IT skills and knowledge of working on sharing platforms.
  •  Ability to adopt a strategic view, take decisions and act promptly.
  •  Great attention to detail and to producing work of a high standard.
  •  Open-mindedness, flexibility, and adaptability.
  • Team Player.
  •  Experience or knowledge of the Host Countries (Canada, Mexico, USA) culture would be an added asset.
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: Commercial Stadium Fan Experience Management - San Francisco

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Tournament Headquarters and the Food and Beverage Managers, the F&B Coordinator will ensure the successful coordination of the F&B Concessions Program in the venue.

Main responsibilities of the F&B Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Ensure that services delivered are compliant with the F&B Concessions Operational Plan (number of staff, SKU allocation and sales prices per concession, activation hours, alcohol policy, SFE activations, etc.).
  • Ensure that the commercial and contractual rights of the Commercial Partners involved in the F&B Concessions Program are respected and implemented (brand exposure and product exclusivity, where applicable; use of dedicated equipment; respect of merchandising guidelines such as planograms, uniforms, menu boards, POS tactics, etc.).
  •  On Match Days: coordinate and report on operations and carry out regular quality controls (readiness by stadium gate opening, measure queuing times, monitor stock shortages, observe quality of customer service, etc.).
  •  Provide daily briefings to the local concessionaire and the Commercial Partner teams on site and ensure smooth communication between relevant stakeholders of the F&B Concessions Program.
  •  Liaise daily with the FIFA central team using the designated report template.
  •  Assist the FWC26 Commercial Rights Delivery Team in all matters related to FIFA Partnerships’ objectives and obligations.
  •  Assist the team with administrative responsibilities.
  •  Perform any other duties as required.

Additionally, FIFA 26 offers a competitive benefits package, which includes medical, dental, vision, disability insurance, life insurance, and a 401k retirement plan.

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting to the Senior Manager VIP/Venue Hotels the Manager VIP Hotel will be a key member of the FIFA World Cup 26 Accommodation team and will work in close collaboration Operational Services and other key individuals responsible for delivering seamless FIFA-related operations at the VIP hotel, including service activation, guest accommodations, meeting space coordination, logistics management, and daily collaboration with hotel staff and FIFA HQ to ensure a world-class experience for VVIP guests, while managing invoicing, on-site issue resolution, and adherence to FIFA service standards.  

The main responsibilities and oversight of the Manager VIP Hotel for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include: 
  • Consolidate requirements from all FIFA Functional Areas at the VIP Hotel.  
  • Provide a centralized overview to hotel management and FIFA HQ.  
  • Oversee the activation of all FIFA-reserved services at the VIP Hotel.  
  • Coordinate the hotel briefing before the first arrivals.  
  • Participate in daily meetings with hotel staff and FIFA functional areas to review arrivals, room allocations, no-shows, and FIFA Club updates.  
  • Manage and verify rooming lists, ensuring accurate room assignments per FIFA service levels.  
  • Conduct pre-checks of VVIP suites to ensure correct room setup and collateral placement.  
  • Ensure a seamless check-in process, briefing reception staff on FIFA Service Levels, and preparing room keys for VVIP guests.  
  • Coordinate and supervise all logistical operations, including storage and deliveries.  
  • Centralize and oversee the booking schedule for meeting room requirements across all functional areas.  
  • Ensure daily readiness of meeting rooms and F&B services.  
  • Manage the relationship with the hotel during the implementation of the FIFA Club.  
  • Provide daily reporting and participate in meetings with FIFA HQ.  
  • Address and resolve on-site issues with hotel staff and FIFA Functional Areas.  
  • Monitor hotel invoices and verify account accuracy daily.  
  • Conduct daily checks of guest charges to be settled at check-out.  
  • Reconcile final invoices with actualized room usage, ensuring credits and complimentary rooms are applied 

Dates
June 6, 2026  to July 4, 2026

  • Postgraduate Education focusing on International Organizations or Sport Management preferred, but not required

Work Experience

  • Strong background in managing high-end or luxury hotel services, ensuring VIP and VVIP guest experiences meet premium standards.  
  • Proven ability to manage high-profile clients, coordinate with hotel management, and ensure seamless guest experiences.  
  • Experience in monitoring invoices, reconciling accounts, and ensuring contract compliance with hotel services. 
  • Ability to liaise with FIFA Functional Areas, hotel staff, and other key stakeholders to implement service levels and operational plans.  
  • Skilled in on-the-spot issue resolution, ensuring smooth operations under pressure.  
  • Understanding of tournament operations and large-scale accommodation management. 
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
We are seeking Tournament Operations Talent to support venue readiness, match-day operations, and live event delivery across stadiums and event sites in each Host City. This role plays a key part in executing a world-class global sporting event in a fast-paced, high-performance environment.

Assignments will be based on operational needs and individual experience.

Key Responsibilities
  • Support venue setup, readiness, and daily operations
  • Assist with match-day and event-day execution
  • Coordinate staff, volunteers, vendors, and internal teams
  • Manage workflows within assigned zones or functions
  • Support crowd flow, access control, and entry operations
  • Ensure facilities and infrastructure meet operational standards
  • Deliver high-quality guest and fan experiences
  • Address operational issues and escalate as needed
  • Maintain compliance with safety, security, and event protocols
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure smooth event delivery
Functional Areas
Access, Accreditation & Ticketing
  • Access Management
  • Accreditation
  • Ticketing
Broadcast, Media & Content
  • Broadcast Operations & Services
  • Communications
  • Content Services
  • Language Services
  • Media Operations & Services
Commercial & Partnerships
  • Licensing & Retail
  • Partnership Rights Delivery
  • Venue Business Strategy
Fan, Hospitality & Guest Operations
  • Accommodation
  • Fan Operations (including FIFA Fan Festival)
  • Guest Operations
  • Hospitality
  • Catering
Safety, Security & Government
  • Government Engagement
  • Safety & Security
Team & Competition Services
  • Competition Management
  • Team Services
Transportation & Movement
  • Arrivals & Departures
  • Event Transport
  • Venue Transport & Mobility
  • Fleet Management
  • Client Services
Venue & Event Operations
  • Ceremonies
  • Dressing & Signage
  • Stadium Entertainment
  • Venue Management
Workforce & Volunteer Operations
  • Volunteer Management
  • Workforce Management
Offsite Assignments
Some roles may support off-site operations, including:
  • Airports, hotels, and team base camps
  • Fan zones and public viewing events
  • Accreditation and uniform distribution centers
  • Media and broadcast centers (IBC/MPC)
  • Transport hubs, fleet operations, and warehouses
  • Workforce and volunteer centers
  • VIP and hospitality locations
  • Host city coordination and stakeholder operations
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Qualifications
  • Must be currently authorized to work onsite in Vancouver, Canada
  • Previous experience in event operations, logistics, hospitality, or a related field preferred
  • Ability to work in fast-paced, dynamic environments
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Highly organized with strong attention to detail
  • Flexible availability, including evenings, weekends, and event days
  • Ability to stand, walk, and work on-site for extended periods
Work Conditions
  • Based in Vancouver, with work across multiple venues and sites
  • Tournament-time role with variable hours based on event schedules
  • May require early mornings, late nights, and extended shifts

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Airport manager, the Deputy Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Operational Services team and will work in close collaboration with the FIFA Transport team and other key individuals responsible for supporting the Airport Manager in overseeing all day-to-day airport operations, ensuring safety, efficiency, and compliance with aviation regulations and FIFA client service levels. This role acts as a second-in-command, providing leadership across multiple functional areas, including airside and landside operations, terminal management, customer service, and emergency response coordination.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Deputy Airport Manager for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:  
  • Support and deputize for the Arrivals & Departures Teams Manager, ensuring continuity of leadership and operational decision-making.
  • Lead, train, and motivate a diverse team of paid staff and volunteers to deliver exceptional airport services in line with FIFA World Cup 2026 standards.
  • Oversee daily and shift briefings to align team performance, ensure service consistency, and address operational challenges during extended hours.
  • Manage all operational aspects of arrivals and departures, ensuring alignment with governance structures and coordination with airport stakeholders.
  • Develop and maintain effective relationships with airlines, airport authorities, transport providers, and FIFA functional areas to facilitate seamless client movements.
  • Direct service planning and delivery for all FIFA constituent groups, ensuring compliance with defined service levels (DSLAs) and tournament objectives.
  • Coordinate with transport providers across multiple modes—including bus, fleet, and public transport—to ensure efficient passenger flow and timely connections.
  • Oversee the integration of arrivals and departures operations with other FWC2026 functional areas at venue and site level, including stadiums and non-competition locations.
  • Lead preparation and delivery of client-facing materials, including transport guides, operational updates, and presentations for key stakeholder meetings.
  • Ensure readiness and excellence in service delivery for all pre-tournament test events and operational simulations.
  • Foster a proactive, collaborative, and safety-focused team culture capable of performing under pressure and meeting tight deadlines.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Project Management, Event Management, Major Sports Events, or a related field; an equivalent combination of education and relevant professional experience may be considered.
  • A master’s degree in a related discipline is considered an asset.
  • Proven experience in large-scale event operations, airport management, or logistics coordination within a complex stakeholder environment preferred.
  • Strong leadership, communication, and problem-solving skills with the ability to perform under pressure and meet strict deadlines.
Work Experience
  • Minimum of 3 years of airport transport operations experience, ideally within major sports, football, or event environments.
  • Proven track record in international mega-event operations, preferably across multiple world-class tournaments (e.g., FIFA World Cup, Olympic Games, or regional championships).
  • Demonstrated success working in multi-functional, fast-paced event organizations, managing complex logistics and tight timelines.
  • Experienced in team leadership, project delivery, and coordinating large-scale operations.
  • Skilled in building and maintaining partnerships with public and private sector stakeholders in collaborative environments.
  • Strong understanding of airport transport operations, service flow, and event-specific service levels.
  • Proficient in administration, planning, and project management principles.
  • Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and presentation abilities.
  • Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple priorities under pressure.
  • Strong interpersonal and facilitation skills, with the ability to motivate diverse teams toward common goals.
  • Adaptive thinker able to balance client needs, authority requirements, and FIFA standards.
  • Comfortable working both independently and collaboratively within corporate and project-based structures.
  • Demonstrates flexibility, resilience, and professionalism in dynamic or changing environments.
Languages
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
• TOC Services Lead Coordination — Serve as the primary coordination point for all Teams and Referee Services integration matters escalated within the TOC.
  • Communications Monitoring — Track activity across all Teams and Referee WhatsApp and Microsoft Teams channels to maintain real‑time situational awareness of client movements.
  • Issue & Risk Management — Identify, document, and coordinate resolution of all Teams and Referee client‑service issues and risks in WeTrack, working closely with Team Services and Refereeing Operations. Provide timely updates to TOC Services Duty Management and during all relevant integration meetings.
  • Daily Forward Look Leadership — Lead the PMA & Referees Daily Forward Look Meetings, ensuring alignment of movement data and confirming that Travel, Arrivals & Departures, Transport, Accommodation, and Logistics services are scheduled to meet client requirements and service standards.
  • Itinerary Validation — Review, cleanse, and verify itineraries across designated systems to ensure accuracy and prevent last‑minute conflicts.
  • Forward Look Reporting — Produce the Teams and Referees Services Forward Look Report, highlighting discrepancies and tracking required follow‑up actions.
  • Critical Journey Assurance — Monitor critical journeys using specialized tools (Itinerary Radar, TMM Live Panel, Monday.com), confirming journey completion and communicating any delays or incidents that may negatively impact client experience.

data.key_responsibilities:
These evergreen roles support VIP Hotel, FIFA Club, Guest Operations, and Leisure Program delivery for the FIFA World Cup 26™ across official Host Cities in the United States, Mexico, and Canada.

Final assignment(s) will be determined based on operational needs and candidate availability.

FIFA Club Assistant Manager - Role Overview
Reporting to the FIFA Club Manager, the FIFA Club Assistant Manager supports daily FIFA Club operations at assigned VIP Hotels, ensuring consistent service delivery, access control, and an exceptional guest experience.
Key Responsibilities
  • Support all FIFA Club operations, including desk services, F&B, entertainment, staffing, and access control
  • Implement Guest Management policies and procedures
  • Coordinate guest needs with Guest Operations, Guest Relations, and Executive Office
  • Maintain FIFA Club presentation and readiness throughout operations
  • Prepare daily operational reports and maintain accurate records

FIFA Club Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the VIP Hotel & Leisure Program Manager, the FIFA Club Coordinator supports frontline FIFA Club operations and guest services.
Key Responsibilities
  • Assist with guest greetings, access control, opening/closing duties, and services
  • Maintain awareness of FIFA Club offerings and daily schedules
  • Support discreet security awareness and guest familiarity
  • Coordinate guest needs with Transport, Accommodations, and Guest Relations
  • Adapt to changing schedules and operational requirements

Guest Hotel GOP Assistant Manager - Role Overview
Reporting to the Guest Hotel Manager, the Guest Hotel GOP Assistant Manager supports hotel-based Guest Operations and VIP hospitality delivery during the tournament.
Key Responsibilities
  • Support Guest Hotel operations and integrated event delivery
  • Assist with implementation of V/VIP guest experience concepts
  • Act as secondary contact for infrastructure and guest experience issues
  • Support training programs for staff and volunteers
  • Coordinate suppliers, reporting, and operational planning

Guest Hotel GOP Manager - Role Overview
Reporting to Central Hotel Operations, the Guest Hotel GOP Manager leads Guest Operations delivery at assigned Guest Hotels.
Key Responsibilities
  • Oversee Guest Hotel operations and VIP hospitality programs
  • Lead implementation of guest experience concepts and service standards
  • Serve as primary escalation point for infrastructure and guest issues
  • Manage suppliers, budgets, timelines, and risk
  • Maintain strategic oversight and reporting throughout the tournament

Leisure Program Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the VIP Hotel & Leisure Program Manager, the Leisure Program Coordinator supports premium leisure activities for priority guest groups.
Key Responsibilities
  • Maintain oversight of leisure offerings, schedules, capacities, and itineraries
  • Enter and manage bookings within leisure systems
  • Coordinate with program providers on logistics and guest needs
  • Ensure service delivery aligns with Guest Operations standards
  • Adapt to schedule changes and guest expectations

VIP Hotel Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the VIP Hotel & Leisure Program Manager, the VIP Hotel Coordinator supports daily VIP Hotel guest services and operations.
Key Responsibilities
  • Support welcome desk and FIFA Club guest services
  • Coordinate arrivals, departures, gifts, tickets, and package deliveries
  • Maintain awareness of hotel offerings and schedules
  • Coordinate guest needs with Transport and Accommodations
  • Ensure hotel presentation and service standards are maintained

Welcome Desk Coordinator - Role Overview
Reporting to the VIP Hotel & Leisure Program Manager, the Welcome Desk Coordinator supports frontline guest engagement at VIP Hotels.
Key Responsibilities
  • Assist with guest arrivals, departures, ticket requests, and services
  • Maintain awareness of daily hotel operations and offerings
  • Ensure compliance with Guest Operations policies
  • Coordinate guest needs with Transport and Accommodations
  • Deliver a consistent, welcoming guest experience

Locations : Assignments span multiple Host Cities. Not all roles are required in every location.

United States: Atlanta, Georgia · Boston, Massachusetts · Dallas, Texas · Houston, Texas · Kansas City, Missouri · Los Angeles, California · Miami, Florida · New York / New Jersey Area · Philadelphia, Pennsylvania · San Francisco, California · Seattle, Washington

Mexico: Guadalajara, Jalisco · Mexico City · Monterrey, Nuevo León

Canada: Toronto, Ontario

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

data.key_responsibilities:
The Non-Competition Site Operations roles are key positions within the FIFA World Cup 26™ Safety and Security team, responsible for ensuring site readiness and delivering site-level security oversight for FIFA and Venue Specific Team Facilities. These facilities consist of, but are not limited to, hotels, training sites, and ticketing centers that a participating national football team or FIFA staff/VIPs will occupy prior to and during tournament time.

This job posting supports multiple Non-Competition Site Operations roles, including: 
  • FIFA Sites Security Specialist
  •  Non-Competition Venue Specific Security Specialist
FIFA Sites Security Specialist - Key Responsibilities
  • Ensure assigned FIFA Sites are operationally ready for security delivery prior to and throughout tournament operations 
  • Verify that private security personnel are correctly briefed, trained, and deployed in accordance with their assigned duties and post orders 
  • Confirm that security infrastructure and equipment (access control points, barriers, CCTV, lighting, radios, signage, etc.) are installed (where applicable), functional, and appropriately configured 
  • Act as the on-site security focal point for assigned FIFA Sites during tournament-time operations 
  • Oversee daily security operations, including access control, patrols, VIP protection measures (where applicable), and response readiness
  • Monitor, document, and report all security incidents, issues, and near misses at assigned FIFA Sites 
  • Initiate and support incident response in coordination with the FWC26TM  Safety & Security Operations Center (SSOC) and Safety and Security Senior Manager 
  • Serve as primary security liaison
  • Submit daily operational reports and ad-hoc updates as required 
Non-Competition Venue Specific Security Specialist - Key Responsibilities
  • Ensure assigned Venue Specific Team Facility sites are operationally ready for security delivery prior to and throughout tournament operations 
  • Verify that private security personnel are correctly briefed, trained, and deployed in accordance with their assigned duties and post orders 
  • Confirm that security infrastructure and equipment are installed (where applicable), functional, and appropriately configured 
  • Act as the on-site security focal point for assigned  Venue Specific Team Facility sites during tournament-time operations, primarily when a Team is in residence 
  • Oversee daily security operations, including access control, patrols, Team protection measures (where applicable), and response readiness 
  • Monitor, document, and report all security incidents, issues, and near misses at assigned  Venue Specific Team Facility sites 
  • Initiate and support incident response in coordination with the Safety & Security Operations Center (SSOC) and Safety and FWC26TM Security Senior Manager 
  • Serve as primary security liaison
  • Submit daily operational reports and ad-hoc updates as required
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor's degree in a related field associated with safety, security and/or project management is a plus  
  • Must be available for duration of the Team’s residence 
  • Must be willing to work long hours and be on call 24/7 during the tournament
Work Experience
  • Minimum 3–5 years of experience in security operations, preferably in major events, hospitality, logistics, or VIP environments 
  • Experience supervising or coordinating private security services 
  • Familiarity with hotel security operations and transport/logistics environments 
  • Strong understanding of access control, incident response, and operational security principles 
  • Ability to work independently, manage multiple sites, and perform under pressure 
  • Major event or international tournament experience is an advantage
  •  Located in: Pachuca, Quintana Roo, Guadalajara, Monterrey, or Mexico City. 
Languages
  • Fluent in english (required)
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a strong plus
  • Additional languages beneficial due to the international nature of FIFA guests.
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organizationally to the Commercial Rights Delivery Headquarter (HQ) Team, the Commercial Rights Delivery Coordinator will play a key role in delivering the successful operational implementation and fulfilment of FIFA Commercial Partners’ World Cup contractual rights and obligations. 

Main responsibilities of the Commercial Rights Delivery Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include: 
  • Support the operational implementation and delivery of FIFA World Cup 2026™ Commercial Partner agreements at the venue level. 
  • Coordinate with stadium Commercial Rights Delivery team and internal functional areas to support the delivery of Commercial Partner rights and obligations.  
  • Support the implementation of clean site and Commercial Partner exclusivity measures across official sites.  
  • Assist with the execution of stadium marketing projects, including Value-in-Kind activities, Special Marketing Programmes, and other partner initiatives. 
  • Coordinate with marketing suppliers and support the delivery of their operational requirements and obligations.  
  • Support the Commercial Rights Delivery team with additional projects and provide operational support to the wider venue team during the tournament period.  
  • Assist with the preparation of tournament debrief materials and post-event reporting. 
  • Perform any other duties as required.

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting to the Senior Manager VIP/Venue Hotels the Manager VIP Hotel will be a key member of the FIFA World Cup 26 Accommodation team and will work in close collaboration Operational Services and other key individuals responsible for delivering seamless FIFA-related operations at the VIP hotel, including service activation, guest accommodations, meeting space coordination, logistics management, and daily collaboration with hotel staff and FIFA HQ to ensure a world-class experience for VVIP guests, while managing invoicing, on-site issue resolution, and adherence to FIFA service standards.  

The main responsibilities and oversight of the Manager VIP Hotel for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include: 
  • Consolidate requirements from all FIFA Functional Areas at the VIP Hotel.  
  • Provide a centralized overview to hotel management and FIFA HQ.  
  • Oversee the activation of all FIFA-reserved services at the VIP Hotel.  
  • Coordinate the hotel briefing before the first arrivals.  
  • Participate in daily meetings with hotel staff and FIFA functional areas to review arrivals, room allocations, no-shows, and FIFA Club updates.  
  • Manage and verify rooming lists, ensuring accurate room assignments per FIFA service levels.  
  • Conduct pre-checks of VVIP suites to ensure correct room setup and collateral placement.  
  • Ensure a seamless check-in process, briefing reception staff on FIFA Service Levels, and preparing room keys for VVIP guests.  
  • Coordinate and supervise all logistical operations, including storage and deliveries.  
  • Centralize and oversee the booking schedule for meeting room requirements across all functional areas.  
  • Ensure daily readiness of meeting rooms and F&B services.  
  • Manage the relationship with the hotel during the implementation of the FIFA Club.  
  • Provide daily reporting and participate in meetings with FIFA HQ.  
  • Address and resolve on-site issues with hotel staff and FIFA Functional Areas.  
  • Monitor hotel invoices and verify account accuracy daily.  
  • Conduct daily checks of guest charges to be settled at check-out.  
  • Reconcile final invoices with actualized room usage, ensuring credits and complimentary rooms are applied
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field (business administration, management, leadership, entrepreneurship) 
  • Postgraduate Education focusing on International Organizations or Sport Management preferred, but not required  
  • Willingness to work outside core working hours (online workshops accommodating different time zones) 
Work Experience 
  • 5 years experience in planning and leading in a guest-related service environment  
  • Strong background in managing high-end or luxury hotel services, ensuring VIP and VVIP guest experiences meet premium standards.  
  • Proven ability to manage high-profile clients, coordinate with hotel management, and ensure seamless guest experiences.  
  • Experience in monitoring invoices, reconciling accounts, and ensuring contract compliance with hotel services. 
  • Ability to liaise with FIFA Functional Areas, hotel staff, and other key stakeholders to implement service levels and operational plans.  
  • Skilled in on-the-spot issue resolution, ensuring smooth operations under pressure.  
  • Understanding of tournament operations and large-scale accommodation management. 
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
data.title: V.I.P Hotel Manager – FIFA World Cup 26™ | Dallas

data.description: FIFA27 established as the Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil, to work together with FIFA HQ in Zurich and globally, to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”).
data.key_responsibilities:
Working alongside the FIFA Workforce Management team and reporting to the FIFA27 Sr Manager Recruitment, the Coordinator, Recruitment is responsible for the recruitment of a talented and diverse workforce required to plan and deliver the FIFA Women's World Cup 2027.

This role plays an integral part in ensuring a successful tournament by , attracting top talent and overseeing the implementation and management of a best-in-class recruitment experience.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • End-to-end recruitment process of individuals hired under FIFA27, including sourcing, screening, shortlisting, interviewing, obtaining reference and background checks, offer/employment contract distribution.
  • Candidate Management: Coordinating interviews, managing candidate communications, and ensuring a smooth candidate experience.
  • Scheduling: Organizing interview schedules and meetings between candidates and hiring managers.
  • Administrative Support: Handling paperwork, maintaining recruitment databases, and assisting with onboarding processes.
  • Job Posting: Creating and posting job advertisements on various platforms.
  • Collaboration: Working closely with FIFA Workforce Management and FIFA27 hiring managers to understand staffing needs and requirements.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
• At least 1-2 years of experience in recruitment or human resources, preferably at a major sporting event or other large-scale mega event
  • Experience with Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS) and other HR software
  • Excellent verbal and written communication in English & Portuguese to interact effectively with candidates and internal teams
  • Strong organizational skills to manage multiple tasks and schedules
    Ability to build relationships and work collaboratively with hiring managers and team members
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
data.title: Recruitment Coordinator

data.description: FIFA27 established as the Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil, to work together with FIFA HQ in Zurich and globally, to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”).
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Head of Marketing, Brand and Promotion (FIFA27, Rio de Janeiro) and the Team Lead, Event Marketing (FIFA HQ, Zurich), the Event Marketing Manager will be a key member of the FIFA27 Marketing, Brand and Promotion team, and will work in close collaboration with the Event Marketing in Zurich and other key individuals responsible for all marketing campaigns for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.
 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Support the creation and implementation of strategic, high impact, end-to-end marketing campaigns to meet and exceed the objectives of the Tournament
  • In line with the wider FIFA27 and FIFA Zurich teams, define and manage comprehensive GTM multi-channel marketing plans (in close liaison with the digital marketing, ticketing, comms, BI, and brand teams) to support strategic goals to drive awareness, attendance, engagement and retainment
  • Liaise directly with internal and external tournament stakeholders as required including Host Cities and Stadiums, Commercial Partners, and Official Media Partners and Licensees to amplify campaigns.
  • Support the development of cross cultural and sector tactical and long-term 
    strategical promotional partnerships with partners and third parties for mutual benefit
  • Create and circulate relevant briefing, publishing plans, planning, valuation, tracking and final reporting documents related to marketing stakeholder’s needs as required
  • Implement influencer strategies in support of marketing objectives. Including mapping of influencers/ambassadors/legends/celebrities and identified events and potentially on-site management/support
  • Ad-hoc support of additional workstreams in relation to event marketing needs which may include contact/planning/liaising with football clubs or participating member associations (PMA’s)
  • Co-Develop launches of major brand assets, such as tournament logos, mascots, posters, and songs, as well as oversee multi-stage global and localised awareness and tournament related sales campaigns 
  • Become an expert in FIFA event marketing milestone planning and challenge the status quo and norm with new and creative marketing milestone concepts to ensure amplification and data capture
  • Provide stakeholders with timely and accurate information on promotional and marketing events, activations and opportunities and ensure stakeholders to drive optimisation of the opportunities.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education
  • Related bachelor’s degree or a similar level of higher education.
Skills & Competencies
  • Adaptable, effective and efficient under pressure, immovable deadlines, and in stressful situations 
  • Effective at working proactively and independently whilst providing regular updates, being able to communicate clearly and effectively via different channels with key stakeholders
  • Ability to convey ideas clearly and collaborate effectively with team members and stakeholders
  • Willingness to work non-traditional hours including evenings and weekends as needed
  • Knowledge of Brazilian and South American markets and culture and trends
  • Demonstrates strong attention to detail in all tasks and deliverables
Work Experience
  • 5-7 years’ proven experience delivering major brand and marketing plans, and favourable to hold experience with sport's governing bodies, LOCs and, or sports and entertainment rightsholders
  • Deep familiarity and understanding of the dynamics of football
  • Well-connected with existing connections with Key Stakeholders, Partners (Qualified Clubs, Leagues, Associations)
  • Experience in Brand Marketing and Marketing Operations, Communications, Events
  • A passion for football
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint), planning software and online collaboration tools (Monday.com) 
Requirements
  • Fluent in English and Portuguese
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
data.title: Event Marketing Manager

data.description: FIFA27 established as the Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil, to work together with FIFA HQ in Zurich and globally, to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”).
data.key_responsibilities:
The FIFA Women’s World Cup™ Trophy Tour exists to build anticipation and engagement ahead of the tournament, serving as a promotional platform to drive ticket sales, tourism, and global visibility. By bringing the Trophy to communities across Brazil and the world, it demonstrates FIFA’s truly global mission while positioning Brazilian host cities as vibrant, capable, and welcoming destinations at the heart of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™.

The FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ Trophy Tour Manager will lead the planning, marketing and delivery of the Host Country phase of the FIFA Women’s World Cup2027™ Trophy Tour across Brazil, ensuring a cohesive, high-impact and locally relevant execution across all Host Cities. They will also support in moments on the Global Tor that require local stakeholders in particular Brazilian Embassies in the 32 participating Member Associations.

Acting as the central point of coordination in-market, this role will drive alignment across FIFA’s global project team in Zurich, local stakeholders and all relevant internal functions. Working in close collaboration with the Experiential Marketing Manager in Zurich, the Manager will ensure that all FIFA Trophy Tour activities are strategically aligned, culturally relevant, and delivered to FIFA standards.

The role will also support the global leg of the FIFA Trophy Tour where required, ensuring continuity and consistency between international and Host Country phases. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Strategy, planning & alignment
  • Lead the development and execution of the Host Country FIFA Trophy Tour strategy and marketing approach across Brazil including all Host Cities in Brazil 
  • Ensure alignment with the overall FIFA Trophy Tour strategy, adapting where needed to maximize local relevance and impact 
  • Act as the primary point of contact for the eight Host Cities, coordinating planning, timelines, and delivery expectations 
  • Ensure integration with the wider FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ project, identifying opportunities to amplify impact across other initiatives 

Stakeholder management & governance
  • Manage relationships with Host Cities, local authorities, agencies and key stakeholders, acting as FIFA’s representative in-market 
  • Serve as the source of truth on local context, sensitivities and opportunities, proactively informing the global team 
  • Lead cross-functional coordination across FIFA internal teams (including Operations, Legends, Digital, Communications, Brand, Partnerships and Media), ensuring all workstreams are aligned, integrated and delivered against a unified plan 
  • Ensure consistent alignment and information flow with the Zurich-based FIFA team, proactively managing dependencies, timelines and decision-making across global and local stakeholders 
  • Oversee and approve local plans, including venues, run of show, schedules and promotional activities 
  • Ensure total compliance with FIFA rights, brand guidelines and partner commitments 
Marketing, communications & content
  • Oversee the development and execution of local promotional plans across channels (digital, media, partners, on-the-ground) in alignment with the broader global marketing and communications plan
  • Ensure Host City activations are culturally relevant, locally resonant and aligned with the overall campaign narrative 
  • Inspire Host Cities and hosting stakeholders at a local level to invest as many resources as possible to deliver the best FIFA Trophy Tour events possible. 
  • Lead coordination of media activities in-market, approved by the project team and FIFA Communications
  • Oversee content capture, approvals and distribution across FIFA channels and stakeholders (including .com, CRM, VNRs and social platforms.
On-site delivery & operations
  • Represent FIFA on-site across selected FIFA Trophy Tour stops in Brazil and support on global stops if needed
  • Oversee delivery of FIFA communications, promotional and content activities, ensuring high-quality execution and stakeholder experience 
  • Ensure proper representation, handling and protection of FIFA assets, including the FIFA Women’s World Cup™ Trophy and FIFA Legend 
  • Coordinate on-site stakeholders to ensure smooth operations, including media, partners and local teams 
Reporting & performance tracking
  • Monitor progress of the FIFA Trophy Tour across Host Cities and ensure timely escalation of risks or issues 
  • Provide regular and comprehensive reporting on activities, performance and key learnings 
  • Contribute to post-event evaluation and recommendations for future FIFA Trophy Tours 
Global coordination
  • Support the Zurich-based project team on the global leg where required.
  • Coordinate with FIFA27 Government relations to pitch the FIFA Trophy Tour opportunities to Brazilian Government representatives and embassies for inclusion both phases of the FIFA Trophy Tour to facilitate their involvement in official events and support local engagement opportunities 
  • Ensure continuity and consistency between the global and Host Country phases, capturing and sharing key learnings to inform delivery across the tour
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
• Bachelor’s Degree or similar level of education in Marketing. 
  • Minimum of 5 years of work experience in Marketing and Events or Experiential projects, ideally within sport or major international events 
  • Preferred previous experience in football and/or professional sports. 
  • Strong understanding of sports marketing, including sponsorship, brand communication and fan engagement
  • Proven ability to deliver integrated marketing communications and promotional campaigns across online and offline channels
  • Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple workstreams in a fast-paced and evolving environment 
  • Experience managing complex internal and external stakeholder relationships, including at senior level
  • Demonstrated ability to work cross-functionally, building strong relationships and aligning diverse teams towards a shared objective
  • Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to navigate discussions, resolve issues and drive decisions in a constructive and controlled manner
  • High level of adaptability and resilience, with the ability to respond to changing priorities, evolving plans and a dynamic operating environment
  • Comfortable working across time zones and flexible working hours, with willingness to travel extensively as required
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools e.g. Monday.com

Requirements
  • Fluent in English and Portuguese
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.
data.title: Event Experiences Marketing Manager - FIFA Trophy Tour

data.description: FIFA27 established as the Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil, to work together with FIFA HQ in Zurich and globally, to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”).
data.key_responsibilities:
The Event Experiences Marketing Manager (FFF) supports and implements all strategic deliverables prescribed by the Experiential Marketing Manager (FFF) based in Zurich. This includes developing, supporting, and delivering innovative and best-in-class FFF deliverables and other experiential marketing activations that might be developed. The core deliverable is to lead the strategic and commercial development and positioning of the FIFA Fan Festival™ across Host Cities of the FIFA Women’s World Cup Brazil 2027, focusing on the commercial, promotional, brand, entertainment, and partner and stakeholder integrations. Working closely with internal and external stakeholders, the manager will help shape the narrative, drive partner and government integration, and develop innovative fan engagement in line with FIFA’s strategic objectives from a local perspective.

This role and team will work in tandem with the Special Events team at FIFA27, who hold accountabilities for all operational elements of the FFF. However, on a day‑to‑day basis, the Strategy and Operations teams will operate and be perceived as one unified team.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

FIFA Fan Festival™ FWWC2027 - Commercial & Strategy
  • Support the development and implementation of the FIFA Fan Festival™ commercial and strategic framework. 
  • Support communications and promotional planning across Host Cities. 
  • Liaise with commercial and media partners (alongside the local Commercial and Media partnership teams) to support project alignment and approvals. 
  • Prepare workshop planning, presentations, and strategic alignment sessions.
  • Maintain all SharePoint documentation and ensure excellent level of project document management 
  • Lead on local conversations and meetings while delivering detailed meetings and project updates including meeting minutes and communications for reporting back to the team in Zurich.
  • Support budget tracking, procurement processes, and relevant strategic documentation. 
  • Support local issue resolution and escalation processes, ensuring key decisions, risks and dependencies are clearly communicated to the Zurich team.
  • Support the review of plans and materials to ensure alignment with FIFA commercial rights, partner obligations, and clean site principles.
  • Provide local market insights and stakeholder contexts to support strategic decision making, planning, and delivery when needed.
FIFA Fan Festival™ FWWC2027 - Host City Coordination
  • Support Host Cities throughout all strategy and commercial processes. 
  • Act as a key point of contact for Host Cities at local level and any other local stakeholders. 
  • Coordinate requests, tracking, review and adaptation of Host City deliverables, including promotional copy, brand assets, content inputs and local information. 
  • Review and adapt Host City content, copy and materials to ensure consistency with FIFA tone, messaging, objectives and standards. 
  • Develop a strong understanding of the FWWC27 brand manual and ensure Host City and stakeholder materials are reviewed in line with FIFA brand requirements. 
  • Coordinate feedback, brand approvals, and final sign-off with relevant internal FIFA teams. Or communicate with FIFA Zurich where necessary.
  • Support the local Special Events team to ensure FIFA Fan Festival™ strategic, commercial, promotional and brand requirements are understood and integrated into Host City planning and delivery.
  • Support the coordination of content capture 
  • Delivery of local reporting inputs and post event documentation for FIFA27 and Host Cities and relevant stakeholders.  Experiential Projects
  • Act as project manager for selected experiential marketing projects as required. 
  • Coordinate project planning, timelines, deliverables, stakeholder alignment, and execution. 
  • Support RFP processes, agency evaluation, supplier onboarding, and procurement processes when required. 
  • Manage day-to-day coordination with agencies and suppliers, ensuring clear briefs, timelines, feedback rounds, and delivery expectations. 
  • Maintain all project documentation, trackers, meeting notes, action lists, and approval records. 
  • Ensure projects are delivered on time, within scope and in line with FIFA standards and identify, track and communicate key project risks, dependencies, decisions and escalation points.
  • Support post-project reporting, learnings and evaluation, including documentation of outcomes, performance and recommendations.

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
The Team Services workforce is responsible for ensuring world-class operational support for all participating Teams across Team Base Camps, Stadiums, and Venue Specific Training Sites during the FIFA World Cup 2026™. 

Reporting organisationally into various leads depending on assignment (Team Services Coordinator, Team Facilities Coordinator), this cluster includes the following roles: 
  • Team Services Venue Officer (TSVO) 
  • These roles work in close collaboration with Training Site Authority, Team Services, and Media Operations, Transportation, Security, and other key Functional Areas to ensure seamless delivery of all operational, logistical, and service requirements for Teams. 
Team Services Venue Officer (TSVO) 
  • Serve as the primary operational point of contact at assigned Team Base Camp (TBC) or Venue Specific Facilities. 
  • Ensure all Team Facilities meet FIFA requirements ahead of exclusive use periods. 
  • Oversee daily operations at the Team Hotel and Training Site. 
  • Coordinate overlay material deliveries and verify equipment readiness. 
  • Act as venue manager interfacing with Training Site Authorities, Teams and all Functional Areas. 
  • Lead cross-functional coordination with Pitch Management, Facility Management, Security, Logistics, and others. 
  • Prepare, coordinate, and accompany Teams on inspection visits. 
  • Guide FA site visits and support table-top exercises and operational planning. 
  • Ensure on-site readiness, cleanliness, safety, and operational continuity at all team facilities. 
Cross-Functional Collaboration 
All Team Services roles must: 
  • Maintain continuous communication with Venue Management, Transportation, Security, Competition Management, and Medical. 
  • Participate in venue-level meetings, daily briefings, conference calls, and urgent response discussions. 
  • Ensure strict compliance with FIFA policies, safeguarding standards, and operational protocols. 
  • Support incident management and escalate issues per FIFA26 guidelines. 
  • Maintain collaborative working relationships with hotels, drivers, event suppliers, and external partners

data.key_responsibilities:
The Transport Supervisor supports daily transport delivery for assigned client groups during the FIFA World Cup 2026. This role ensures safe, efficient, and professional transport services and supports vehicle dispatch, driver coordination, and client movement.

Client Group Service Coordination 
  • Execute specialized transport support based on client group needs 
  • Manage driver check-in, briefing, and deployment 
  • Ensure accurate vehicle allocation and staging for client movements 
  • Support loading, dispatch, and arrival coordination at designated points 
Driver & Workforce Support 
  • Facilitate driver technology setup (tablets, mobile apps, TMM credentials) 
  • Distribute job cards and ensure VAPP/accreditation compliance 
  • Promote uniform, conduct, and service standards 
Operational Delivery 
  • Ensure vehicles are clean, fueled, and operational prior to dispatch 
  • Coordinate arrival/departure flows to maintain on-time performance 
  • Assist with routing, fleet adjustments, and incident escalation 
Safety & Compliance 
  • Enforce depot and venue safety procedures 
  • Monitor access control, permissions, and safe vehicle movements 
  • Support emergency or contingency response as directed 
Documentation & Communication 
  • Maintain shift logs, attendance sheets, driver rosters, and reports 
  • Participate in briefings, handovers, and operational calls 
  • Provide proactive updates to the Depot Manager and Command Center

  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Transport Operations, Logistics, or Hospitality, or equivalent experience.

Work Experience 
  • 3-5 years of experience in transport, hospitality, or event operations. 
  • Experience working in a supervisory role within large-scale or high-profile events preferred.
