data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
The Arrivals & Departures Manager is responsible for planning, coordinating, and delivering all Arrivals & Departures operations for and during the tournament. This role ensures seamless, safe, and efficient transportation services for all FIFA client groups and spectators, guaranteeing operational readiness and flawless delivery on all airports used for the tournament. The position also liaises closely with the Airport Operators, Federal Government and Host City authorities.

This role reports to the Senior Transport Manager and oversees key arrivals and departures sub-projects, including international and inter-city planning and operations, as well as aviation venue management. The Arrivals & Departures Manager collaborates closely with Airport Operators, Federal Government agencies, Host City authorities, the LFS Senior Transport Manager, the Transport Government Liaison & Integration Manager, and FIFA to define and implement an integrated concept of operations for Arrivals & Departures services. It is also the responsibility of this position to develop airport operational plans that provide essential support to both the Transport Functional Area and Event Services.

The successful candidate is expected to be a proactive and resilient professional, capable of leading and managing a team while operating efficiently under pressure and delivering to strict timelines. Managing the area’s activities and resources within the approved budget is also a key component of the role. The Arrivals & Departures Manager must ensure strong integration with all other FWWC2027 functional areas present at the airport—including Workforce, Volunteers, Guest Operations & Protocol Etiquette, Team Services, and IT—to ensure alignment with established FIFA SLAs and the seamless delivery of airport operations.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

·      
·       Designing and implementing a suitable A&D service model that covers all tournament airports, to service all FIFA Constituent Groups (Teams, Referees, FIFA Officials, VIPs, Workforce, Dignitaries, Media, Broadcast, Commercial Affiliates) at all official point-of-entry/exit sites, based on FIFA requirements and agreed service levels including but not limited to:


o   Final Draw


o   Team Workshops


o   FWWC2027 Tournament


o   Test Events


o   Any other official side events


·       Designing the initial structure of International and Inter-city Arrivals and Departures / Aviation and Airport Management and sub-projects including but not limited to A&D Information management, Venue planning, Constituent Group requirements, Tournament time operations.


·       Planning and implementation of an Arrivals & Departures Plan for each FIFA Constituent Group based on FIFA requirements and service levels.


·       Negotiating with the airport authorities and government agencies to ensure that all the structures and procedures of the airports are aligned to deliver the best operation on peak throughout the FWWC2027 operational period.


·       Designing the concept of operations, the venue operating plan, and all relevant A&D policies and procedures.


·       Integration with others LFS Transport workstreams functions within Event Transport for Airport pick-ups and drop-offs.


·       Coordination with meet and greet companies regarding VIP arrivals and departures services.


·       Contributing to the slot’s allocation plan of the HC Airports for the period of the tournament ensuring that priority is given to the FWWC2027-related aircraft movements (mainly PMA charter flights and FIFA VIP private jets).


·       Ensuring successful integration of the FWWC2027 air traffic into the overall international airline schedule.


·       Participate proactively in the Airport/ Mobility Working Groups and ensure (via close collaboration with HC Transport, Civil Aviation and Airport Operators and Authorities) that the air traffic match the airports and terminal capacities.


·       Planning and development (in coordination with Airport Authorities, HC Transport, and other FWWC2027 Functional Areas (Security, Logistics, Team Services, Guest Operations, Marketing, etc.) the tournament time Operational Plan for the Airport (Airside and Landside Operations).


·       Ensuring integration of the Airport Operational Plan with the Airport Transportation Plan, as well as correlation with all other Transport services and operations.


·       Delivery of the final operational plan and ensure successful and smooth operations for all constituent groups.


·       Implementing Project Management within A&D and Airport management, identifying key milestones of International and Inter-city Arrivals and Departures / Aviation and Airport Management sub-projects.


·       Identifying and addressing key risks of International and Inter-city Arrivals and Departures / Aviation and Airport Management sub-projects.


·       Content preparation (slides, handouts, etc.) and preparation of reports for the FWWC2027 related management meetings and working group meetings.


·       Support recruiting, hiring each Host City A&D Manager, assigning responsibilities, and managing the Team from planning through delivery. Overall accountability for appropriate staffing levels and quality employees relating to all Airports. 


·       Ensure all Airport staff are properly trained by developing and maintaining training materials and coordinating the delivery of operational workshops and briefings, and role-specific instructions. This HQ manager maintains consistent training standards across all Host Cities Airports.


·       Provide regular reporting to the Transport Senior Manager, FIFA counterparts, and other key stakeholders on coordination progress, issues, and decisions.


·       Support test events, rehearsals, and operational readiness exercises. Oversee the installation and readiness of signage, transport desk, wayfinding etc.


·       Maintain constant live communication with the Transport Command and Control, providing real-time updates on airport operations, incidents, delays, and any emerging issues. Ensure that all critical issue is escalated promptly, enabling rapid decision-making and coordinated responses across all transport functions, Government agencies and Airports operators. This includes monitoring on-ground conditions, reporting deviations from the plan, and implementing solutions to ensure smooth and uninterrupted service delivery.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Qualifications:


Education – Bachelor’s Degree in Transport/Logistics/Civil Engineering/Project management/Event Management or a combination of education and experience relating to position. Master’s Degree is an additional asset.


 


Experience:


·       Minimum of ten (10) years of event Arrivals and Departures (Airport Operations) experience in international major events in at least three (3) world-class sport/football events. E.g. FIFA World Cup/Olympic Games. Experience should include solid experience in multiple aspects of event transport operations and project management. 


·       Minimum of five (5) years of management experience in a similar role within an organising committee leading large teams to deliver a time critical project.


·       Proven experience of successfully managing and delivering transport projects of a similar size and scale.


·       Proven experience of managing Airport Operations projects of a similar size and scale.


·       Experience working with public and private sector partners in a collaborative environment and ability to build strong partnerships.


 


Skills:


·       Advanced knowledge of administration and management principles.


·       Advanced knowledge of teams’ leadership, business process management. 


·       Advanced communication, interpersonal and negotiation skills, the ability to influence others and manage relationships.


·       Ability to design, implement and operate a transport system in the Brazilian event environment.


·       Advanced knowledge of transportation technical operating processes and activities.


·       Ability to motivate others toward a common goal and manage a multidisciplinary team through conflicting priorities and continuous change requests.


·       Ability to work and communicate in a corporate and project focused organisation.  


·       Proven project management skills within a complex project.


·       Language skills - English language fluency both written and spoken. 


·       Strong analytical and organisation skills.


·       Proven solution orientated decision-making skills.

Ability to work under pressure to immoveable deadlines.


Requirements
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Bilingual - Portuguese and English. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the IPTV/CATV Manager, the Live & VOD Stream Operations Technician will be a key member of the FIFA26 Technology team, and work in close collaboration with the Technology teams based in Canada, Mexico and Zurich and other key individuals responsible for delivering the Live Stream solutions required for the FIFA World Cup 2026 operations.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Live & VOD Stream Operations Technician for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:
  • Execute live OTT streaming operations for FIFA World Cup 26™ matches and press conferences, including stream activation, real-time monitoring and incident escalation.
  • Validate event streaming workflows and ensure service readiness prior to each event.
  • Manage post-event VOD processing, including content extraction, asset validation, metadata verification and publishing to the Media HUB platform.
  • Coordinate with Broadcast, Production, OTT providers and internal Technology teams during live operations.
  • Maintain operational logs, event reports and document incidents and corrective actions.
  • Escalate technical risks or service-impacting issues to the IPTV/CATV Manager.
  • Ensure compliance with FIFA26 Technology governance, operational procedures and change management standards
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • University degree or technical diploma in Information Technology, Media Technology, Broadcast, or related field (or equivalent practical experience).
  • Basic understanding of live OTT streaming workflows and VOD asset handling.
  • Familiarity with common streaming formats and protocols (e.g., H.264/H.265, RTMP, HLS).
  • Experience working with OTT platforms, CMS systems, or video publishing tools is an advantage.
  • Ability to support live event operations and perform first-line troubleshooting.
Work Experience
  • 3–5 years of experience in OTT streaming, digital media operations, or broadcast environments
  • Experience supporting live streaming operations during events
  • Hands-on experience with live stream activation, monitoring, and post-event VOD publishing
  • Experience working with OTT platforms or content management systems
Languages
  • Fluent in English
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
The Uniforms Manager is responsible for the planning and operational delivery of the corporate FIFA27 Business Casual uniform as well as the FWWC2027 Tournament Dedicated and FIFA Formal uniform programmes in line with the overall FIFA Uniform Strategy and the approved usage and entitlement matrix. 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Responsible for project management and implementation of the FWWC2027 uniform programme including issuance operations that support both workforce and volunteers
  • Ensure all FIFA27 workforce have uniform according to the entitlement and usage matrix
  • Effectively collaborate with key FIFA Zurich and FIFA27 stakeholders including Workforce Planning, Volunteer Management, Host City Management, Logistics, Accreditation and Partnership Rights Delivery
  • Create strategy, process and flows for the full project plan for FWWC2027 uniform programme capturing risks and mitigations throughout
  • Develop the operational plan, setting up affiliated systems, identify functional distribution spaces, engage with stakeholders, contract local suppliers to deliver the FWWC2027 uniform programme, across all the Uniforms Distribution Centers
  • Oversee FIFA27 uniform budget, including creating accurate forecasts for each FIFA27 related event e.g. FWWC2027 Draw
  • Manage relationships with external uniform suppliers that may be required, ensuring compliance in line with Framework Agreement and enforcing contractual obligations 
  • Recruit and effectively train and manage the uniforms team (both workforce and volunteers) during planning and delivery periods ensuring best practices are clearly defined and the service level provided across all Uniform Distribution Centers is consistent
  • Manage stock levels and keep up-to-date records of each uniform item during pre-tournament (e.g. Business Casual uniform/ formal items that may be required for side events) and tournament time.
  • Communicate the agreed FIFA27 Uniform Guidelines and Usage Policy at each FIFA tournament and event 
  • Ensure the uniform guidelines are upheld and enforced across all client groups including FIFA Formal Uniform eligibility
  • Plan uniform distribution to all related FWW2027 additional events, e.g. Final Draw, 
  • Work with relevant FIFA27 stakeholders to agree and provide guidelines to Suppliers who are not entitled to Tournament Dedicated Uniforms Other duties as necessary to ensure the smooth running of the FWWC2027 Uniform operations.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
• Minimum experience of 3 years in Uniform Management; preferably with experience in mega (sports) events and corporate organisations  
  • Excellent people manager, motivating, inspiring and seeing results through others
  • Knowledge of event planning and event project management processes 
  • Strong IT skill including MS Office (Word, Excel, Visio, PowerPoint) 
  • Fluency in Brazilian Portuguese and English. Other languages are an added benefit  
  • Strong written and oral communication 
  • Proven track record in administering uniform projects across complex quantity structures 
  • Team Player, willing to work to tight timelines 
  • Positive attitude, patience and persistence, with a pragmatic approach to solving problems
  • Excellent time management skills and ability to balance competing priorities 
  • Demonstrate initiative and a strong focus on providing a high level of service 
  • Right to work in Brazil. 
  • Ability to re-locate to Rio de Janeiro.

data.description:
At FIFA26, our vision is to unite the world through the greatest sporting event of all time. The FIFA World Cup 26™ will mark the first time that the tournament will feature 48 teams and be hosted by three countries: Canada, Mexico and the USA. This new format will redefine excellence, generating unique opportunities for greater participation and engagement among fans and players across North America and all over the world. Now is your time to become a game changer and join the workforce that will plan and deliver this extraordinary and unforgettable experience.

FIFA World Cup 26 Skills Framework

ACCOUNTABILITY: Demonstrate commitment to responsibilities and objectively analyze one’s behavior, while assuming consequences of one’s own actions and/or decisions and learning from one’s mistakes.

DECISION MAKING: Ability to identify and swiftly make decisions in critical situations, take initiative and calculated risks, explore alternative solutions, and respond promptly to unforeseen circumstances.

INCLUSIVITY: Achieve shared organizational objectives by sharing information with colleagues across all levels and divisions, tangibly influencing others, and maintaining a network of support contacts.

INITIATIVE:  Capacity to do things on one’s own initiative and to influence the course of events, assume responsibilities, and achieve objectives with a minimum of supervision and control.

INNOVATION: Capacity to break new ground, look for unconventional solutions, and produce fresh ideas.

LEADERSHIP: Ability to lead one’s contacts/colleagues/partners, encourage teamwork, and delegate authority/decision-making power, as needed, to produce the expected results and make efficient use of the team’s skills and talents.

VISION: Ability to perceive the organization's global environmental impact, anticipate decision consequences, grasp organizational missions, and formulate strategic plans.
data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organizationally to the Venue Transport Parking and Permitting Manager, the Venue Transport Parking Sr. Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, collaborating closely with the Traffic Management and Venue Transport Teams.  

The Sr. Coordinator will support the implementation of parking and permitting strategies across all official competition sites, including stadiums. A core focus of this role will involve assisting with the implementation and lifecycle management of the Vehicle Access and Parking Permit Scheme (VAPPS), a centralized system designed to control access and parking. 

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Venue Transport Parking Sr. Coordinator for the FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
  • Assist the Parking and Permitting Manager ensuring that the tournament wide VAPP requirements (digital and printed) are implemented on the ground operationally. 
  • Assist in managing a robust VAPP database system to ensure accurate data capture and use throughout the lifecycle of FIFA26. 
  • Manage stadium parking numbers to align parking capacity with the correct number of VAPPs allocated and distributed to constituent groups. 
  • Mange Match Day operations and distribution of VAPP PDAs (scanners) ensuring they are collected and charged for the next match day. 
  • Manage on site VAPP PDA setup, configuration and troubleshooting 
  • Manage VAPP boards related to car park operations.  
  • Ensure compliance with VAPP Policies and Procedures 
  • Support the management of a centralized application and distribution process for Printed VAPPs. 
  • Help manage VAPP help desk to address staff and stakeholder queries and requests during the tournament. 
  • Implement an issue resolution process to address VAPP-related challenges efficiently. 
  • Compile daily VAPP scanning reports
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s Degree in Transport, Civil Engineering, Project management, Event Management, Major Sport events or a combination of education and experience relating to position. 
Work Experience
  • 1-2 years’ work experience: ideally within the football, sport, or event industry. 
  • Able to effectively present information and respond to questions from support staff, managers, and clients.  
  • Experience working in multi-functional event organizations and in dynamic and fast paced environments. 
  • Must be motivated, capable of working independently, and have excellent written and oral communication skills.  
  • Willingness to learn transit operations involved in moving Client Groups. 
Languages
  • Fluent in English.  
  • Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools 
  • Stadium technology parking schemes 
  • Experience in data management systems (e.g. PARCS, Digital Parking Platforms or Parking Access Control Systems and Applications) 
In accordance with Washington' s Equal Pay and Opportunity Act, the pay range for this position is $31 per hour. This estimate is based on this position being performed in Washington, and the range may not apply to similar positions in other locations. In determining actual base pay, FIFA 26 considers candidate education, experience, and other job-related factors allowed by law. An employee’s position within the salary range will be determined by various factors, including relevant education, experience, qualifications, certifications, skills, geographic location, performance metrics, shift, travel requirements, collective bargaining agreements, and business needs.
data.title: Sr. Parking Coordinator – FIFA World Cup 26™ | Seattle, WA

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
The Venue Technology Manager is responsible for supporting the Technology team with venue specific technology related deliverables for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027 TM (FWWC 2027) within Brazil. The role entails coordinating with all relevant parties the planning, implementation, readiness and operations of all Event Technology services pertinent to the tournament and / or associated events within Brazil.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Responsible to ensure all aspects of Technology, Telecommunications and Broadcast Technology Services are implemented in accordance with the pre-agreed FIFA Technology requirements at the assigned venue(s)
  • Manage a strong diverse Venue Technology team composed of paid staff, contractors and volunteers for operating the technology services at the assigned venue(s) 
  • Collaborate with stakeholders’ groups, clients, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring the delivery of technology services across all venues, in accordance with the defined governance structure
  • Manage and contribute to the venue technology working groups in accordance with the agreed governance structure across all aspects of the tournament programme (planning, implementation, readiness and operations) 
  • Manage the technology demand / needs requirements for technology services across the tournament space (Technology Equipment Allocation Process) 
  • Plan the delivery of suitable end user technology equipment and services to all FWWC 2027 departments and FIFA constituent groups, including hardware, services and consumables 
  • Facilitate the collection and distribution of technology requirements of all client groups involved in the FWWC 2027 across all client groups, including, Media, Broadcast, Commercial, Football Technology etc. 
  • Ensure that all information pertaining to infrastructure, services and spaces needed for the delivery and support of all technology services are communicated to, and understood by, venue/site authorities and respective delivery teams 
  • Provide guidance and direction to relevant teams on matters relating to the structural build-out and technology services implementation, including the commissioning and final decommissioning of tournament venues  
  • In collaboration with assigned personnel and Stadium partners, relevant parties (private and governmental) enable / support the timely delivery of venue technology services. 
  • Identify and report on the progress of all critical dependencies in accordance with the reporting methodologies 
  • Manage the asset tracking and management of deployed technology equipment 
  • Track the progress of structural build-out and IT implementation leading up to the timely availability of components and services and including the commissioning and final decommissioning of the venues 
  • Develop regular progress, risk and issue management reports in accordance with the governance structure / reporting methodologies and, or PMO systems 
  •  Follow-up with various stakeholders, providers, and government entities in relation to ensuring delivery of technology services / dependencies as per agreed requirements, service levels etc. 
  • Support and ensure the team adhere to the IT Service management protocols 
  • Ensure positive attitude from the Venue Technology teams and good relationship with all the parties, including FIFA teams, Local FIFA Subsidiary (LFS) areas/projects, end-users, suppliers, Host Country/ City counterparts
  • Manage the on-site IT Technicians and support teams during tournament time operations, including end-of-day reporting and incident escalation to Technology Command Centre.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education
  • University degree or similar higher education, in Information Technology, Computer Science or related field
Experience
  • Minimum of 8 years overall IT experience and minimum 4 years of experience in a similar role
  • Experience in the Sports / major event industry is a must
  • Proven track record of successful delivery of complex IT programs and projects
Skills & Competencies
  • Manager with proven track record in similar projects
  • Good knowledge of venue technology services across major sporting events 
  • Have up to date knowledge and project management expertise in all event related technologies, including, all types of technology business solutions, community access TV (CATV), radio communications, wired communication, printing, broadcast contribution network, spectrum, audio and visual. 
  • Ability to always maintain a professional demeanor. Consistently interacts professionally and works productively with all levels of employee. 
  • Excellent cross group collaboration skills and demonstrated ability to lead virtual teams across groups, conflict management and driving consensus to deliver on program milestones  
  • Good Leadership skills and able to manage and motivate other team members 
  • Highly adept at consulting, negotiating, communicating, consensus building and facilitation
  • Excellent verbal and written communication, training, and presentation skills
  • Ability to adapt speech to audience coming from a broad spectrum of disciplines and with various levels of technical knowledge
  • Leadership and accountability across all areas of relevance  
  • Strong political acumen and adaptable to a wide range of working environments

data.description:
The Zimbabwe Football Association (ZIFA) is inviting applications from suitably qualified, experienced, and visionary individuals for the position of General Secretary.

The General Secretary is the Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of the Association and is responsible for the overall administration, strategic leadership, operational management, governance, and commercial growth of football in Zimbabwe. Reporting to the ZIFA President, the successful candidate will drive the implementation of ZIFA’s strategic objectives while ensuring absolute compliance with FIFA, CAF, COSAFA, and national regulatory frameworks.
data.key_responsibilities:
• Implementing decisions passed by the Congress and Executive Committee in compliance with the President’s directives;
  • Attending the Congress and meetings of the Executive Committee, the Standing Committee and adhoc committees;
  • Organising the Congress and meetings of the Executive Committee and other bodies;
  • Compiling the minutes for the meetings of the Congress, Executive Committee, Standing and adhoc committees;
  • Managing and keeping the accounts;
  • Correspondence;
  • The relations with the members, committees, FIFA, CAF and COSAFA, under the direction of the President;
  • Organising the General Secretariat;
  • The appointment and dismissal of staff working in the General Secretariat, in line with the relevant provisions within the Internal Organisation Regulations;
  • Providing the Electoral Committees with logistic and operative support for the elections.
data.key_responsibilities_header: Key Responsibilities of the Job (refer to article 45 of the ZIFA Statutes)
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
The incumbent's performance shall be rigorously assessed against clear key performance areas (KPAs), including:
  • Strategic leadership & policy execution.
  • Financial sustainability, revenue growth, and strict expense management.
  • Commercial sponsorship and corporate partnership development.
  • National team performance and structural football development metrics.
  • Measurable growth in grassroots participation and local football structures.
  • Governance, compliance, and risk-mitigated operational efficiency.
  • Brand reputation recovery and corporate stakeholder confidence.
  • Secretariat performance, leadership and successful delivery of set strategic objectives.

Minimum Qualifications Required:
  • A University Degree in Sports Management, Business Administration, Commerce, Law, or a related field.
  • A Master’s Degree in Sports Management, Business Administration (MBA), or related field is an added advantage.
  • A Diploma in Sports Administration will be a distinct added advantage.
Experience Required:
  • A minimum of 10 years’ experience in sports administration or business leadership, with at least 5 years spent at a Senior Decision-Making level or a comparable executive
  • environment.
  • Demonstrated experience in strategic planning, institutional governance, stakeholder management, and complex organizational transformation.
  • Proven experience in financial oversight, budgetary controls, and commercial property or portfolio development.
  • A strong, documented understanding of football governance structures and international football administration frameworks.
Core Competencies & Attributes
  • Visionary and transformational leadership capabilities.
  • Strong commercial, financial, and risk-management acumen.
  • Excellent communication, high-level negotiation, and international diplomacy skills.
  • High levels of personal integrity, professionalism, and institutional accountability.
  • Strategic thinking, rapid decision-making capacity, and a clear understanding of the consequence of error.
  • Ability to lead cultural shift, organizational change, and a performance-driven work environment.
  • Resilience and the capacity to operate effectively under intense, high-pressure situations.

Terms of Appointment
  • The position offers a highly competitive executive remuneration package commensurate with qualifications and experience.
  • The successful candidate will be required to enter into a strict, performance-based two-year contract with the Association.
data.title: General Secretary - Zimbabwe Football Association (ZIFA)

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to the Client Services PMA Cluster Supervisors, the Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator will be a key member of the FIFA26 Event Transport team, and work in close collaboration with the Team Services Team, Operational Services Team and other key individuals responsible for delivering Transport Operations. 

The Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator is responsible for the full scope of transport operations at an assigned Team Base Camp Hotel or Venue Specific Hotel, including the
hotel and training site. This role ensures the seamless delivery of bus, fleet, and airport/FBO (Arrivals & Departures) services for participating teams, in strict alignment with FIFA service levels and operational procedures. The coordinator serves as the primary liaison between drivers, suppliers, the Team (Security) Liaison Officer (TLO/TSLO), airport authorities, and FIFA Transport, ensuring bubble-to-bubble operations, client satisfaction, and readiness at all times.

The main responsibilities and oversights of the Client Services PMA Transport Coordinator FIFA World Cup 26™ include:   
Communication 
  • Maintain daily communication with Fleet, Bus, A&D, Venue Transport, Security, Logistics, and Client Services functions. 
  • Hold daily face-to-face meetings with TLO/TSLO to align on schedules, procedures, and driver management. 
  • Receive and inspect all vehicles before each shift, conducting driver briefings on daily activities and changes. 
  • Escalate issues immediately to ensure continuity of operations. 
Client Services 
  • Act as the primary point of contact for TLO/TSLO at the TBC or Venue Specific Team Hotel. 
  • Ensure compliance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) and all FIFA transport policies. 
  • Coordinate with TLO/TSLO to maintain strict bubble-to-bubble operations. 
  • Ensure load zones are available and maintained for all team activities. 
  • Support drivers in resolving operational issues, escalating when required to depot or FIFA transport. 
  • Liaise with training site and hotel staff, ensuring readiness of signage, cones, and transport infrastructure. 
  • Submit daily reports on concerns, incidents, and operational performance to the Client Services Cluster Supervisor. 
Fleet & Bus Operations 
  • Manage daily driver check-in/out and shift scheduling at the TBCH or VSTH. 
  • Ensure Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) are correctly reflected in TMM and followed by staff. 
  • Secure and manage staging areas, load zones, and parking at the TBC and training site or VSTH. 
  • Oversee all aspects of TMM system management, including last-minute schedule or vehicle changes. 
  • Validate/facilitate driver training, conduct test runs, and ensure familiarity with routes. 
  • Supervise drivers during shifts, ensuring compliance with procedures, grooming standards, and readiness. 
  • Ensure all vehicles are properly maintained, fueled, branded, equipped with VAAPs, and operationally ready. 
  • Provide accurate driver and vehicle information as required. 
  • Manage rate card requests in collaboration with TLO/TSLO. 
  • Coordinate with the depot team for vehicle/driver issues, including intercity and convoy movements. 
Arrivals & Departures (A&D) / FBO Operations 
  • Act as the on-site coordinator for all team arrivals and departures via airports and FBOs. 
  • Communicate with FBO teams, ACS, and airport authorities for vehicle staging and tarmac access. 
  • Ensure drivers know approved routes, timings, and positioning for FBO operations. 
  • Guarantee all vehicles are in position at least 60 minutes prior to ETA and properly briefed. 
  • Coordinate security convoys, logistic trucks, and family/friends rate card vehicles when applicable. 
  • Liaise with ACS for live convoy tracking and resolve any discrepancies on site. 
  • Inspect and establish SOPs for first international arrivals and last international departures, training colleagues in all procedures. 
  • Liaise with Bus/Fleet and TSLO for convoy planning on all movements. 
  • Coordinate media access and badging for first arrivals, ensuring compliance with FBO/media protocols. 
  • Stay onsite until after ETD to confirm successful departures or resolve contingencies. 
  • Any other duties that may be required.  
Dates
May 1, 2026  to July 2, 2026
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications 
  • Bachelor’s degree in Event Management, Sports Management, Hotel Management, Project Management, Transport Operations, Logistics, or Hospitality, or equivalent experience. 
  • Knowledge of transport operations, guest services, or event logistics is an asset. 
Work Experience 
  • Proven experience in Event transport, logistics, or operations, preferably at large-scale international events. 
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple stakeholders and staff under pressure. 
  • Experience supervising drivers, staff, or suppliers in a dynamic operational environment. 
  • Familiarity with transport systems (e.g., TMM) and airport/FBO procedures is a strong advantage. 
  • Experience working in a client-facing supervisory role within large-scale or high-profile events preferred. 
  • Strong interpersonal skills, proactive communication, and confidence in decision-making. 
  • Demonstrates the highest levels of integrity, professionalism, and commitment.  
  • Positive attitude, patience, and persistence in problem-solving.  
  • Strong cultural awareness and the ability to work in an international environment. 
Languages 
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus 
Technology 
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
Reporting organisationally to Operational Center Lead, the Workforce Operations Manager will be a key member of the FIFA26 Workforce Management team, and work in close collaboration with the FIFA Workforce Management team and other key individuals responsible for delivering workforce-related services in line with the agreed service levels, resolving on-site issues, and supporting the smooth delivery of operations through close collaboration with the central Workforce team and local Functional Area (FA) counterparts.
  • Support day-to-day workforce operations across assigned stadiums and venue sites, assisting with issue resolution and coordinating escalations through the appropriate channels.
  • Coordinate and support the setup and operation of workforce welcome activities at FWC26 venues, assisting with inquiries and directing workforce questions or issues to the right resources or personnel.
  • Assist in daily workforce issue resolution across all locations, working collaboratively with Accommodation (ACM), Catering (CAT), Transport (TPT), Venue Management (VUM), Volunteer Management (VOL), Security (SEC), Access Management (ACS), and Partner Rights Delivery (PRD) to help ensure seamless service delivery.
  • Coordinate with Accommodation (ACM) on matters related to laundry, breakfast, and hotel services in line with workforce entitlements.
  • Support the planning and coordination of Workforce Welcome and Final Celebrations, working closely with the Workforce Engagement team and relevant Functional Areas to ensure smooth execution on-site.
  • Assist with the delivery of venue orientation sessions, helping present the Workforce Venue Guide, service levels, and key policies/procedures to align all workforce members during tournament time.
  • Support the day-to-day coordination of catering operations within the Workforce Restaurant/Break area in collaboration with Catering (CAT) and Volunteer Management (VOL).
  • Work closely with ACS & SEC to understand PSAs and assist TPT with coordinating shuttle pick-up/drop-off points and general workforce transportation logistics.
  • Maintain awareness of first aid locations and medical stations in collaboration with MED to support workforce safety and response readiness.
  • Support the coordination of storage, movement, and distribution of reward and recognition items, contingency uniforms, and other assets with Logistics and Uniforms teams, helping ensure accuracy and accountability at every stage.
  • Liaise with PRD to confirm placement of VIK items and assist with preparing operational areas and rest spaces for workforce use.
  • Provide support to Uniform Distribution Centers (UDACs) to ensure consistent alignment across all workforce touchpoints.
  • Maintain regular communication with the Central Workforce Team, sharing updates on site status, operational progress, and emerging issues.
  • Support tournament close-out efforts, assisting with final distribution of reward items and post-event reporting to ensure a smooth handover and debrief with the central team.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
Education & Qualifications
  •  Bachelor’s degree preferred
Work Experience
  • Must be available for a minimum of five virtual (5) training sessions prior to deployment and willing to travel for one (1) to three (3) months, as required.
  • Demonstrate willingness to learn and understand all relevant policies, procedures, and operational documents related to workforce services and venue operations.
Language
  • Fluent in English. Spanish and/or French proficiency is a plus
Technology
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools

data.key_responsibilities:
• Developing and maintaining a competitions strategy in line with FIFA/CAF/COSAFA requirements and best practices.
  • Directing competition operations, including match officials, volunteers, logistics, venues, and match-day requirements.
  • Ensuring fixture scheduling, competitions documentation, and competitions reporting are completed accurately
  • and on time, in liaison with club licensing and technical functions.
  • Managing risk, safeguarding integration, and regulatory compliance, including incident documentation and post-event
  • evaluation.
  • Overseeing operational planning for competitions, including venue setup, safety considerations, emergency
  • response arrangements, and quality assurance.
  • Coordinating competition-related stakeholder engagement, including internal departments and external
  • partners (as applicable).
  • Managing and monitoring programme delivery under relevant FIFA/CAF initiatives.
  • Supporting continuous improvement through best-practice research and process improvement initiative.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
• Degree in Sports Management (or a relevant field).
  • FIFA Certificate in Safeguarding (or equivalent), an added advantage.
  • Masters Degree in Sports Management (an added advantage).

MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS:
  • Minimum of 5 years of experience in a leadership role and in a sporting organization, with proven exposure
  • to competitions/event management and/or programme delivery.
  • Strong innovative and transformational leadership capabilities.
  • Excellent communication skills and ability to work effectively under pressure while maintaining confidentiality and professionalism.

HOW TO APPLY:
  • Interested candidates should submit an application letter, a detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV), certified copies of academic and professional qualifications, and at least three traceable professional references.
  • Applications must be clearly marked “Vacancy – Head - Competitions” in the subject line and submitted to: Email:hr@zifa.co.zw and copy (cc) recruitment@samaracc.co.zw
  • Remuneration is negotiable and commensurate with experience. Closing Date for Applications: 7th June 2026.
  • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
data.title: Head Competitions - Zimbabwe Football Association (ZIFA)

data.description:
FIFA has established a Local FIFA Subsidiary in Brazil to work together with FIFA Zurich to deliver the biggest women’s sporting event in the world, the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ (“FWWC2027”) that for the first time ever, is hosted in South America in Brazil. Now is your time to be part of the workforce that is creating this unique and unforgettable experience.

Please submit your CV in English, as it will be reviewed by an international team.
data.key_responsibilities:
Sitting within the Ticketing & Hospitality Department, Hospitality (along with Ticketing) aims to deliver the most engaging and innovative FIFA Women's World Cup 2027™ experience for all commercial partners, customers, guests and the FIFA football family.

Reporting organizationally to the Head of FWWC27 Hospitality Operations, the Hospitality Operations & Infrastructure Venue Manager will help serve Hospitality efforts for the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™ that is hosting 32 teams across 8 venues.

This role will be responsible for the supervision of the implementation of all stadium Hospitality infrastructure requirements and technical operations across all stadiums of the FIFA Women’s World Cup 2027™, and later assuming a fixed position as Rio de Janeiro Stadium Hospitality Operations & Infrastructure Venue Manager during tournament time. She/he will work closely with the Official Hospitality Provider and FIFA’s stadium operations and infrastructure functional areas to ensure contractual and requirements compliance.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES
  • Responsible for the management and operational readiness and delivery of the Affiliates and Commercial Hospitality programmes, reporting duties, maintenance/creation of operational and infrastructure documentations, etc
  • Ensuring Infrastructure readiness and maintenance services of Hospitality areas, in close cooperation with the FIFA other functional areas, the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP) and Stadium Authorities
  • Responsible for the on-site management of the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP) and their suppliers, and Hospitality Volunteers
  • Checking and validating the Operational plans from the service-providing departments to Hospitality (Cleaning and Waste, Security, Medical, Signage, Transport, Fan Operations, Accreditation, Power, IT, TV, ticketing, etc.)
  • Ensuring excellent service to the FIFA Affiliates on site, overlooking the set-up and readiness of their facilities, monitoring service delivery from the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP) from the set-up phase through to match day delivery of services
  • Ensuring contractual deliverables to the FIFA Commercial Affiliates and to the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP)
  • Overall on-site hospitality guest management
  • Ensure service consistency throughout the different hospitality programmes across all matches in the venue
  • Validating all on-site hospitality directional signage
  • Ensuring Health and Safety regulations are strictly observed and respected by the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP)
  • Ensuring Hospitality guest numbers are updated and shared with all relevant FIFA stakeholders
  • Ensuring that all stakeholders are appropriately accredited. Manage the distribution of SADs (supplementary access devices) to all relevant functional areas, restricting access to hospitality areas as needed
  • Ensure the catering service runs as expected in the Affiliates and Commercial Hospitality programmes
  • Handling all hospitality related issues on Match Day
  • Attend the FIFA Daily Coordination Meeting at the Venue and hold daily Hospitality Team Meetings with the Official Hospitality Provider (OHP)
  • Reporting on a daily basis to the FIFA HQ, reporting issues in good time
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
• Bachelor’s Degree or similar level of education in Event Management, Sports Hospitality Management, Project Management, etc.
  • Minimum of 5 years of work experience in stadium hospitality or venue management, managing several venues simultaneously, preferably for major sports or entertainment events.
  • Proven experience with event overlay (including provision of temporary power and water), ticketing, dressing and signage, catering, logistics and security operations
  • Proven experience managing multiple service providers, particularly for Technical services, Cleaning and Waste services, Security Services, Access control Services, Accreditation, etc.
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
  • Strong customer-service attitude, even when under pressure.
  • Ability to work independently and in a team across simultaneous work streams.
  • Exquisite organizational skills.
  • Ability to lead, manage, and motivate a team.
  • Fluent in Portuguese and English. Additional languages considered an asset.
  • Proficient in MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Visio & Project) and planning software and online collaboration tools
  • Excellent knowledge of AutoCAD and literacy of architectural drawings
data.title: Hospitality Operations & Infrastructure Venue Manager

data.benefits:
The biggest benefit of a career at FIFA is the privilege to work for the beautiful game and create something that matters.

Alongside that privilege, we also offer some extra perks and benefits:
  • Health and fitness: Regular football sessions for all abilities, plus on-site gym.
  • Home office: Up to two days of home office per week if work permits and home office allowance for all.
  • Language courses: Offered to all staff to aid personal and professional growth.
  • Continuous learning: We care about your development and encourage internal mobility, offering a variety of training and education to help achieve it.
  • Pension: Flexible pension benefits with generous employer contributions.
  • Relocation assistance: A dedicated team will be on hand to support your relocation.
  • Sustainability: We support all employees using public transport.
  • Vacation: Maintain a healthy work/life balance with a generous vacation allowance.
  • Volunteering: Make an impact beyond your regular role and bond with colleagues by volunteering with one of our partners.
data.description:
As the organiser of some of the biggest and most iconic sport events in the world, we lead the way in the sport and event industry.

Join the team and support us on our mission.
data.key_responsibilities:
These are your key tasks:
  • Manage end-to-end financial operations for multiple FIFA subsidiaries,
  • Accounts Payable, including invoice processing, payment runs and account reconciliations across multiple countries and currencies,
  • Manage statutory reporting, including financial statements, VAT/GST, corporate tax and withholding tax compliance across multiple jurisdictions, in collaboration with external advisors,
  • Perform monthly and year-end closing activities, including posting journal entries, accruals and reconciliations (balance sheet, bank, payroll, intercompany),
  • Support the year-end external audit, internal audits and project-related audits,
  • Contribute to finance transformation initiatives, including SAP implementations, expense tools rollout and corporate card programmes.
data.skills_knowledge_expertise:
We work hard at FIFA.
We are dedicated, ambitious and innovative.

And we respect our values. Always.

For all roles, we seek talented people with an entrepreneurial spirit and a global mindset.

The specific competencies we require for this position are:
  • Minimum 5 years of professional experience in accounting or a similar role,
  • Experience in a multinational / international environment,
  • Proven exposure to tax, statutory reporting and intercompany accounting
    Experience in finance transformation or system implementations is an advantage,
  • Strong SAP experience,
  • High proficiency in Excel,
  • Experience with banking and treasury platforms,
  • Expense management platforms, Power BI and AI‑driven tools are a plus (Yokoy/Perk),
  • Ability to manage multiple entities and priorities in a fast-paced environment,
  • Hands-on, proactive and solution-oriented mindset,
  • Able to work across the full range of finance activities in complex international environments,
  • Business apprenticeship, or degree in Accounting/Finance, or professional certification,
  • Proficient in English,
  • Knowledge of another FIFA language (French, German, Spanish) is an asset.
data.title: Accountant - FIFA Subsidiaries
